\\n281: def _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n282:     insts: list[Instruction] = []\\n283:     _initial_push_null(insts)\\n284:     insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\\n285:     insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\\n286:     return insts\\n287: \\n288: \\n289: class ContinueExecutionCache:\\n290:     cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\\n291:     generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\\n292: \\n293:     @classmethod\\n294:     def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\\n295:         if code not in cls.cache:\\n296:             cls.cache[code] = {}\\n297:         key = tuple(key)\\n298:         if key not in cls.cache[code]:\\n299:             cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\\n300:         return cls.cache[code][key]\\n301: \\n302:     @classmethod\\n303:     def generate(\\n304:         cls,\\n305:         code: types.CodeType,\\n306:         lineno: int,\\n307:         offset: int,\\n308:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\\n309:         nstack: int,\\n310:         argnames: tuple[str, ...],\\n311:         argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\\n312:         setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\\n313:         stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\\n314:         argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\\n315:         null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\\n316:         # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\\n317:         # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\\n318:         nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\\n319:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n320:         assert offset is not None\\n321:         assert not (\\n322:             code.co_flags\\n323:             & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\\n324:         )\\n325:         assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\\n326:         if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\\n327:             return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n328:                 code,\\n329:                 lineno,\\n330:                 offset,\\n331:                 setup_fn_target_offsets,\\n332:                 nstack,\\n333:                 argnames,\\n334:                 argnames_null,\\n335:                 setup_fns,\\n336:                 stack_ctx_vars,\\n337:                 argnames_ctx_vars,\\n338:                 null_idxes,\\n339:                 nested_code_objs,\\n340:             )\\n341: \\n342:         is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\\n343:         meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\\n344: \\n345:         def update(\\n346:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n347:         ) -> None:\\n348:             meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\\n349: \\n350:             args = [\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\", \\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"]\\n351:             args += [f\\\"___stack{i}\\\" for i in range(nstack)]\\n352:             args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\\n353:             freevars = tuple(code_options[\\\"co_cellvars\\\"] or []) + tuple(\\n354:                 code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"] or []\\n355:             )\\n356:             freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\\n357:             code_options[\\\"co_name\\\"] = (\\n358:                 f\\\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\\\"\\n359:             )\\n360:             if is_py311_plus:\\n361:                 qualified_path = code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"].rsplit(\\\".\\\", maxsplit=1)\\n362:                 if len(qualified_path) == 1:\\n363:                     code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"] = code_options[\\\"co_name\\\"]\\n364:                 else:\\n365:                     assert len(qualified_path) == 2\\n366:                     module_name, co_name = qualified_path\\n367:                     code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"] = (\\n368:                         f\\\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\\\"\\n369:                     )\\n370:             code_options[\\\"co_firstlineno\\\"] = lineno\\n371:             code_options[\\\"co_cellvars\\\"] = ()\\n372:             code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"] = freevars\\n373:             code_options[\\\"co_argcount\\\"] = len(args)\\n374:             code_options[\\\"co_posonlyargcount\\\"] = 0\\n375:             code_options[\\\"co_kwonlyargcount\\\"] = 0\\n376:             code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] = tuple(\\n377:                 args\\n378:                 + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\\n379:                 + [v for v in code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] if v not in args]\\n380:                 + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\\n381:             )\\n382:             code_options[\\\"co_flags\\\"] = code_options[\\\"co_flags\\\"] & ~(\\n383:                 CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\\n384:             )\\n385:             target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n386: \\n387:             prefix = []\\n388:             if is_py311_plus:\\n389:                 if freevars:\\n390:                     prefix.append(\\n391:                         create_instruction(\\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\", arg=len(freevars))\\n392:                     )\\n393:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"RESUME\\\", arg=0))\\n394: \\n395:             # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\\n396:             # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\\n397:             # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\\n398:             prefix.extend(\\n399:                 [\\n400:                     create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=True),\\n401:                     create_instruction(\\n402:                         \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n403:                     ),\\n404:                 ]\\n405:             )\\n406: \\n407:             cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\\n408:             hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\\n409:             hook_target_offsets = {\\n410:                 fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\\n411:                 for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\\n412:             }\\n413:             offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\\n414:             # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\\n415:             old_hook_target_remap = {}\\n416:             null_idxes_i = 0\\n417:             stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\\n418:             for i in range(nstack):\\n419:                 while (\\n420:                     null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\\n421:                     and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\\n422:                 ):\\n423:                     prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"))\\n424:                     null_idxes_i += 1\\n425:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=f\\\"___stack{i}\\\"))\\n426:                 if i in hooks:\\n427:                     hook = hooks.pop(i)\\n428:                     hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\\n429:                     prefix.extend(hook_insts)\\n430:                     if is_py311_plus:\\n431:                         hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\\n432:                         old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\\n433:                         meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\\n434:                         old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\\n435:                 if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\\n436:                     # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\\n437:                     # Load args for context variable and construct it\\n438:                     prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\\n439: \\n440:             if is_py311_plus:\\n441:                 # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\\n442:                 # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\\n443:                 meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\\n444:                     reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\\n445:                 )\\n446: \\n447:             assert not hooks\\n448: \\n449:             # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\\n450:             # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\\n451:             for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\\n452:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=name))\\n453:                 prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\\n454:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=name))\\n455: \\n456:             # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\\n457:             if argnames_null:\\n458:                 assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\\n459:                 for v in argnames_null:\\n460:                     assert v not in args\\n461:                     prefix.extend(\\n462:                         [\\n463:                             create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"),\\n464:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=v),\\n465:                         ]\\n466:                     )\\n467: \\n468:             # Call nested resume function\\n469:             if nested_code_objs:\\n470:                 prefix.extend(\\n471:                     [\\n472:                         # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\\n473:                         *add_push_null(\\n474:                             [\\n475:                                 create_instruction(\\n476:                                     \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"\\n477:                                 ),\\n478:                                 create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n479:                                 create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n480:                             ]\\n481:                         ),\\n482:                         # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\\n483:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n484:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n485:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n486:                         # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\\n487:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n488:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n489:                         create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=2),\\n490:                         # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\\n491:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n492:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n493:                         create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n494:                         # create [\\n495:                         #     __nested_resume_fns,\\n496:                         #     __nested_frame_values,\\n497:                         #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\\n498:                         # ]\\n499:                         create_instruction(\\\"LIST_EXTEND\\\", arg=1),\\n500:                         # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\\n501:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n502:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n503:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n504:                         # delete __nested values\\n505:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n506:                         create_instruction(\\n507:                             \\\"DELETE_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"\\n508:                         ),\\n509:                         # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\\n510:                         # in the nested resume\\n511:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=False),\\n512:                         create_instruction(\\n513:                             \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n514:                         ),\\n515:                         # finish the call\\n516:                         create_instruction(\\\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\\\", arg=0),\\n517:                     ]\\n518:                 )\\n519:             else:\\n520:                 # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\\n521:                 prefix.extend(\\n522:                     [\\n523:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=False),\\n524:                         create_instruction(\\n525:                             \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n526:                         ),\\n527:                     ]\\n528:                 )\\n529: \\n530:             prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\\n531: \\n532:             # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\\n533:             # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\\n534:             # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\\n535:             for inst in instructions:\\n536:                 if inst.offset == target.offset:\\n537:                     break\\n538:                 inst.starts_line = None\\n539:                 if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n540:                     inst.positions = None\\n541: \\n542:             if cleanup:\\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\\n545: \\n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\\n550:                     if (\\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\\n553:                     ):\\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\\n556:                         ]\\n557: \\n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\\n560: \\n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\\n563:         return new_code\\n564: \\n565:     @staticmethod\\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n567:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n568:         return [\\n569:             create_load_const(None),\\n570:             create_instruction(\\\"RAISE_VARARGS\\\", arg=1),\\n571:         ]\\n572: \\n573:     @classmethod\\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n575:         cls,\\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\\n577:         lineno: int,\\n578:         offset: int,\\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\\n580:         *args: Any,\\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n582:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: This module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\n3: primarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\n4: bytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\n5: \n6: - Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\n7: - Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\n8: - Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\n9: - Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\n10: - Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\n11: \n12: The module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\n13: Python semantics and execution state.\n14: \"\"\"\n15: \n16: import copy\n17: import dataclasses\n18: import sys\n19: import types\n20: from collections.abc import Iterable\n21: from contextlib import AbstractContextManager\n22: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\n23: \n24: from .bytecode_transformation import (\n25:     add_push_null,\n26:     bytecode_from_template,\n27:     create_call_function,\n28:     create_instruction,\n29:     create_jump_absolute,\n30:     create_load_const,\n31:     Instruction,\n32:     overwrite_instruction,\n33:     transform_code_object,\n34:     unique_id,\n35: )\n36: from .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\n37: \n38: \n39: # taken from code.h in cpython\n40: CO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\n41: CO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\n42: CO_VARARGS = 0x0004\n43: CO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\n44: CO_NESTED = 0x0010\n45: CO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\n46: CO_NOFREE = 0x0040\n47: CO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\n48: CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\n49: CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200\n50: \n51: # trace_rules.py import this constant for consistency\n52: TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX = \"torch_dynamo_resume_in\"\n53: IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME = \"__is_tracing_resume_prologue\"\n54: \n55: \n56: def _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n57:     if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n58:         insts.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n59:         if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\n60:             insts.append(create_instruction(\"SWAP\", arg=2))\n61: \n62: \n63: # Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\n64: def _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n65:     template: Callable[..., Any],\n66:     stack_index: int,\n67:     varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\n68: ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\n69:     template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\n70:     template_code.append(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n71: \n72:     # adjust exception table entry depth\n73:     for inst in template_code:\n74:         if inst.exn_tab_entry:\n75:             inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\n76: \n77:     # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\n78:     dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\n79:         (\n80:             (i, inst)\n81:             for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\n82:             if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"dummy\"\n83:         ),\n84:         (None, None),\n85:     )\n86:     assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\n87: \n88:     # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\n89:     overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n90: \n91:     # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\n92:     assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \"POP_TOP\"\n93:     overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n94: \n95:     return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\n96: \n97: \n98: def _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\n99:     # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\n100:     # on torch._dynamo.utils.\n101:     global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\n102:     try:\n103:         dummy\n104:     except:  # noqa: E722, B001\n105:         __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n106:             stack_var_name\n107:         )\n108:         raise\n109: \n110: \n111: @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n112: class ReenterWith:\n113:     stack_index: int\n114:     target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\n115: \n116:     def try_except_torch_function_mode(\n117:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n118:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\n119:         \"\"\"\n120:         Codegen based off of:\n121:         try:\n122:             (rest)\n123:         except:\n124:             (restore previous tf mode stack)\n125:             raise\n126:         \"\"\"\n127:         from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\n128: \n129:         setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n130:             _try_except_tf_mode_template,\n131:             self.stack_index,\n132:             varname_map={\"stack_var_name\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\n133:         )\n134:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n135: \n136:         return setup_try_except\n137: \n138:     # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\n139:     # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\n140:     def try_finally(\n141:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n142:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\n143:         \"\"\"\n144:         Codegen based off of:\n145:         load args\n146:         enter context\n147:         try:\n148:             (rest)\n149:         finally:\n150:             exit context\n151:         \"\"\"\n152:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n153:         load_args = []\n154:         if self.target_values:\n155:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n156:         ctx_name = unique_id(f\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\")\n157:         if ctx_name not in code_options[\"co_varnames\"]:\n158:             code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (ctx_name,)\n159:         for name in [\"__enter__\", \"__exit__\"]:\n160:             if name not in code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n161:                 code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n162: \n163:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n164:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n165:         create_ctx.extend(\n166:             [\n167:                 *load_args,\n168:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n169:                 create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=ctx_name),\n170:             ]\n171:         )\n172: \n173:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n174:             ctx.__enter__()\n175:             try:\n176:                 dummy\n177:             finally:\n178:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n179: \n180:         setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n181:             _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\"ctx\": ctx_name}\n182:         )\n183:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n184:         return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\n185: \n186:     def __call__(\n187:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n188:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\n189:         \"\"\"\n190:         Codegen based off of:\n191:         with ctx(args):\n192:             (rest)\n193:         \"\"\"\n194:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n195:         load_args = []\n196:         if self.target_values:\n197:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n198: \n199:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n200:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n201:         create_ctx.extend(\n202:             [\n203:                 *load_args,\n204:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n205:             ]\n206:         )\n207: \n208:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n209:             with ctx:\n210:                 dummy\n211: \n212:         setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n213:             _template, self.stack_index\n214:         )\n215:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n216: \n217:         load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\n218:             (\n219:                 inst\n220:                 for inst in setup_with\n221:                 if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"ctx\"\n222:             ),\n223:             None,\n224:         )\n225:         assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\n226:         # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\n227:         overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n228: \n229:         # 3.11+ only\n230:         push_exc_info_gen = (\n231:             inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\"\n232:         )\n233:         push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\n234:         # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\n235:         assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\n236: \n237:         return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\n238: \n239: \n240: @dataclasses.dataclass\n241: class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\n242:     code: types.CodeType\n243:     instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n244:     # Python 3.11+ fields\n245:     # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \"block\" consists\n246:     # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\n247: \n248:     # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n249:     prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\n250:         default_factory=list\n251:     )\n252:     # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n253:     block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\n254:         default_factory=dict\n255:     )\n256: \n257: \n258: def _filter_iter(\n259:     l1: Iterable[Any],\n260:     l2: Iterable[Any],\n261:     cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\n262: ) -> list[Any]:\n263:     \"\"\"\n264:     Two-pointer conditional filter.\n265:     e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n266:     returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n267:     \"\"\"\n268:     it = iter(l2)\n269:     res: list[Instruction] = []\n270:     try:\n271:         cur = next(it)\n272:         for val in l1:\n273:             if cond(val, cur):\n274:                 res.append(val)\n275:                 cur = next(it)\n276:     except StopIteration:\n277:         pass\n278:     return res\n279: \n280: \n281: def _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\n282:     insts: list[Instruction] = []\n283:     _initial_push_null(insts)\n284:     insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\n285:     insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\n286:     return insts\n287: \n288: \n289: class ContinueExecutionCache:\n290:     cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n291:     generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n292: \n293:     @classmethod\n294:     def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\n295:         if code not in cls.cache:\n296:             cls.cache[code] = {}\n297:         key = tuple(key)\n298:         if key not in cls.cache[code]:\n299:             cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\n300:         return cls.cache[code][key]\n301: \n302:     @classmethod\n303:     def generate(\n304:         cls,\n305:         code: types.CodeType,\n306:         lineno: int,\n307:         offset: int,\n308:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\n309:         nstack: int,\n310:         argnames: tuple[str, ...],\n311:         argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\n312:         setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\n313:         stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n314:         argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n315:         null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\n316:         # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\n317:         # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\n318:         nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\n319:     ) -> types.CodeType:\n320:         assert offset is not None\n321:         assert not (\n322:             code.co_flags\n323:             & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\n324:         )\n325:         assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\n326:         if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\n327:             return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n328:                 code,\n329:                 lineno,\n330:                 offset,\n331:                 setup_fn_target_offsets,\n332:                 nstack,\n333:                 argnames,\n334:                 argnames_null,\n335:                 setup_fns,\n336:                 stack_ctx_vars,\n337:                 argnames_ctx_vars,\n338:                 null_idxes,\n339:                 nested_code_objs,\n340:             )\n341: \n342:         is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n343:         meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\n344: \n345:         def update(\n346:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n347:         ) -> None:\n348:             meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\n349: \n350:             args = [\"__nested_resume_fns\", \"__nested_frame_values\"]\n351:             args += [f\"___stack{i}\" for i in range(nstack)]\n352:             args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\n353:             freevars = tuple(code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] or []) + tuple(\n354:                 code_options[\"co_freevars\"] or []\n355:             )\n356:             freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\n357:             code_options[\"co_name\"] = (\n358:                 f\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\"\n359:             )\n360:             if is_py311_plus:\n361:                 qualified_path = code_options[\"co_qualname\"].rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n362:                 if len(qualified_path) == 1:\n363:                     code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = code_options[\"co_name\"]\n364:                 else:\n365:                     assert len(qualified_path) == 2\n366:                     module_name, co_name = qualified_path\n367:                     code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = (\n368:                         f\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\"\n369:                     )\n370:             code_options[\"co_firstlineno\"] = lineno\n371:             code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] = ()\n372:             code_options[\"co_freevars\"] = freevars\n373:             code_options[\"co_argcount\"] = len(args)\n374:             code_options[\"co_posonlyargcount\"] = 0\n375:             code_options[\"co_kwonlyargcount\"] = 0\n376:             code_options[\"co_varnames\"] = tuple(\n377:                 args\n378:                 + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\n379:                 + [v for v in code_options[\"co_varnames\"] if v not in args]\n380:                 + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\n381:             )\n382:             code_options[\"co_flags\"] = code_options[\"co_flags\"] & ~(\n383:                 CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\n384:             )\n385:             target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n386: \n387:             prefix = []\n388:             if is_py311_plus:\n389:                 if freevars:\n390:                     prefix.append(\n391:                         create_instruction(\"COPY_FREE_VARS\", arg=len(freevars))\n392:                     )\n393:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"RESUME\", arg=0))\n394: \n395:             # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\n396:             # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\n397:             # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\n398:             prefix.extend(\n399:                 [\n400:                     create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=True),\n401:                     create_instruction(\n402:                         \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n403:                     ),\n404:                 ]\n405:             )\n406: \n407:             cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n408:             hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n409:             hook_target_offsets = {\n410:                 fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n411:                 for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n412:             }\n413:             offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n414:             # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n415:             old_hook_target_remap = {}\n416:             null_idxes_i = 0\n417:             stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n418:             for i in range(nstack):\n419:                 while (\n420:                     null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n421:                     and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n422:                 ):\n423:                     prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n424:                     null_idxes_i += 1\n425:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n426:                 if i in hooks:\n427:                     hook = hooks.pop(i)\n428:                     hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n429:                     prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n430:                     if is_py311_plus:\n431:                         hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n432:                         old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n433:                         meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n434:                         old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n435:                 if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n436:                     # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n437:                     # Load args for context variable and construct it\n438:                     prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n439: \n440:             if is_py311_plus:\n441:                 # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n442:                 # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n443:                 meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n444:                     reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n445:                 )\n446: \n447:             assert not hooks\n448: \n449:             # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n450:             # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n451:             for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n452:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n453:                 prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n454:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n455: \n456:             # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n457:             if argnames_null:\n458:                 assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n459:                 for v in argnames_null:\n460:                     assert v not in args\n461:                     prefix.extend(\n462:                         [\n463:                             create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n464:                             create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n465:                         ]\n466:                     )\n467: \n468:             # Call nested resume function\n469:             if nested_code_objs:\n470:                 prefix.extend(\n471:                     [\n472:                         # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n473:                         *add_push_null(\n474:                             [\n475:                                 create_instruction(\n476:                                     \"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"\n477:                                 ),\n478:                                 create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n479:                                 create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n480:                             ]\n481:                         ),\n482:                         # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\n483:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n484:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n485:                         create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n486:                         # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\n487:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n488:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n489:                         create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n490:                         # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\n491:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n492:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n493:                         create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n494:                         # create [\n495:                         #     __nested_resume_fns,\n496:                         #     __nested_frame_values,\n497:                         #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\n498:                         # ]\n499:                         create_instruction(\"LIST_EXTEND\", arg=1),\n500:                         # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\n501:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n502:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n503:                         create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n504:                         # delete __nested values\n505:                         create_instruction(\"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n506:                         create_instruction(\n507:                             \"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"\n508:                         ),\n509:                         # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\n510:                         # in the nested resume\n511:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n512:                         create_instruction(\n513:                             \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n514:                         ),\n515:                         # finish the call\n516:                         create_instruction(\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\", arg=0),\n517:                     ]\n518:                 )\n519:             else:\n520:                 # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\n521:                 prefix.extend(\n522:                     [\n523:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n524:                         create_instruction(\n525:                             \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n526:                         ),\n527:                     ]\n528:                 )\n529: \n530:             prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\n531: \n532:             # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\n533:             # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\n534:             # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\n535:             for inst in instructions:\n536:                 if inst.offset == target.offset:\n537:                     break\n538:                 inst.starts_line = None\n539:                 if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n540:                     inst.positions = None\n541: \n542:             if cleanup:\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n545: \n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\n550:                     if (\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n553:                     ):\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n556:                         ]\n557: \n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n560: \n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n563:         return new_code\n564: \n565:     @staticmethod\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n567:         \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n568:         return [\n569:             create_load_const(None),\n570:             create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n571:         ]\n572: \n573:     @classmethod\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n575:         cls,\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\n577:         lineno: int,\n578:         offset: int,\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n580:         *args: Any,\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\n582:         \"\"\"\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n587:         resume functions).\n588:         \"\"\"\n589: \n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n591:             code\n592:         ]\n593:         new_offset = -1\n594: \n595:         def find_new_offset(\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n597:         ) -> None:\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n601:             (new_target,) = (\n602:                 i2\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n604:                 if i1 is target\n605:             )\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\n609: \n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\n612: \n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n620:                     new_offset\n621:                 ] = {}\n622: \n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n625:                 ) -> None:\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n630:                     # replaced.\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n635:                         ):\n636:                             break\n637:                         if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n639: \n640:                     # offsets into prefix\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n643:                     ):\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n645: \n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n649:                     )\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n653:                     )\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n656:                     )\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n659:                         targets,\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n661:                     )\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n664: \n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n666: \n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n671:             )\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n674:         )\n675: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py"
  }
}
3. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -

  "endLine": 100,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Core module responsible for converting Python bytecode into TorchDynamo's symbolic execution format.\\n3: \\n4: This module implements the bytecode-level tracing system that allows TorchDynamo to analyze\\n5: and transform Python code. It converts Python bytecode instructions into a symbolic format\\n6: that tracks the flow of tensors and other values through the program.\\n7: \\n8: Key components:\\n9: - InstructionTranslatorBase: Base class for converting bytecode to symbolic execution\\n10: - InstructionTranslator: Main translator for function bytecode\\n11: - InliningInstructionTranslator: Handles inlining of called functions\\n12: - SpeculationLog: Manages state for speculative execution and rollback\\n13: \\n14: The symbolic conversion process handles:\\n15: - Control flow (loops, conditionals, etc.)\\n16: - Function inlining and call stack management\\n17: - Tracking of program values and side effects\\n18: - Graph breaks and resumption points\\n19: - Exception handling and stack frame management\\n20: \\n21: This is a core part of TorchDynamo's tracing system that enables ahead-of-time\\n22: optimization of PyTorch programs.\\n23: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n24: \\n25: from __future__ import annotations\\n26: \\n27: import collections\\n28: import collections.abc\\n29: import contextlib\\n30: import copy\\n31: import dataclasses\\n32: import dis\\n33: import functools\\n34: import importlib\\n35: import inspect\\n36: import itertools\\n37: import linecache\\n38: import logging\\n39: import operator\\n40: import re\\n41: import sys\\n42: import threading\\n43: import traceback\\n44: import types\\n45: import weakref\\n46: from traceback import StackSummary\\n47: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, NoReturn, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\\n48: from typing_extensions import TypeAlias, TypeIs\\n49: from unittest.mock import patch\\n50: \\n51: import torch\\n52: import torch._logging\\n53: from torch._dynamo.exc import ObservedException, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\\n54: from torch._guards import tracing, TracingContext\\n55: from torch._logging.structured import dump_file\\n56: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import guard_bool\\n57: from torch.utils._functools import cache_method\\n58: \\n59: from . import (\\n60:     config,\\n61:     exc,\\n62:     graph_break_hints,\\n63:     logging as torchdynamo_logging,\\n64:     trace_rules,\\n65:     variables,\\n66: )\\n67: from .bytecode_analysis import (\\n68:     get_indexof,\\n69:     JUMP_OPNAMES,\\n70:     livevars_analysis,\\n71:     propagate_line_nums,\\n72: )\\n73: from .bytecode_transformation import (\\n74:     cleaned_instructions,\\n75:     create_binary_slice,\\n76:     create_call_function,\\n77:     create_copy,\\n78:     create_dup_top,\\n79:     create_instruction,\\n80:     create_jump_absolute,\\n81:     create_rot_n,\\n82:     create_swap,\\n83:     get_code_keys,\\n84:     Instruction,\\n85:     is_generator,\\n86:     is_jump_absolute,\\n87:     unique_id,\\n88: )\\n89: from .code_context import code_context\\n90: from .codegen import PyCodegen\\n91: from .exc import (\\n92:     ArgsMismatchError,\\n93:     BackendCompilerFailed,\\n94:     collapse_resume_frames,\\n95:     format_graph_break_message,\\n96:     get_stack_above_dynamo,\\n97:     ResumePrologueTracingError,\\n98:     unimplemented_v2,\\n99:     Unsupported,\\n100: )\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Core module responsible for converting Python bytecode into TorchDynamo's symbolic execution format.\n3: \n4: This module implements the bytecode-level tracing system that allows TorchDynamo to analyze\n5: and transform Python code. It converts Python bytecode instructions into a symbolic format\n6: that tracks the flow of tensors and other values through the program.\n7: \n8: Key components:\n9: - InstructionTranslatorBase: Base class for converting bytecode to symbolic execution\n10: - InstructionTranslator: Main translator for function bytecode\n11: - InliningInstructionTranslator: Handles inlining of called functions\n12: - SpeculationLog: Manages state for speculative execution and rollback\n13: \n14: The symbolic conversion process handles:\n15: - Control flow (loops, conditionals, etc.)\n16: - Function inlining and call stack management\n17: - Tracking of program values and side effects\n18: - Graph breaks and resumption points\n19: - Exception handling and stack frame management\n20: \n21: This is a core part of TorchDynamo's tracing system that enables ahead-of-time\n22: optimization of PyTorch programs.\n23: \"\"\"\n24: \n25: from __future__ import annotations\n26: \n27: import collections\n28: import collections.abc\n29: import contextlib\n30: import copy\n31: import dataclasses\n32: import dis\n33: import functools\n34: import importlib\n35: import inspect\n36: import itertools\n37: import linecache\n38: import logging\n39: import operator\n40: import re\n41: import sys\n42: import threading\n43: import traceback\n44: import types\n45: import weakref\n46: from traceback import StackSummary\n47: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, NoReturn, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\n48: from typing_extensions import TypeAlias, TypeIs\n49: from unittest.mock import patch\n50: \n51: import torch\n52: import torch._logging\n53: from torch._dynamo.exc import ObservedException, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\n54: from torch._guards import tracing, TracingContext\n55: from torch._logging.structured import dump_file\n56: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import guard_bool\n57: from torch.utils._functools import cache_method\n58: \n59: from . import (\n60:     config,\n61:     exc,\n62:     graph_break_hints,\n63:     logging as torchdynamo_logging,\n64:     trace_rules,\n65:     variables,\n66: )\n67: from .bytecode_analysis import (\n68:     get_indexof,\n69:     JUMP_OPNAMES,\n70:     livevars_analysis,\n71:     propagate_line_nums,\n72: )\n73: from .bytecode_transformation import (\n74:     cleaned_instructions,\n75:     create_binary_slice,\n76:     create_call_function,\n77:     create_copy,\n78:     create_dup_top,\n79:     create_instruction,\n80:     create_jump_absolute,\n81:     create_rot_n,\n82:     create_swap,\n83:     get_code_keys,\n84:     Instruction,\n85:     is_generator,\n86:     is_jump_absolute,\n87:     unique_id,\n88: )\n89: from .code_context import code_context\n90: from .codegen import PyCodegen\n91: from .exc import (\n92:     ArgsMismatchError,\n93:     BackendCompilerFailed,\n94:     collapse_resume_frames,\n95:     format_graph_break_message,\n96:     get_stack_above_dynamo,\n97:     ResumePrologueTracingError,\n98:     unimplemented_v2,\n99:     Unsupported,\n100: )\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL1-100"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"2060:                 context=f\\\"raised exception {curr_exc.python_type_name()}({curr_exc.args})\\\",  # type: ignore[union-attr]\\n2061:                 explanation=observed_exn_gb_explanation,\\n2062:                 hints=[\\n2063:                     *graph_break_hints.USER_ERROR,\\n2064:                     *graph_break_hints.SUPPORTABLE,\\n2065:                 ],\\n2066:             )\\n2067: \\n2068:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n2069:             exn_tab_entry = self.current_instruction.exn_tab_entry\\n2070:             if exn_tab_entry:\\n2071:                 # Implementation is based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt\\n2072: \\n2073:                 # 1) pop values from the stack until it matches the stack depth\\n2074:                 # for the handler\\n2075:                 while len(self.stack) > exn_tab_entry.depth:\\n2076:                     self.pop()\\n2077: \\n2078:                 # 2) if 'lasti' is true, then push the offset that the exception was raised at\\n2079:                 if exn_tab_entry.lasti:\\n2080:                     self.push(\\n2081:                         variables.ConstantVariable(self.current_instruction.offset)\\n2082:                     )\\n2083: \\n2084:                 # 3) push the exception to the stack\\n2085:                 self.push(self.exn_vt_stack.get_current_exception())\\n2086: \\n2087:                 # 4) jump to the handler\\n2088:                 self.jump(exn_tab_entry)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2089:             else:\\n2090:                 # No handler found. Bubble the exception to the parent\\n2091:                 # instruction translator. We use special exception for this.\\n2092:                 self.stack.clear()\\n2093:                 if type(self) is InstructionTranslator:\\n2094:                     bubble_exception_to_interpreter()\\n2095:                 raise raised_exception\\n2096:         else:\\n2097:             if len(self.block_stack):\\n2098:                 # base implementation - https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L4455\\n2099: \\n2100:                 block_stack_entry = self.block_stack.pop()\\n2101: \\n2102:                 while block_stack_entry.inst.opname == \\\"EXCEPT_HANDLER\\\":\\n2103:                     # TODO(anijain2305) - This is not tested .. unable to create a testcase\\n2104:                     # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L1456\\n2105:                     self.popn(3)\\n2106:                     self.exn_vt_stack.pop()\\n2107:                     if len(self.block_stack) == 0:\\n2108:                         # No handler found in this frame. Bubble the exception to the parent\\n2109:                         # instruction translator.\\n2110:                         self.stack.clear()\\n2111:                         if type(self) is InstructionTranslator:\\n2112:                             unimplemented_v2(\\n2113:                                 gb_type=\\\"Observed exception (EXCEPT_HANDLER)\\\",\\n2114:                                 context=str(raised_exception),\\n2115:                                 explanation=observed_exn_gb_explanation\\n2116:                                 + \\\" This graph break is unexpected.\\\",\\n2117:                                 hints=[*graph_break_hints.DYNAMO_BUG],\\n2118:                             )\\n2119: \\n2120:                         raise raised_exception\\n2121: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2060-2120\"}",

self.popn(2)\\n2444: \\n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n2475:                 hints=[],\\n2476:             )\\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\\n2478:             self,\\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\\\"store_attr\\\", [self.frame_summary()]),\\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\\n2481:         )\\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\\n2483:         self.popn(2)\\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\\n2487:             )\\n2488:         )\\n2489: \\n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\\n2491:         obj = self.pop()\\n2492:         BuiltinVariable(delattr).call_function(\\n2493:             self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2494:             [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval)],\\n2495:             {},\\n2496:         )\\n2497: \\n2498:     def create_call_resume_at(\\n2499:         self,\\n2500:         inst: Instruction,\\n2501:         all_stack_locals_metadata: Any,\\n2502:         disable_current_frame_resume: bool,\\n2503:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n2504:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2505:         Codegen resume function(s) and call it.\\n2506:         Assumes that the unsupported instruction has already been run.\\n2507: \\n2508:         Expects the stack to be in the state:\\n2509:             [\\n2510:                 frame N locals,\\n2511:                 frame N-1 stack + locals,\\n2512:                 ...,\\n2513:                 frame 1 stack + locals\\n2514:             ], frame N stack (post-instruction)\\n2515: \\n2516:         Args:\\n2517:             - inst: the instruction of the current (deepest) frame to resume at\\n2518:             - all_stack_locals_metadata: metadata returned from OutputGraph.compile_subgraph - contains\\n2519:                 metadata such as local names, NULL positions, stack length, etc.\\n2520:             - disable_current_frame_resume: If True, disable tracing on the current frame's resume function.\\n2521: \\\"RETURN_VALUE\\\":\\n2527:             return [create_instruction(\\\"RETURN_VALUE\\\")]\\n2528:         elif inst.opname == \\\"RETURN_CONST\\\":\\n2529:             return [create_instruction(\\\"RETURN_CONST\\\", argval=inst.argval)]\\n2530: \\n2531:     "content": "2430:         self.push(result)\n2431: \n2432:     def LOAD_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2433:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\n2434:             if inst.arg % 2:\n2435:                 self.LOAD_METHOD(inst)\n2436:                 return\n2437:         self._load_attr(inst)\n2438: \n2439:     def STORE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2440:         speculation = self.speculate()\n2441:         if speculation.failed(self):\n2442:             return self.store_attr_graph_break(inst)\n2443:         val, obj = self.popn(2)\n2444: \n2445:         if isinstance(obj, NNModuleVariable) and not isinstance(val, ConstantVariable):\n2446:             # We don't allow side effects during export on non-constant values\n2447:             # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\n2448:             assert not self.export, (\n2449:                 f\"Mutating module attribute {inst.argval} during export.\"\n2450:             )\n2451: \n2452:         try:\n2453:             BuiltinVariable(setattr).call_function(\n2454:                 self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2455:                 [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval), val],\n2456:                 {},\n2457:             )\n2458:             return\n2459:         except Unsupported as e:\n2460:             if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2461:                 raise\n2462:             log.debug(\"STORE_ATTR triggered compile\", exc_info=True)\n2463:             e.remove_from_stats()\n2464:             e.add_to_stats(\"graph_break\")\n2465:         speculation.fail_and_restart_analysis(self.error_on_graph_break)\n2466: \n2467:     def store_attr_graph_break(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2468:         log_graph_break(self.code_options, reason=\"STORE_ATTR-caused graph break\")\n2469:         if not self.should_compile_partial_graph():\n2470:             unimplemented_v2(\n2471:                 gb_type=\"Should not compile partial graph (STORE_ATTR)\",\n2472:                 context=\"\",\n2473:                 explanation=\"Dynamo has determined when encountering an unsupported \"\n2474:                 \"STORE_ATTR instruction (i.e. `obj.attr = val`) that it should not compile the partial graph.\",\n2475:                 hints=[],\n2476:             )\n2477:         all_stack_locals_metadata = self.output.compile_subgraph(\n2478:             self,\n2479:             reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n2480:             stack_pops=2,\n2481:         )\n2482:         self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])\n2483:         self.popn(2)\n2484:         self.output.add_output_instructions(\n2485:             self.create_call_resume_at(\n2486:                 self.next_instruction, all_stack_locals_metadata, False\n2487:             )\n2488:         )\n2489: \n2490:     def DELETE_ATTR(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n2491:         obj = self.pop()\n2492:         BuiltinVariable(delattr).call_function(\n2493:             self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2494:             [obj, ConstantVariable.create(inst.argval)],\n2495:             {},\n2496:         )\n2497: \n2498:     def create_call_resume_at(\n2499:         self,\n2500:         inst: Instruction,\n2501:         all_stack_locals_metadata: Any,\n2502:         disable_current_frame_resume: bool,\n2503:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\n2504:         \"\"\"\n2505:         Codegen resume function(s) and call it.\n2506:         Assumes that the unsupported instruction has already been run.\n2507: \n2508:         Expects the stack to be in the state:\n2509:             [\n2510:                 frame N locals,\n2511:                 frame N-1 stack + locals,\n2512:                 ...,\n2513:                 frame 1 stack + locals\n2514:             ], frame N stack (post-instruction)\n2515: \n2516:         Args:\n2517:             - inst: the instruction of the current (deepest) frame to resume at\n2518:             - all_stack_locals_metadata: metadata returned from OutputGraph.compile_subgraph - contains\n2519:                 metadata such as local names, NULL positions, stack length, etc.\n2520:             - disable_current_frame_resume: If True, disable tracing on the current frame's resume function.\n2521:                 Used for implementing nested step_graph_break.\n2522:         \"\"\"\n2523: \n2524:         self.instruction_pointer = None\n2525: \n2526:         if inst.opname == \"RETURN_VALUE\":\n2527:             return [create_instruction(\"RETURN_VALUE\")]\n2528:         elif inst.opname == \"RETURN_CONST\":\n2529:             return [create_instruction(\"RETURN_CONST\", argval=inst.argval)]\n2530: \n2531: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2430-2530"
  }
}

Handle inactive context variables.\\n2567:         # The resume function assumes that context variables are the class, NOT the object.\\n2568:         # e.g. torch.set_grad_enabled(True) will be reconstructed as torch.set_grad_enabled\\n2569:         # NOTE: if the unsupported instruction modifies the inactive context variable, it may\\n2570:         # result in silent incorrectness!\\n2571:         for i, meta in enumerate(all_stack_locals_metadata):\\n2572:             if i == 0 and disable_current_frame_resume:\\n2573:                 continue\\n2574: \\n2575:             for (j, _), j_orig in zip(meta.stack_ctx_args, meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig):\\n2576:                 # This handles the case where we\\n2618:             # graph break again in a nested function before jump-resuming\\n2619:             # this frame.\\n2620:             if is_jump_absolute(resume_inst):\\n2621:                 assert resume_inst.target\\n2622:                 resume_inst = resume_inst.target\\n2623:             resume_name = unique_id(f\\\"__resume_at_{resume_inst.offset}\\\")\\n2624:             resume_names.append(resume_name)\\n2625: \\n2626:             # the current instruction is a CALL.\\n2630:             if cur_tx is self:\\n2631:                 reads = livevars_analysis(cur_tx.instructions, resume_inst)\\n2632:                 all_argnames = tuple(\\n2633:                     k\\n2634:                     for k in cur_tx.symbolic_locals.keys()\\n2635:                     if k in reads and k not in cur_tx.cell_and_freevars()\\n2636:                 )\\n2637:                 argnames_null_set = set(meta.locals_null_keys)\\n2638:                 argnames = tuple(k for k in all_argnames if k not in argnames_null_set)\\n2639:                 argnames_null = tuple(k for k in all_argnames if k in argnames_null_set)\\n2640: \\n2641:                 # compile_subgraph did not codegen any NULLs,\\n2683:             # so we should not count NullVariables\\n2684:             stack_len = len(cur_tx.stack) - len(meta.stack_null_idxes)\\n2685: \\n2686:             new_code: types.CodeType = ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n2687:                 cur_tx.f_code,\\n2688:                 cur_tx.lineno,\\n2689:                 resume_inst.offset,\\n2690:                 tuple(b.target.offset for b in cur_tx.block_stack),\\n2691:                 stack_len,\\n2692:                 argnames,\\n2693:                 argnames_null,\\n2694:                 tuple(b.resume_fn() for b in cur_tx.block_stack),\\n2695:                 tuple(meta.stack_ctx_args),\\n2696:                 tuple(meta.locals_ctx_args),\\n2697:                 tuple(meta.stack_null_idxes),\\n2698:                 tuple(resume_codes),\\n2699:             )\\n2700:             resume_codes.append(new_code)\\n2701: \\n2702:             # Add original GraphModule context to the resume function to handle\\n2703:             # the case of a graph break while tracing a GraphModule\\n2704:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(cur_tx.f_code).get(\\n2705:                 \\\"orig_graphmodule\\\", lambda: None\\n2706:             )()\\n2707:             if orig_graphmodule_maybe is not None:\\n2708:                 code_context.get_context(new_code)[\\\"orig_graphmodule\\\"] = weakref.ref(\\n2709:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe\\n2710:                 )\\n2711: \\n2712:             # add resume function to the global scope\\n2713:             if new_code.co_freevars:\\n2714:                 # expose code object for debugging purposes\\n2715:                 cur_tx.output.install_global_unsafe(resume_name, new_code)\\n2716:                 package_name = None\\n2717:             else:\\n2718:                 # This is safe: we pre-generate a unique name\\n2719:                 cur_tx.output.install_global_unsafe(\\n2720:                     resume_name,\\n2721:                     types.FunctionType(new_code, cur_tx.f_globals, resume_name),\\n2722:                 )\\n2723:                 package_name = resume_name\\n2724: \\n2725:             if cur_tx.package is not None:\\n2726:                 cur_tx.package.add_resume_function(\\n2727:                     new_code, cur_tx.f_globals[\\\"__name__\\\"], package_name\\n2728:                 )\\n2729: \\n2730:         if disable_current_frame_resume:\\n2731:             from .eval_frame import skip_code\\n2732: \\n2733:             skip_code(resume_codes[0])\\n2734: \\n2735:         # load first resume function (to be called this frame)\\n2736:         if resume_codes[-1].co_freevars:\\n2737:             cg.make_function_with_closure(\\n2738:                 txes[-1], resume_names[-1], resume_codes[-1], True, 1\\n2739:             )\\n2740:         else:\\n2741:             cg.extend_output(cg.load_function_name(resume_names[-1], True, 1))\\n2742: \\n2743:         # load all other resume functions (to be called later)\\n2744:         resume_names.pop()\\n2745:         resume_codes.pop()\\n2746:         for tx, name, code in zip(txes, resume_names, resume_codes):\\n2747:             if code.co_freevars:\\n2748:                 cg.make_function_with_closure(tx, name, code, False, 0)\\n2749:             else:\\n2750:                 cg.extend_output(cg.load_function_name(name, False, 0))\\n2751:         cg.extend_output(\\n2752:             [\\n2753:                 create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=len(resume_codes)),\\n2754:                 *create_swap(2),\\n2755:             ]\\n2756:         )\\n2757: \\n2758:         # resume 1 (+ NULL), [resume N, ..., resume 2], frames\\n2759: \\n2760:         # load top level-frame; final stack state should be:\\n2761:         # first resume function (+ NULL),\\n2762:         # [\\n2763:         #     [resume N, ..., resume 2],\\n2764:         #     [\\n2765:         #         frame N stack + locals,\\n2766:         #         ...,\\n2767:         #         frame 2 stack + locals,\\n2768:         #     ], *(frame 1 stack + locals)\\n2769:         # ]\\n2770:         cg.extend_output(\\n2771:             [\\n2772:                 create_dup_top(),\\n2773:                 create_dup_top(),\\n2774:                 # frames, frames, frames\\n2775:                 cg.create_load_const(-1),\\n2776:                 cg.create_binary_subscr(),\\n2777:                 # frames, frames, frames[-1]\\n2778:                 *create_swap(2),\\n2779:                 # frames, frames[-1], frames\\n2780:                 cg.create_load_const(-1),\\n2781:                 create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n2782:             ]\\n2783:         )\\n2784: \\n2785:         # TOS: resumes, frames (popped), frame 1 stack + locals\\n2786:         cg.extend_output(\\n2787:             [\\n2788:                 *create_rot_n(3),\\n2789:                 create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=2),\\n2790:                 *create_swap(2),\\n2791:                 # [resumes, frames (popped)], frame 1 stack + locals\\n2792:                 create_instruction(\\\"LIST_EXTEND\\\", arg=1),\\n2793:             ]\\n2794:         )\\n2795: \\n2796:         # TOS: [resumes, frames, *(frame 1 stack + locals)]\\n2797:         cg.extend_output(\\n2798:             [\\n2799:                 create_instruction(\\\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\\\", arg=0),\\n2800:                 create_instruction(\\\"RETURN_VALUE\\\"),\\n2801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2670-2800\"}",
