    "content": "2670:                         *create_binary_slice(meta.num_stack, None, True),\n2671:                         # frames[i][num_stack:] = frame i live locals\n2672:                     ]\n2673:                 )\n2674:                 # current stack state: frames\n2675:             else:\n2676:                 argnames = tuple(meta.locals_names.keys())\n2677:                 argnames_null = tuple(meta.locals_null_keys)\n2678: \n2679:             if sys.version_info < (3, 12):\n2680:                 assert len(argnames_null) == 0, \"variables should not be NULL in < 3.12\"\n2681: \n2682:             # compile_subgraph did not codegen any NULLs,\n2683:             # so we should not count NullVariables\n2684:             stack_len = len(cur_tx.stack) - len(meta.stack_null_idxes)\n2685: \n2686:             new_code: types.CodeType = ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n2687:                 cur_tx.f_code,\n2688:                 cur_tx.lineno,\n2689:                 resume_inst.offset,\n2690:                 tuple(b.target.offset for b in cur_tx.block_stack),\n2691:                 stack_len,\n2692:                 argnames,\n2693:                 argnames_null,\n2694:                 tuple(b.resume_fn() for b in cur_tx.block_stack),\n2695:                 tuple(meta.stack_ctx_args),\n2696:                 tuple(meta.locals_ctx_args),\n2697:                 tuple(meta.stack_null_idxes),\n2698:                 tuple(resume_codes),\n2699:             )\n2700:             resume_codes.append(new_code)\n2701: \n2702:             # Add original GraphModule context to the resume function to handle\n2703:             # the case of a graph break while tracing a GraphModule\n2704:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(cur_tx.f_code).get(\n2705:                 \"orig_graphmodule\", lambda: None\n2706:             )()\n2707:             if orig_graphmodule_maybe is not None:\n2708:                 code_context.get_context(new_code)[\"orig_graphmodule\"] = weakref.ref(\n2709:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe\n2710:                 )\n2711: \n2712:             # add resume function to the global scope\n2713:             if new_code.co_freevars:\n2714:                 # expose code object for debugging purposes\n2715:                 cur_tx.output.install_global_unsafe(resume_name, new_code)\n2716:                 package_name = None\n2717:             else:\n2718:                 # This is safe: we pre-generate a unique name\n2719:                 cur_tx.output.install_global_unsafe(\n2720:                     resume_name,\n2721:                     types.FunctionType(new_code, cur_tx.f_globals, resume_name),\n2722:                 )\n2723:                 package_name = resume_name\n2724: \n2725:             if cur_tx.package is not None:\n2726:                 cur_tx.package.add_resume_function(\n2727:                     new_code, cur_tx.f_globals[\"__name__\"], package_name\n2728:                 )\n2729: \n2730:         if disable_current_frame_resume:\n2731:             from .eval_frame import skip_code\n2732: \n2733:             skip_code(resume_codes[0])\n2734: \n2735:         # load first resume function (to be called this frame)\n2736:         if resume_codes[-1].co_freevars:\n2737:             cg.make_function_with_closure(\n2738:                 txes[-1], resume_names[-1], resume_codes[-1], True, 1\n2739:             )\n2740:         else:\n2741:             cg.extend_output(cg.load_function_name(resume_names[-1], True, 1))\n2742: \n2743:         # load all other resume functions (to be called later)\n2744:         resume_names.pop()\n2745:         resume_codes.pop()\n2746:         for tx, name, code in zip(txes, resume_names, resume_codes):\n2747:             if code.co_freevars:\n2748:                 cg.make_function_with_closure(tx, name, code, False, 0)\n2749:             else:\n2750:                 cg.extend_output(cg.load_function_name(name, False, 0))\n2751:         cg.extend_output(\n2752:             [\n2753:                 create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=len(resume_codes)),\n2754:                 *create_swap(2),\n2755:             ]\n2756:         )\n2757: \n2758:         # resume 1 (+ NULL), [resume N, ..., resume 2], frames\n2759: \n2760:         # load top level-frame; final stack state should be:\n2761:         # first resume function (+ NULL),\n2762:         # [\n2763:         #     [resume N, ..., resume 2],\n2764:         #     [\n2765:         #         frame N stack + locals,\n2766:         #         ...,\n2767:         #         frame 2 stack + locals,\n2768:         #     ], *(frame 1 stack + locals)\n2769:         # ]\n2770:         cg.extend_output(\n2771:             [\n2772:                 create_dup_top(),\n2773:                 create_dup_top(),\n2774:                 # frames, frames, frames\n2775:                 cg.create_load_const(-1),\n2776:                 cg.create_binary_subscr(),\n2777:                 # frames, frames, frames[-1]\n2778:                 *create_swap(2),\n2779:                 # frames, frames[-1], frames\n2780:                 cg.create_load_const(-1),\n2781:                 create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n2782:             ]\n2783:         )\n2784: \n2785:         # TOS: resumes, frames (popped), frame 1 stack + locals\n2786:         cg.extend_output(\n2787:             [\n2788:                 *create_rot_n(3),\n2789:                 create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n2790:                 *create_swap(2),\n2791:                 # [resumes, frames (popped)], frame 1 stack + locals\n2792:                 create_instruction(\"LIST_EXTEND\", arg=1),\n2793:             ]\n2794:         )\n2795: \n2796:         # TOS: [resumes, frames, *(frame 1 stack + locals)]\n2797:         cg.extend_output(\n2798:             [\n2799:                 create_instruction(\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\", arg=0),\n2800:                 create_instruction(\"RETURN_VALUE\"),\n2801: ",

wrap torch.profiler.* as NullContextVariable and do nothing\\n173:         def fn(x):\\n174:             y = x**2\\n175:             with torch.profiler.profile():\\n176:                 y = y + 2\\n177:                 with torch.profiler.record_function(\\\"my_function\\\"):\\n178:                     z = y**3\\n179:                     z.tolist()  # graph break\\n180:                     z = z + 1\\n181:             return z\\n182: \\n183:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\\n184:         ref = fn(x)\\n185:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n186:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n187:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n188:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n189:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n190: \\n191:     def test_autograd_profiler(self):\\n192:         # wrap torch.autograd.profiler.* as NullContextVariable and do nothing\\n193:         def fn(x):\\n194:             y = x**2\\n195:             with torch.autograd.profiler.profile():\\n196:                 y = y + 2\\n197:                 with torch.autograd.profiler.record_function(\\\"my_function\\\"):\\n198:                     z = y**3\\n199:                     z.tolist()  # graph break\\n200:                     z = z + 1\\n201:             return z\\n202: \\n203:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\\n204:         ref = fn(x)\\n205:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n206:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n207:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n208:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n209:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n210: \\n211:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \\\"requires cuda\\\")\\n212:     def test_cuda_stream_context_manager1(self):\\n213:         def fn(x):\\n214:             s = torch.cuda.Stream()\\n215:             x = torch.mul(x, 5)\\n216:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\\n217:             current_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()\\n218:             s.wait_stream(current_stream)\\n219:             with torch.cuda.stream(s):\\n220:                 x = torch.relu(x)\\n221: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL140-220\"}",

{
  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"26a1b9c\",\"date\":\"2025-09-08\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D81938289](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81938289)\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162318\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914\"},{\"author\":\"jon-chuang <jon-chuang@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"d1d5d16\",\"date\":\"2023-02-14\",\"message\":\"dynamo: handle straight-line graph breaks for autocast context manager with constant args (#94137)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/93890\\n\\nWe do the following:\\n1. fix __init__constructor for `AutocastModeVariable` with exisiting `mode` while copying\\n2. `resume_execution` is made aware of constant args (`target_values`), by storing said args in `ReenterWith`. To propagate between subgraphs (in straightline code), we also store the constant args in the downstream's `code_options[\\\"co_consts\\\"]` if not already.\\n\\n---\\n\\nFuture work:\\n1. handle instantiating context manager in non-inline... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"dynamo: handle straight-line graph breaks for autocast context manager with constant args (#94137)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d1d5d16df3766bc6a6c9ebe1168ff652bcdcaa5e\"},{\"author\":\"Bin Bao <binbao@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"7e9cbc6\",\"date\":\"2024-01-11\",\"message\":\"[CI] Catch more exception types when running eager in PT2 tests (#117120)\\n\\nSummary: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/7467073391/job/20320251143#step:16:1332 shows a case where model loading fails with KeyError but the error is not logged in the report csv file, which can cause an eager model failure silently ignored in the PT2 integration test.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/117120\\nApproved by: https://github.com/huydhn\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[CI] Catch more exception types when running eager in PT2 tests (#117120)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7e9cbc683495104e96b12fa20a0190aa1d3b451f\"},{\"author\":\"drisspg <drisspguessous@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"5b6ba41\",\"date\":\"2023-08-31\",\"message\":\"Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)\\n\\n# Summary\\nAs a stop gap to supporting the FP8 Dtype within inductor we would like to fallback to eager. Currently there are 3 ops that are needed for this:\\n`_scaled_mm` ( matmul for fp8 types)\\n`clone` (for creating new copies of fp8 tensors)\\n`to` ( for converting to and from fp8 types).\\n\\nThis PR registers a fallback for _scaled_mm. And adds fp8 to trigger `unsupported_input_tensor`\\n\\nPrior to these changes this was failing with:\\n``` Shell\\n  File \\\"/home/drisspg/meta/pytorch/torch/_inductor/codegen/triton_utils.py\\\", line 11, in signature_of\\n... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5b6ba4110be80934bc18461bc8f04d1cb2b6b148\"},{\"author\":\"Aaron Gokaslan <aaronGokaslan@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"d22c403\",\"date\":\"2025-05-10\",\"message\":\"[Ez][BE]: Fix KeyError LOGNAME (#153324)\\n\\nUnblocks #153020 which accidentally improves the CircularImportLinter to check all Python files. It doesn't set a logname so it errors, there is another FSDP script which already defaults LOGNAME to '' if not specified, this does the same.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153324\\nApproved by: https://github.com/awgu\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Ez][BE]: Fix KeyError LOGNAME (#153324)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d22c40373f97a1ca288bb39fcee3a4333ecd0681\"},{\"author\":\"Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"162eba2\",\"date\":\"2024-10-18\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Remove `mutable_local.source` and index on `VariableTracker` rather than `MutableLocalBase` (#137905)\\n\\nThis patch addresses parts of the side-effect refactor proposed in #133027;\\nspecifically, it does 3 things:\\n\\n1. Change `SideEffects.store_attr_mutations` and `PyCodegen.tempvars`\\n   to index on `VariableTracker` rather than `MutableLocalBase`.\\n2. Remove the `source` field from `MutableSideEffects` and\\n   `AttributeMutation`, and use `VariableTracker.source` instead.\\n3. Plumb a `overridden_sources: Dict[Source, Source]` from\\n   `handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists` to `PyCodegen` so that ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Remove `mutable_local.source` and index on `VariableTracker` rather than `MutableLocalBase` https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/58443\\n\\nTest Plan: arc lint\\n\\nReviewed By: vkuzo\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D28494119\\n\\nfbshipit-source-id: 05abf4e12051afc237096812fb0ee08a8b9447f9\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fix KeyError returned by _maybe_get_last_node_only_observer (#58443)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/07da584dbd2fa89a0967892a2e977f62394f92c5\"},{\"author\":\"titaiwangms <titaiwang@microsoft.com>\",\"commit\":\"b23f11c\",\"date\":\"2024-12-18\",\"message\":\"[ONNX] Automatically convert dynamic_axes to dynamic_shapes with torch.export.Dim.AUTO (#143158)\\n\\nWith https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/133620 introducing Dim.AUTO, we can now automatically convert dynamic_axes to dynamic_shapes without specifying min and max. However, exporting still could be crashed when there are same specs shared between inputs and there is no guarantee that the axes will be dynamic (see PR description).\\n\\n~~Therefore, a~~ follow-up PR should create a post-processing ONNX side pass to ~~enable the missed dynamic axes~~ rename the dynamic shapes (s0,  s1, ...) to dyna... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[ONNX] Automatically convert dynamic_axes to dynamic_shapes with torch.export.Dim.AUTO (#143158)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b23f11c529c18638cd13749613a9483ebc33ed9c\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"ae80bce\",\"date\":\"2024-09-24\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] refactor resume_execution.py to use bytecode templates (#136483)\\n\\nUse bytecode from template instead of hardcoding bytecode in resume_execution.py. Gets rid of a lot of Python-version dependent bytecode generation. Also makes resume_execution.py easier to support in future Python version updates.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/136483\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] refactor resume_execution.py to use bytecode templates (#136483)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/ae80bce4966271f7ce0bdd45b9baa62955a8ef60\"},{\"author\":\"Edward Z. Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"386b313\",\"date\":\"2024-09-06\",\"message\":\"Handle KeyError for compiler collective in scalars too (#135385)\\n\\nSigned-off-by: Edward Z. Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/135385\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Handle KeyError for compiler collective in scalars too (#135385)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/386b313028e88de2e8d4f3ef7eba5d286fe7c8ee\"},{\"author\":\"Huy Do <huydhn@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"3b2ddb6\",\"date\":\"2024-01-11\",\"message\":\"Update TorchBench pinned commit (#117073)\\n\\n~~To match their recent v4.36.2 release https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/commits/v4.36.2. I think this can be updated in main too because the current pinned commit is already 4-month old.~~\\n\\nCheck with @desertfire, trying to update TorchBench pinned commit instead.\\n\\nThe test is also failing in main https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/7467073391/job/20320251143#step:16:1120, but for some... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Update TorchBench pinned commit (#117073)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/3b2ddb6f71027ff54c08d9042209a0e17d6c4c99\"},{\"author\":\"Lucas Kabela <lucaskabela@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"656885b\",\"date\":\"2025-07-18\",\"message\":\"[Dynamo][Better Engineering] Type devices, resume_execution and testing utils (#158593)\\n\\nAs part of better engineering week, we would like to improve out type support to improve dev experience in dynamo\\n\\nThis PR adds strict typing support to a set of utilities in dynamo, `device_interface.py`, `resume_execution.py`, `tensor_version_ops.py`, `test_case.py`, and `test_minifier_common.py`\\n\\nRunning\\n```\\nmypy torch/_dynamo/device_interface.py torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py torch/_dynamo/tensor_version_op.py torch/_dynamo/test_case.py torch/_dynamo/test_minifier_common.py  --linecount-report /tmp/... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Dynamo][Better Engineering] Type devices, resume_execution and testing utils (#158593)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/656885b6147e7e77db38de2898ef27f389e06461\"},{\"author\":\"Xiaoyi Liu <xiaoyiliu@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"52e76a3\",\"date\":\"2023-10-11\",\"message\":\"fix ShardedTensor.gather when shard is empty (#110962)\\n\\nSummary:\\ncurrent ShardedTensor.gather is not working as expectation when the shard is empty on any rank\\n\\nThe root cause is identified that when a sharded tensor has no placement on a specific rank, the metadata doesn't include that rank's placement which introduces KeyError in :                 ```shard_offset = shard_placement[shard. Metadata][1]```\\n\\nIt's fixed by adding an empty tensor check.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nbefore change:\\n\\nafter change:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D50114085\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/110962... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"fix ShardedTensor.gather when shard is empty (#110962)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/52e76a305677dfaf26cd5d59bd1aa239375f833c\"},{\"author\":\"Chong Gu <chonggu@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"431846a\",\"date\":\"2025-08-23\",\"message\":\"[AMD] Fix AMD User Defined Kernel Autotune (#160671)\\n\\nSummary: AMD specific kwargs need to be removed from the guard, otherwise a keyerror will be raised when executing the kernel.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\n```\\nbuck2 run mode/opt-amd-gpu -m rocm641 -c fbcode.split-dwarf=true -c fbcode.use_link_groups=true -c fbcode.enable_gpu_sections=true //hpc/new/models/feed/benchmark:feed_lower_benchmark -- --load=manifold://ads_storage_fblearner/tree/user/facebook/fblearner/predictor/894698382/0/gpu_lowering/new_input8 --skip-eager --skip-flop-estimation --sync-mode=0 --lower-backend=AOT_INDUCTOR\\n```\\ncan succeed after ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[AMD] Fix AMD User Defined Kernel Autotune (#160671)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/431846a6323c6f1d02da49e311ac694324f386f4\"},{\"author\":\"drisspg <drisspguessous@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"98aa374\",\"date\":\"2023-08-31\",\"message\":\"Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)\\n\\n# Summary\\nAs a stop gap to supporting the FP8 Dtype within inductor we would like to fallback to eager. Currently there are 3 ops that are needed for this:\\n`_scaled_mm` ( matmul for fp8 types)\\n`clone` (for creating new copies of fp8 tensors)\\n`to` ( for converting to and from fp8 types).\\n\\nThis PR registers a fallback for _scaled_mm. And adds fp8 to trigger `unsupported_input_tensor`\\n\\nPrior to these changes this was failing with:\\n``` Shell\\n  File \\\"/home/drisspg/meta/pytorch/torch/_inductor/codegen/triton_utils.py\\\", line 11, in signature_of\\n... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/98aa3745c258827cde8d081d0713ba2cd67c864e\"},{\"author\":\"lyuwenyu <wenyu.lyu@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"1b1cdd9\",\"date\":\"2018-11-16\",\"message\":\"Keep `ModuleList` consistent with python `list` in `__setitem__` function. (#13102)\\n\\nSummary:\\n`ModuleList` class function `__setitem__` has implicit rist\\n```\\nIn [26]: mlist = nn.ModuleList([nn.ReLU(), nn.Conv2d(10, 10, 3, 1)])\\n\\nIn [27]: mlist\\nOut[27]:\\nModuleList(\\n  (0): ReLU()\\n  (1): Conv2d(10, 10, kernel_size=(3, 3), stride=(1, 1))\\n)\\n\\nIn [28]: mlist[-1] = nn.ReLU()\\n\\nIn [29]: mlist\\nOut[29]:\\nModuleList(\\n  (0): ReLU()\\n  (1): Conv2d(10, 10, kernel_size=(3, 3), stride=(1, 1))\\n  (-1): ReLU()\\n)\\n\\nIn [30]: mlist[-1]\\n---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nKeyError  ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Keep `ModuleList` consistent with python `list` in `__setitem__` function. (#13102)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1b1cdd944c73a45c483afc6fc5c8ee2bdc9fa271\"},{\"author\":\"henrylhtsang <henrylhtsang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"7412b33\",\"date\":\"2025-05-13\",\"message\":\"[inductor] Use get to avoid possible keyerror at the end of precompilation (#153417)\\n\\nShameful admission: I have encountered this error 1-2 times, but don't have a repro.\\n\\ntorch/_inductor/select_algorithm.py\\\", line 2022, in wait_on_futures\\n    elapsed_times[future],\\n    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^\\ntorch._inductor.exc.InductorError: KeyError: <Future at 0x7fc4e394fb90 state=finished returned tuple>\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153417\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/ColinPeppler\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[inductor] Use get to avoid possible keyerror at the end of precompilation (#153417)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7412b33e91a70327478f59c0aa3b7737bedac42d\"},{\"author\":\"Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"a89cf2b\",\"date\":\"2024-10-14\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Don't codegen temporary cells for pre-existing cells (#137907)\\n\\nThis patch removes tempvar codegen for the `NewCellVariable` that has\\n`AttributeMutationExisting`, because these tempvar will never get used.\\nNote that tempvar codegen for other objects also follow this pattern,\\ni.e., it only fires on `AttributeMutationNew`.\\n\\nTo visualize, in the following program, we'll see the modified bytecode\\ncontains redundant `make_cell` calls, and stores the result to a local\\n`tmp_2` which is never used again.\\n\\n```python\\nimport torch\\n\\ndef test_write_cell():\\n    count = torch.ones(1)\\n    def inc():\\n... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Don't codegen temporary cells for pre-existing cells (#137907)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a89cf2b59a31e94942fc56764b558e86ecc575fc\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"c0b87af\",\"date\":\"2024-06-11\",\"message\":\"[RELAND2][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\\n\\nTracing through `__init__`  is important because it initializes (calls STORE_ATTR) on members. By doing that, we kick in the mutation tracking for these objects. So, things like mutating `_modules` etc is tracked automatically.\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/111837\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/126578\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[RELAND2][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/c0b87afcade3fc93d73bcbe4b61d732565019340\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"b2d6023\",\"date\":\"2024-06-10\",\"message\":\"[RELAND][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\\n\\nTracing through `__init__`  is important because it initializes (calls STORE_ATTR) on members. By doing that, we kick in the mutation tracking for these objects. So, things like mutating `_modules` etc is tracked automatically.\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/111837\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/126578\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\\nghstack dependencies: #128295\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[RELAND][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b2d602306a9eb19e30328cbaee941c874f8148a9\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"5b671ce\",\"date\":\"2024-01-23\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] fix typo in 3.11 resume_execution.py (#118108)\\n\\nwhoopsie\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/118108\\nApproved by: https://github.com/angelayi, https://github.com/zou3519\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] fix typo in 3.11 resume_execution.py (#118108)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5b671ce48650c1769e1daa92a7462ff2b592eab4\"},{\"author\":\"Scott Todd <scott.todd0@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"bfc873d\",\"date\":\"2025-08-12\",\"message\":\"[ROCm][Windows] Revert copying hipblaslt and rocblas dirs. (#159083)\\n\\nThis reverts the changes from https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/commit/b367e5f6a6c5853d0206bfd43d8b4a7cb76704f1. This will also close https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/158922.\\n\\nSince https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/commit/30387ab2e485384ab2e67084a1e2c5569190ba92, ROCm is bootstrapped using the 'rocm' Python module which contains these files (see https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/blob/main/docs/packaging/python_packaging.md), so they do not need to be bundled into torch/lib.\\n\\nThere was also a bug in here - if `ROCM_DI... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[ROCm][Windows] Revert copying hipblaslt and rocblas dirs. (#159083)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/bfc873d02ec413344717493e4175a902921359fd\"},{\"author\":\"Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"0530254\",\"date\":\"2025-05-16\",\"message\":\"[Set] Raise KeyError on empty `set.pop()` (#152907)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152907\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #150792, #152987, #152988, #152904, #152901, #152902, #152903, #152905, #152906, #152989\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Set] Raise KeyError on empty `set.pop()` (#152907)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/053025494f3afdd19c1f9278b256872b96154704\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"9647a25\",\"date\":\"2023-07-11\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Dataclass variables with default field (#104840)\\n\\nThe main complexity comes from the __init__ function of Dataclass variables which look something like this\\n\\n```\\n[2023-07-10 05:01:29,548] torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert: [DEBUG] INLINING <code object __init__ at 0x7f7015154450, file \\\"<string>\\\", line 2>\\n  3           0 LOAD_FAST                1 (b)\\n              2 LOAD_FAST                0 (self)\\n              4 STORE_ATTR               0 (b)\\n\\n  4           6 LOAD_FAST                2 (named_tensors)\\n              8 LOAD_DEREF               0 (_HAS_DEFAULT_FACTORY)\\n             10 IS... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Dataclass variables with default field (#104840)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/9647a251cb760bffb08a7f5453e92220d0649629\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"88c8c2b\",\"date\":\"2023-04-17\",\"message\":\"[dynamo 3.11] implement 3.11 exceptiontable (#96511)\\n\\nSummary of changes:\\n- Add CPython exceptiontable parsing/assembling functions in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py, based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt.\\n- Add optional `exn_tab_entry` field to dynamo `Instruction`s in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py in order to virtualize exception table entries (start, end, target instructions).\\n- Add checks guarding against duplicate instructions in dynamo, so that jump/exceptiontable targets are unambiguous. See `get_indexof` in torch/... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo 3.11] implement 3.11 exceptiontable (#96511)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/88c8c2b71b7b2dac0b346dfadc15e96eacaa7755\"},{\"author\":\"Soumith Chintala <soumith@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"157310c\",\"date\":\"2022-10-18\",\"message\":\"[inductor][triton] if device is a torch.device, then make cuda_properties index it correctly (#87174)\\n\\nWithout this, I was running into obvious `KeyError`s that were assuming that the device was an integer when running `examples/imagenet`.\\n\\n```python\\n(pytorch) soumith@bluebox:~/code/examples/imagenet$ python main.py --gpu 0 /home/soumith/dataset/imagenet\\n/home/soumith/code/vision/torchvision/io/image.py:13: UserWarning: Failed to load image Python extension:\\n  warn(f\\\"Failed to load image Python extension: {e}\\\")\\n/home/soumith/code/examples/imagenet/main.py:100: UserWarning: You have chosen a sp... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[inductor][triton] if device is a torch.device, then make cuda_properties index it correctly (#87174)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/157310c85ddcf1047377adecd1b905994436d613\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"6c4ce43\",\"date\":\"2024-08-01\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][exception] Raise Observed KeyError exception for dict __getitem__ (#132425)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/132425\\nApproved by: https://github.com/yanboliang, https://github.com/Skylion007\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][exception] Raise Observed KeyError exception for dict __getitem__ (#132425)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6c4ce4331ca4df046a5ce240cf3d0b30a4628d92\"},{\"author\":\"Yanbo Liang <ybliang8@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"186192b\",\"date\":\"2022-11-21\",\"message\":\"[Dynamo] Fix bugs when calling tensor.data and tensor.layout (#89257)\\n\\nFix bugs in [7k github models](https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1884).\\n* Legacy code still use ```tensor.data```, I think we can use ```tensor.detach``` to rewrite, not sure if there is anything I didn't anticipate.\\n* Support ```tensor.layout```.\\n\\nThe root cause of these issues are: dynamo wraps unimplemented ```tensor.x``` call into ```GetAttrVariable(TensorVariable, x)```, but this op was not inserted into FX graph. Hence, during the fake tensor propagation, it throws ```KeyError: 'example_value` ```.\\n\\nFor th... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Dynamo] Fix bugs when calling tensor.data and tensor.layout (#89257)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/186192bb26a71ec9b0131a6c49fdf19e76d208d7\"},{\"author\":\"Kunal Bhalla <kunalb@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"b00a4b7\",\"date\":\"2022-09-09\",\"message\":\"[torch.fx.wrap] Use callable / function.__name__ instead of function.__code__.co_name (#84373)\\n\\nRan across this issue while using torch.fx.wrap on a decorated function: it triggered a KeyError: 'wrapper_inside_decorator'. torch.fx.wrap stores function.__code__.co_name, but that isn't set correctly (and doesn't match it's name in the global namespace) for decorators; function.__name__ is set correctly.\\n\\nAlso adjusted to checking for callable instead of checking for the existing of __code__ to allow for a broader variety of functions that can be passed in. Eg. using functools.cache returns a cal... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[torch.fx.wrap] Use callable / function.__name__ instead of function.__code__.co_name (#84373)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b00a4b7cf1655cf9a6cf78ef9536b63898cbfb08\"},{\"author\":\"Michael Suo <suo@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"179dbd4\",\"date\":\"2020-06-30\",\"message\":\"[jit] preserve keys on dictionary input tracing (#40792)\\n\\nSummary:\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/40792\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/40529.\\n\\nOne followup should be to produce a better error message when a new\\ndictionary has different keys than the traced input. Right now it\\npresents as a fairly opaque `KeyError`.\\n\\nTest Plan: Imported from OSS\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D22311731\\n\\nPulled By: suo\\n\\nfbshipit-source-id: c9fbe0b54cf69daed2f11a191d988568521a3932\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[jit] preserve keys on dictionary input tracing (#40792)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/179dbd4f25288a098099e806b16098468e6f0b2f\"},{\"author\":\"Kefei Lu <kefeilu@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"a95c94f\",\"date\":\"2021-10-30\",\"message\":\"[fx2trt] fix acc_tracer when run against module that contains ScriptModule submodules (#67567)\\n\\nSummary:\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/67567\\n\\n- Fix an issue to allow it to work against modules that contains ScriptModule submodules.\\n- Fix a bug where `getattr(base_class, method_name)` could raise KeyError\\n\\nTest Plan: linter; CI;\\n\\nReviewed By: 842974287\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D31956070\\n\\nfbshipit-source-id: <tarasg@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"f11f9a8\",\"date\":\"2020-10-05\",\"message\":\"[pytorch][improvement] Improve torch logging to identify problematic key (#45766)\\n\\nSummary:\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/45766\\n\\nAs per subj, making KeyError message more verbose.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nVerified that breakage can be successfully investigated with verbose error message\\nunit tests\\n\\nReviewed By: esqu1\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D24080362\\n\\nfbshipit-source-id: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152903\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #150792, #152987, #152988, #152904, #152901, <jbschlosser@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"07b618e\",\"date\":\"2024-03-26\",\"message\":\"Graph break cleanly in Dynamo for module parametrization (#121041)\\n\\nFixes #118795\\n\\nThis is a graph breaking partial fix for #120914. We still need -actual- module parametrization tracing support, but at least it doesn't blow up hard now.\\n\\n**Background**: Module parametrization injects a property as the module parameter attribute that calls a `nn.Module` whose forward takes in a module parameter and returns a reparametrized module parameter.\\nExample:\\n```\\nclass MyParametrization(nn.Module):\\n    def forward(X):\\n        # This reparametrization just negates the original parameter value\\n        ret... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Graph break cleanly in Dynamo for module parametrization (#121041)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/07b618e2d4a46f2ecd57131f34d92d47f109d220\"},{\"author\":\"Menglu Yu <mengluy@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"4f0f25b\",\"date\":\"2024-02-20\",\"message\":\"[Inductor][bugFix] fix a bug in merge_splits (#119956)\\n\\nSummary: RecGPT got a keyerror when running the split_cat, and it was caused by a corner case hit.\\n\\nTest Plan: P1184947021\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D53791839\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/119956\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jackiexu1992\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Inductor][bugFix] fix a bug in merge_splits (#119956)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/4f0f25b7ce5e5b39fc2999933d210948dae0460e\"},{\"author\":\"Edward Z. Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"66f67d9\",\"date\":\"2023-09-08\",\"message\":\"Print restart attempt as part of Dynamo log context (#108864)\\n\\nNow looks like:\\n\\n```\\n[2023-09-08 06:04:48,532] [0/0] torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert: [DEBUG] TRACE STORE_ATTR foo [ConstantVariable(int), NNModule\\nVariable()]\\n[2023-09-08 06:04:48,532] [0/0] torch._dynamo.convert_frame: [INFO]\\nRestarting analysis due to _dynamo/variables/nn_module.py\\n:138 in convert_to_unspecialized\\n[2023-09-08 06:04:48,533] [0/0_1] torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert: [INFO] Step 1: torchdynamo start tracing f /data/users/ezyang/c/pytorch/a.py:6\\n[2023-09-08 06:04:48,533] [0/0_1] torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert.__trace_s... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Print restart attempt as part of Dynamo log context (#108864)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/66f67d9a256ea3e8240caa96559ef6ae5f99631a\"},{\"author\":\"Kevin Tse <ktse@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"14b660f\",\"date\":\"2022-08-02\",\"message\":\"[DataPipe] Correct the type of exception that is being raised by ShufflerMapDataPipe (#82666)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/data/issues/708\\n\\nThe following code snippet used to fail, now it has been added as a test case:\\n```python\\ndp1 = dp.map.SequenceWrapper(range(10))\\nshuffle_dp1 = dp1.shuffle()\\ndp2 = dp.map.SequenceWrapper(range(10))\\nshuffle_dp2 = dp2.shuffle()\\nzip_dp = shuffle_dp1.zip(shuffle_dp2)\\nlist(zip_dp)  # This used to fail\\n```\\n\\nThe issue was that `ShufflerMapDataPipe` raises a `KeyError` when an out of bound index is passed into it, but that was not handled by `zip_dp`'s `__geti... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[DataPipe] Correct the type of exception that is being raised by ShufflerMapDataPipe (#82666)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/14b660fcc0c32a7478c69a25a253b67d0ed36364\"},{\"author\":\"vfdev <vfdev.5@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"456b745\",\"date\":\"2025-06-27\",\"message\":\"Minor error message fix in device_mesh.py (#157096)\\n\\nFixed error message:\\nOn main:\\n```\\nKeyError: (\\\"Invalid mesh_dim_names ('dp_shard', 'dp_shard') specified. \\\", 'Found mesh dim indices to slice: [(1,), (1,)]. ', 'Mesh dim indices should be in ascending order.')\\n```\\nOn PR:\\n```\\nKeyError: Invalid mesh_dim_names ('dp_shard', 'dp_shard') specified. Found mesh dim indices to slice: [(1,), (1,)]. Mesh dim indices should be in ascending order.'\\n```\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157096\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Minor error message fix in device_mesh.py (#157096)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/456b7451c78096fd512c60e26cc618386a251bc7\"},{\"author\":\"Peter Bell <peterbell10@live.co.uk>\",\"commit\":\"2784b3f\",\"date\":\"2024-07-24\",\"message\":\"[inductor] Fix split-scan interaction with multi-kernel (#131044)\\n\\nThis fixes a couple errors that come up when multi-kernel is used with\\nsplit-scan.\\n1. The split-scan was being marked as a persistent kernel, which allowed\\n   a multi-kernel to be created but this isn't supported. Fix is to\\n   never mark split-scan as persistent.\\n2. Benchmark codegen was not handling WorkspaceArg, and would raise a\\n   KeyError during codegen.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/131044\\nApproved by: https://github.com/shunting314\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[inductor] Fix split-scan interaction with multi-kernel (#131044)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2784b3f1b7e574f0dfe1ad5f5a3624f0bb63a855\"},{\"author\":\"angelayi <yiangela7@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"01388d0\",\"date\":\"2024-01-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Slightly better error message if key not in dict (#117902)\\n\\nWas debugging an export issue, and currently when `key` does not exist in `self.items`, the error message is\\n```\\n  File \\\"/opt/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/variables/dicts.py\\\", line 208, in getitem_const\\n    return self.items[key]\\n           ~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^\\ntorch._dynamo.exc.InternalTorchDynamoError: <torch._dynamo.variables.dicts.ConstDictVariable._HashableTracker object at 0x7fd7697cbf90>\\n```\\nThis PR changes it to be the following.\\n```\\nFile \\\"/data/users/angelayi/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/variables/dicts.py\\\", line 199, in getitem_const\\n... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Slightly better error message if key not in dict (#117902)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/01388d07900ff9adea4de42cbd07ffca384d2dbb\"},{\"author\":\"XiaobingSuper <mergennachin@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"dde9aff\",\"date\":\"2022-11-04\",\"message\":\"Populate self.export in InstructionTranslatorBase (#88508)\\n\\nSummary:\\n\\nThis is a followup to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/88354/files#diff-622913fdb49db90d6f3a8ab225b4badb7996023e6498e9f7c6d03fe9f32d0986R836\\n\\nReference to self.export got added to InstructionTranslatorBase (i.e. STORE_ATTR) but self.export is populated only for InstructionTranslators.\\n\\nHere's an example failure\\n\\n```\\n   File \\\"/scratch/williamwen/work/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\\\", line 322, in step\\n    getattr(self, inst.opname)(inst)\\n  File \\\"/scratch/williamwen/work/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Populate self.export in InstructionTranslatorBase (#88508)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/dde9affeaafff957a6d5bf98e33e4119b14cd2d5\"},{\"author\":\"Kaiyu Shi <skyisno.1@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"444f792\",\"date\":\"2019-04-23\",\"message\":\"Fix lack of state init for adagrad and add share_memory flag (#17679)\\n\\nSummary:\\nThe current code initialize the `state` in `__init__` method, but the initialization process is not invoked in `add_parameter_group`.\\n\\nI followed the same approach in other Optimizers to init the `state`.\\n\\n```python\\nimport torch\\n\\nemb = torch.nn.Embedding(10,10)\\nemb2 = torch.nn.Embedding(10,10)\\n\\noptim = torch.optim.Adagrad(emb.parameters())\\nprint(optim.state[emb.weight])  # already initialized\\n\\noptim.add_param_group({'params': emb2.parameters()})\\nprint(optim.state[emb2.weight])  # empty dict\\n\\nloss = emb2.weight.sum(... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fix lack of state init for adagrad and add share_memory flag (#17679)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/444f792fa66efe485a77d1da724156eeab39c211\"},{\"author\":\"Zhang, Jianyi <jianyi.zhang@intel.com>\",\"commit\":\"7f52875\",\"date\":\"2025-04-17\",\"message\":\"[Inductor] fix torch._inductor.exc.InductorError: KeyError (#151424)\\n\\nFixes #151423, which is a regression after #150845\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/151424\\nApproved by: https://github.com/eellison\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Inductor] fix torch._inductor.exc.InductorError: KeyError (#151424)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7f528751ccb42428aace70c5c35ec7e47eb843d2\"},{\"author\":\"Eric Hanson <5846501+ericphanson@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"33dee72\",\"date\":\"2024-12-14\",\"message\":\"Reraise worker errors as runtime errors in more cases when the original exception can't be constructed (#140911)\\n\\nrelated to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/34130\\n\\nwhen pytorch attempts to re-raise an exception from a worker process (e.g. multiprocessing dataloader), if it can't reconstruct the original exception message due to a type error, it instead raises it as a runtime error. However, if it can't reconstruct the exception for some other reason, it throws an error with a stacktrace pointing to the `ExceptionWrapper` code rather than the original underlying issue.\\n\\nOne case in wh... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Reraise worker errors as runtime errors in more cases when the original exception can't be constructed (#140911)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/33dee721ae0e97ac3a0cf704e73a9de8b8128890\"},{\"author\":\"Wu, Chunyuan <chunyuan.wu@intel.com>\",\"commit\":\"1fb498d\",\"date\":\"2024-08-04\",\"message\":\"Add try except for _maybe_evaluate_static call in IndexPropagation (#132128)\\n\\nFixes the Inductor max-autotune mode failures of the below models:\\n- GPT2ForSequenceClassification\\n- PegasusForConditionalGeneration\\n- XGLMForCausalLM\\n- hf_GPT2\\n- tnt_s_patch16_224\\n```log\\n  File \\\"/pytorch/torch/_inductor/index_propagation.py\\\", line 329, in statically_true\\n    evaluated = self.shape_env._maybe_evaluate_static(\\n  File \\\"/pytorch/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py\\\", line 1499, in wrapper\\n    return fn_cache(self, *args, **kwargs)\\n  File \\\"/pytorch/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py\\\", line 4539... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Add try except for _maybe_evaluate_static call in IndexPropagation (#132128)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1fb498d6e34e0e9b43b2c26dc0a18a4fc3a52605\"},{\"author\":\"Mergen Nachin <mergennachin@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"2c46d57\",\"date\":\"2022-11-02\",\"message\":\"Disallow module attribute mutation (#88354)\\n\\nSummary:\\n\\nSee https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1475\\n\\nNot allowing any new mutations happen inside forward() function during\\nexport.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\n\\nRun `python test/dynamo/test_export.py` and make sure it passes\\n\\nAdded new unit tests (3 positive tests and 4 negative tests)\\n\\nHere's what the actual error looks like\\n\\n```\\n  File \\\"/home/mnachin/local/miniconda3/envs/pytorch/lib/python3.9/site-packages/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\\\", line 322, in step\\n    getattr(self, inst.opname)(inst)\\n  File \\\"/home/mnachin/local/miniconda3/envs/pytorch/li... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Disallow module attribute mutation (#88354)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2c46d5725e3b89d8f83ed2ba940225fa57a7156f\"},{\"author\":\"rraminen <rraminen@amd.com>\",\"commit\":\"8c185e6\",\"date\":\"2021-02-17\",\"message\":\"torchvision hipify revamp fix (#51453)\\n\\nSummary:\\nThe torchvision build error from hipify revamp, \\\"KeyError: '/usr/include/libpng16/png.h'\\\" is fixed in this PR\\n\\nDescription:\\n\\nTraceback (most recent call last):\\n  File \\\"setup.py\\\", line 471, in <module>\\n    ext_modules=get_extensions(),\\n  File \\\"setup.py\\\", line 329, in get_extensions\\n    extra_compile_args=extra_compile_args\\n  File \\\"/opt/conda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torch/utils/cpp_extension.py\\\", line 892, in CUDAExtension\\n    is_pytorch_extension=True,\\n  File \\\"/opt/conda/lib/python3.6/site-packages/torch/utils/hipify/hipify_python.py\\\", line 9... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"torchvision hipify revamp fix (#51453)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8c185e62f99c272bf991b8e0558fccb5f8c780c0\"},{\"author\":\"D\u00e1vid Komorowicz <dawars00@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"b5a5e29\",\"date\":\"2019-05-24\",\"message\":\"Support 3D mesh/point cloud (#20413)\\n\\nSummary:\\nI started adding support for the new **[mesh/point cloud](https://github.com/tensorflow/graphics/blob/master/tensorflow_graphics/g3doc/tensorboard.md)** data type introduced to TensorBoard recently.\\n\\nI created the functions to add the data, created the appropriate summaries.\\nThis new data type however requires a **Merged** summary containing the data for the vertices, colors and faces.\\n\\nI got stuck at this stage. Maybe someone can help. lanpa?\\n\\nI converted the example code by Google to PyTorch:\\n```python\\nimport numpy as np\\nimport trimesh\\n\\nimport t... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Support 3D mesh/point cloud (#20413)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b5a5e296aa2f3fad6b2f99f87644237837bf3c82\"},{\"author\":\"Stas Bekman <stas@stason.org>\",\"commit\":\"c0a2452\",\"date\":\"2019-03-29\",\"message\":\"multiline KeyError msg python bug workaround (#18557)\\n\\nSummary:\\nmake multiline KeyError msg readable by working around a python bug https://bugs.python.org/issue2651\\n\\ndiscussion: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/16647\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/18557\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D14681086\\n\\nPulled By: soumith\\n\\nfbshipit-source-id: acbd13a823302c854c3d364028ed414fd8ce6bc8\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"multiline KeyError msg python bug workaround (#18557)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/c0a2452ffe1bf4202763bbfcafa5867461158de3\"},{\"author\":\"Yichen Yan <wenji.yyc@alibaba-inc.com>\",\"commit\":\"5dad6a5\",\"date\":\"2024-08-31\",\"message\":\"[ONNX][DORT] Lazy-import `onnxruntime` (#134662)\\n\\nCurrently, if installed, `onnxruntime` will be imported when importing `torch._inductor` (which will be imported by some other library, e.g. transformer-engine):\\n\\n```\\n  /mnt/c.py(53)<module>()\\n-> from torch._inductor.utils import maybe_profile\\n  /usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/torch/_inductor/utils.py(49)<module>()\\n-> import torch._export\\n  /usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/torch/_export/__init__.py(25)<module>()\\n-> import torch._dynamo\\n  /usr/local/lib/python3.10/site-packages/torch/_dynamo/__init__.py(2)<module>()\\n-> from . impo... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[ONNX][DORT] Lazy-import `onnxruntime` (#134662)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5dad6a5a849cdd3ce322069866086eda5440ca9e\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"7ede78f\",\"date\":\"2024-06-06\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\\n\\nTracing through `__init__`  is important because it initializes (calls STORE_ATTR) on members. By doing that, we kick in the mutation tracking for these objects. So, things like mutating `_modules` etc is tracked automatically.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/126578\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\\nghstack dependencies: #128001\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7ede78f9f5d7e6c993faa1a70a5f0b0eaec5640d\"},{\"author\":\"ydwu4 <ydwu2014@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"33c94b8\",\"date\":\"2023-09-13\",\"message\":\"Better error handling for cond (#108817)\\n\\n## Exception in cond:\\nFor code below:\\n```python\\nimport torch\\nimport functorch.experimental.control_flow as control_flow\\ndef true_fn(x):\\n    return x.sin()\\n\\ndef false_fn(x):\\n    return x, x\\n\\ndef f(x, y):\\n    return control_flow.cond(y, true_fn, false_fn, [x])\\n\\nf(torch.ones(3, 4), torch.tensor(False))\\n```\\nThe original exception stack trace is:\\n```python\\nTraceback (most recent call last):\\n  File \\\"/home/yidi/local/pytorch/test_exc.py\\\", line 33, in <module>\\n    f(torch.ones(3, 4), torch.tensor(False))\\n  File \\\"/home/yidi/local/pytorch/test_exc.py\\\", line 31, ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Better error handling for cond (#108817)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/33c94b8b1680d875ba6bd0d379d8e8540caad943\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"998c077\",\"date\":\"2023-06-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] fix deep nested closure cell KeyError (#104222)\\n\\nFix https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99639 by handling the case in `InliningInstructionTranslator`'s `LOAD_CLOSURE` definition when the requested cell is not in `self.closure_cells`.\\n\\nMy intuition is that the behavior of `LOAD_DEREF` and `STORE_DEREF` on a cell/freevar should not depend on whether or not we called `LOAD_CLOSURE` (that is, we shouldn't create a new cell var in `LOAD_CLOSURE` like in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/101357). But we need a way to push cells created by the inlined function that were not pres... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] fix deep nested closure cell KeyError (#104222)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/998c07799f22a0bb63a39c84d15eae29778631f4\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:resume_execution OR message:keyerror OR message:STORE_ATTR) repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d count:50\",\"totalCount\":58}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "26a1b9c",
        "date": "2025-09-08",
        "message": "[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\n\nDifferential Revision: [D81938289](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81938289)\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162318\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914"
      },
      {
        "author": "jon-chuang <jon-chuang@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "d1d5d16",
        "date": "2023-02-14",
        "message": "dynamo: handle straight-line graph breaks for autocast context manager with constant args (#94137)\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/93890\n\nWe do the following:\n1. fix __init__constructor for `AutocastModeVariable` with exisiting `mode` while copying\n2. `resume_execution` is made aware of constant args (`target_values`), by storing said args in `ReenterWith`. To propagate between subgraphs (in straightline code), we also store the constant args in the downstream's `code_options[\"co_consts\"]` if not already.\n\n---\n\nFuture work:\n1. handle instantiating context manager in non-inline... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "dynamo: handle straight-line graph breaks for autocast context manager with constant args (#94137)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d1d5d16df3766bc6a6c9ebe1168ff652bcdcaa5e"
      },
      {
        "author": "Bin Bao <binbao@meta.com>",
        "commit": "7e9cbc6",
        "date": "2024-01-11",
        "message": "[CI] Catch more exception types when running eager in PT2 tests (#117120)\n\nSummary: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/7467073391/job/20320251143#step:16:1332 shows a case where model loading fails with KeyError but the error is not logged in the report csv file, which can cause an eager model failure silently ignored in the PT2 integration test.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/117120\nApproved by: https://github.com/huydhn",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[CI] Catch more exception types when running eager in PT2 tests (#117120)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7e9cbc683495104e96b12fa20a0190aa1d3b451f"
      },
      {
        "author": "drisspg <drisspguessous@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "5b6ba41",
        "date": "2023-08-31",
        "message": "Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)\n\n# Summary\nAs a stop gap to supporting the FP8 Dtype within inductor we would like to fallback to eager. Currently there are 3 ops that are needed for this:\n`_scaled_mm` ( matmul for fp8 types)\n`clone` (for creating new copies of fp8 tensors)\n`to` ( for converting to and from fp8 types).\n\nThis PR registers a fallback for _scaled_mm. And adds fp8 to trigger `unsupported_input_tensor`\n\nPrior to these changes this was failing with:\n``` Shell\n  File \"/home/drisspg/meta/pytorch/torch/_inductor/codegen/triton_utils.py\", line 11, in signature_of\n... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5b6ba4110be80934bc18461bc8f04d1cb2b6b148"
      },
      {
        "author": "Aaron Gokaslan <aaronGokaslan@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "d22c403",
        "date": "2025-05-10",
        "message": "[Ez][BE]: Fix KeyError LOGNAME (#153324)\n\nUnblocks #153020 which accidentally improves the CircularImportLinter to check all Python files. It doesn't set a logname so it errors, there is another FSDP script which already defaults LOGNAME to '' if not specified, this does the same.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153324\nApproved by: https://github.com/awgu",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[Ez][BE]: Fix KeyError LOGNAME (#153324)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d22c40373f97a1ca288bb39fcee3a4333ecd0681"
      },
      {
        "author": "Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>",
        "commit": "162eba2",
        "date": "2024-10-18",
        "message": "[dynamo] Remove `mutable_local.source` and index on `VariableTracker` rather than `MutableLocalBase` (#137905)\n\nThis patch addresses parts of the side-effect refactor proposed in #133027;\nspecifically, it does 3 things:\n\n1. Change `SideEffects.store_attr_mutations` and `PyCodegen.tempvars`\n   to index on `VariableTracker` rather than `MutableLocalBase`.\n2. Remove the `source` field from `MutableSideEffects` and\n   `AttributeMutation`, and use `VariableTracker.source` instead.\n3. Plumb a `overridden_sources: Dict[Source, Source]` from\n   `handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists` to `PyCodegen` so that ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Remove `mutable_local.source` and index on `VariableTracker` rather than `MutableLocalBase` (#137905)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/162eba2dee25575c2d8121959119d2e667741123"
      },

        "message": "[dynamo] Enable typechecking for resume_execution.py (#112564)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/112564\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/eellison\nghstack dependencies: #112561, #112562, #112563",

        "author": "Huy Do <huydhn@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "5315317",
        "date": "2023-04-20",
        "message": "Skip some detectron2_maskrcnn models with KeyError _ignore_torch_cuda_oom (#99599)\n\nThese tests are failing in trunk https://hud.pytorch.org/pytorch/pytorch/commit/233cc34d3b8a1b92eeeea78661463f8ec660fbcd with `KeyError: '_ignore_torch_cuda_oom'`\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/99599\nApproved by: https://github.com/malfet",

      },
      {
        "author": "Emily Shen <mleshen@fb.com>",
        "commit": "07da584",
        "date": "2021-05-18",
        "message": "Fix KeyError returned by _maybe_get_last_node_only_observer (#58443)\n\nSummary: Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/58443\n\nTest Plan: arc lint\n\nReviewed By: vkuzo\n\nDifferential Revision: D28494119\n\nfbshipit-source-id: 05abf4e12051afc237096812fb0ee08a8b9447f9",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Fix KeyError returned by _maybe_get_last_node_only_observer (#58443)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/07da584dbd2fa89a0967892a2e977f62394f92c5"
      },
      {
        "author": "titaiwangms <titaiwang@microsoft.com>",
        "commit": "b23f11c",
        "date": "2024-12-18",
        "message": "[ONNX] Automatically convert dynamic_axes to dynamic_shapes with torch.export.Dim.AUTO (#143158)\n\nWith https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/133620 introducing Dim.AUTO, we can now automatically convert dynamic_axes to dynamic_shapes without specifying min and max. However, exporting still could be crashed when there are same specs shared between inputs and there is no guarantee that the axes will be dynamic (see PR description).\n\n~~Therefore, a~~ follow-up PR should create a post-processing ONNX side pass to ~~enable the missed dynamic axes~~ rename the dynamic shapes (s0,  s1, ...) to dyna... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[ONNX] Automatically convert dynamic_axes to dynamic_shapes with torch.export.Dim.AUTO (#143158)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b23f11c529c18638cd13749613a9483ebc33ed9c"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "ae80bce",
        "date": "2024-09-24",
        "message": "[dynamo] refactor resume_execution.py to use bytecode templates (#136483)\n\nUse bytecode from template instead of hardcoding bytecode in resume_execution.py. Gets rid of a lot of Python-version dependent bytecode generation. Also makes resume_execution.py easier to support in future Python version updates.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/136483\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] refactor resume_execution.py to use bytecode templates (#136483)",

        "message": "Handle KeyError for compiler collective in scalars too (#135385)\n\nSigned-off-by: Edward Z. Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/135385\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel",

        "message": "Update TorchBench pinned commit (#117073)\n\n~~To match their recent v4.36.2 release https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/commits/v4.36.2.  This is to fix the KeyError showing on release branch https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/7451512288/job/20279117324#step:16:1336.  I think this can be updated in main too because the current pinned commit is already 4-month old.~~\n\nCheck with @desertfire, trying to update TorchBench pinned commit instead.\n\nThe test is also failing in main https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/7467073391/job/20320251143#step:16:1120, but for some...       {
        "author": "Lucas Kabela <lucaskabela@meta.com>",
        "commit": "656885b",
        "date": "2025-07-18",
        "message": "[Dynamo][Better Engineering] Type devices, resume_execution and testing utils (#158593)\n\nAs part of better engineering week, we would like to improve out type support to improve dev experience in dynamo\n\nThis PR adds strict typing support to a set of utilities in dynamo, `device_interface.py`, `resume_execution.py`, `tensor_version_ops.py`, `test_case.py`, and `test_minifier_common.py`\n\nRunning\n```\nmypy torch/_dynamo/device_interface.py torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py torch/_dynamo/tensor_version_op.py torch/_dynamo/test_case.py torch/_dynamo/test_minifier_common.py  --linecount-report /tmp/... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[Dynamo][Better Engineering] Type devices, resume_execution and testing utils (#158593)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/656885b6147e7e77db38de2898ef27f389e06461"
      },
      {
        "author": "Xiaoyi Liu <xiaoyiliu@meta.com>",
        "commit": "52e76a3",
        "date": "2023-10-11",
        "message": "fix ShardedTensor.gather when shard is empty (#110962)\n\nSummary:\ncurrent ShardedTensor.gather is not working as expectation when the shard is empty on any rank\n\nThe root cause is identified that when a sharded tensor has no placement on a specific rank, the metadata doesn't include that rank's placement which introduces KeyError in :                 ```shard_offset = shard_placement[shard. Metadata][1]```\n\nIt's fixed by adding an empty tensor check.\n\nTest Plan:\nbefore change:\n\nafter change:\n\nDifferential Revision: D50114085\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/110962... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "fix ShardedTensor.gather when shard is empty (#110962)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/52e76a305677dfaf26cd5d59bd1aa239375f833c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Chong Gu <chonggu@meta.com>",
        "commit": "431846a",
        "date": "2025-08-23",
        "message": "[AMD] Fix AMD User Defined Kernel Autotune (#160671)\n\nSummary: AMD specific kwargs need to be removed from the guard, otherwise a keyerror will be raised when executing the kernel.\n\nTest Plan:\n```\nbuck2 run mode/opt-amd-gpu -m rocm641 -c fbcode.split-dwarf=true -c fbcode.use_link_groups=true -c fbcode.enable_gpu_sections=true //hpc/new/models/feed/benchmark:feed_lower_benchmark -- --load=manifold://ads_storage_fblearner/tree/user/facebook/fblearner/predictor/894698382/0/gpu_lowering/new_input8 --skip-eager --skip-flop-estimation --sync-mode=0 --lower-backend=AOT_INDUCTOR\n```\ncan succeed after ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[AMD] Fix AMD User Defined Kernel Autotune (#160671)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/431846a6323c6f1d02da49e311ac694324f386f4"
      },
      {
        "author": "drisspg <drisspguessous@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "98aa374",
        "date": "2023-08-31",
        "message": "Fallback to eager for float8 ops in inductor (#108293)\n\n# Summary\nAs a stop gap to supporting the FP8 Dtype within inductor we would like to fallback to eager. Currently there are 3 ops that are needed for this:\n`_scaled_mm` ( matmul for fp8 types)\n`clone` (for creating new copies of fp8 tensors)\n`to` ( for converting to and from fp8 types).\n\nThis PR registers a fallback for _scaled_mm. And adds fp8 to trigger `unsupported_input_tensor`\n\nPrior to these changes this was failing with:\n``` Shell\n  File \"/home/drisspg/meta/pytorch/torch/_inductor/codegen/triton_utils.py\", line 11, in signature_of\n... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

      {
        "author": "lyuwenyu <wenyu.lyu@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "1b1cdd9",
        "date": "2018-11-16",
        "message": "Keep `ModuleList` consistent with python `list` in `__setitem__` function. (#13102)\n\nSummary:\n`ModuleList` class function `__setitem__` has implicit rist\n```\nIn [26]: mlist = nn.ModuleList([nn.ReLU(), nn.Conv2d(10, 10, 3, 1)])\n\nIn [27]: mlist\nOut[27]:\nModuleList(\n  (0): ReLU()\n  (1): Conv2d(10, 10, kernel_size=(3, 3), stride=(1, 1))\n)\n\nIn [28]: mlist[-1] = nn.ReLU()\n\nIn [29]: mlist\nOut[29]:\nModuleList(\n  (0): ReLU()\n  (1): Conv2d(10, 10, kernel_size=(3, 3), stride=(1, 1))\n  (-1): ReLU()\n)\n\nIn [30]: mlist[-1]\n---------------------------------------------------------------------------\nKeyError  ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Keep `ModuleList` consistent with python `list` in `__setitem__` function. (#13102)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1b1cdd944c73a45c483afc6fc5c8ee2bdc9fa271"
      },
      {
        "author": "henrylhtsang <henrylhtsang@meta.com>",
        "commit": "7412b33",
        "date": "2025-05-13",
        "message": "[inductor] Use get to avoid possible keyerror at the end of precompilation (#153417)\n\nShameful admission: I have encountered this error 1-2 times, but don't have a repro.\n\ntorch/_inductor/select_algorithm.py\", line 2022, in wait_on_futures\n    elapsed_times[future],\n    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^\ntorch._inductor.exc.InductorError: KeyError: <Future at 0x7fc4e394fb90 state=finished returned tuple>\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153417\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/ColinPeppler",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[inductor] Use get to avoid possible keyerror at the end of precompilation (#153417)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7412b33e91a70327478f59c0aa3b7737bedac42d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>",
        "commit": "a89cf2b",
        "date": "2024-10-14",
        "message": "[dynamo] Don't codegen temporary cells for pre-existing cells (#137907)\n\nThis patch removes tempvar codegen for the `NewCellVariable` that has\n`AttributeMutationExisting`, because these tempvar will never get used.\nNote that tempvar codegen for other objects also follow this pattern,\ni.e., it only fires on `AttributeMutationNew`.\n\nTo visualize, in the following program, we'll see the modified bytecode\ncontains redundant `make_cell` calls, and stores the result to a local\n`tmp_2` which is never used again.\n\n```python\nimport torch\n\ndef test_write_cell():\n    count = torch.ones(1)\n    def inc():\n... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Don't codegen temporary cells for pre-existing cells (#137907)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a89cf2b59a31e94942fc56764b558e86ecc575fc"
      },
      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "c0b87af",
        "date": "2024-06-11",
        "message": "[RELAND2][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\n\nTracing through `__init__`  is important because it initializes (calls STORE_ATTR) on members. By doing that, we kick in the mutation tracking for these objects. So, things like mutating `_modules` etc is tracked automatically.\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/111837\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/126578\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[RELAND2][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/c0b87afcade3fc93d73bcbe4b61d732565019340"
      },
      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "b2d6023",
        "date": "2024-06-10",
        "message": "[RELAND][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\n\nTracing through `__init__`  is important because it initializes (calls STORE_ATTR) on members. By doing that, we kick in the mutation tracking for these objects. So, things like mutating `_modules` etc is tracked automatically.\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/111837\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/126578\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\nghstack dependencies: #128295",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[RELAND][dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b2d602306a9eb19e30328cbaee941c874f8148a9"
      },
      {

        "commit": "5b671ce",

        "message": "[dynamo] fix typo in 3.11 resume_execution.py (#118108)\n\nwhoopsie\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/118108\nApproved by: https://github.com/angelayi, https://github.com/zou3519",

        "author": "Scott Todd <scott.todd0@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "bfc873d",
        "date": "2025-08-12",
        "message": "[ROCm][Windows] Revert copying hipblaslt and rocblas dirs. (#159083)\n\nThis reverts the changes from https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/commit/b367e5f6a6c5853d0206bfd43d8b4a7cb76704f1. This will also close https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/158922.\n\nSince https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/commit/30387ab2e485384ab2e67084a1e2c5569190ba92, ROCm is bootstrapped using the 'rocm' Python module which contains these files (see https://github.com/ROCm/TheRock/blob/main/docs/packaging/python_packaging.md), so they do not need to be bundled into torch/lib.\n\nThere was also a bug in here - if `ROCM_DI... [truncated]",

        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "9647a25",
        "date": "2023-07-11",
        "message": "[dynamo] Dataclass variables with default field (#104840)\n\nThe main complexity comes from the __init__ function of Dataclass variables which look something like this\n\n```\n[2023-07-10 05:01:29,548] torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert: [DEBUG] INLINING <code object __init__ at 0x7f7015154450, file \"<string>\", line 2>\n  3           0 LOAD_FAST                1 (b)\n              2 LOAD_FAST                0 (self)\n              4 STORE_ATTR               0 (b)\n\n  4           6 LOAD_FAST                2 (named_tensors)\n              8 LOAD_DEREF               0 (_HAS_DEFAULT_FACTORY)\n             10 IS... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Dataclass variables with default field (#104840)",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@fb.com>",
        "commit": "88c8c2b",
        "date": "2023-04-17",
        "message": "[dynamo 3.11] implement 3.11 exceptiontable (#96511)\n\nSummary of changes:\n- Add CPython exceptiontable parsing/assembling functions in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py, based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt.\n- Add optional `exn_tab_entry` field to dynamo `Instruction`s in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py in order to virtualize exception table entries (start, end, target instructions).\n- Add checks guarding against duplicate instructions in dynamo, so that jump/exceptiontable targets are unambiguous. See `get_indexof` in torch/... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo 3.11] implement 3.11 exceptiontable (#96511)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/88c8c2b71b7b2dac0b346dfadc15e96eacaa7755"
      },

        "author": "Soumith Chintala <soumith@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "157310c",
        "date": "2022-10-18",
        "message": "[inductor][triton] if device is a torch.device, then make cuda_properties index it correctly (#87174)\n\nWithout this, I was running into obvious `KeyError`s that were assuming that the device was an integer when running `examples/imagenet`.\n\n```python\n(pytorch) soumith@bluebox:~/code/examples/imagenet$ python main.py --gpu 0 /home/soumith/dataset/imagenet\n/home/soumith/code/vision/torchvision/io/image.py:13: UserWarning: Failed to load image Python extension:\n  warn(f\"Failed to load image Python extension: {e}\")\n/home/soumith/code/examples/imagenet/main.py:100: UserWarning: You have chosen a sp... [truncated]",

        "commit": "6c4ce43",

        "message": "[dynamo][exception] Raise Observed KeyError exception for dict __getitem__ (#132425)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/132425\nApproved by: https://github.com/yanboliang, https://github.com/Skylion007",
