      {
        "author": "Yanbo Liang <ybliang8@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "186192b",
        "date": "2022-11-21",
        "message": "[Dynamo] Fix bugs when calling tensor.data and tensor.layout (#89257)\n\nFix bugs in [7k github models](https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1884).\n* Legacy code still use ```tensor.data```, I think we can use ```tensor.detach``` to rewrite, not sure if there is anything I didn't anticipate.\n* Support ```tensor.layout```.\n\nThe root cause of these issues are: dynamo wraps unimplemented ```tensor.x``` call into ```GetAttrVariable(TensorVariable, x)```, but this op was not inserted into FX graph. Hence, during the fake tensor propagation, it throws ```KeyError: 'example_value` ```.\n\nFor th... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[Dynamo] Fix bugs when calling tensor.data and tensor.layout (#89257)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/186192bb26a71ec9b0131a6c49fdf19e76d208d7"
      },
      {
        "author": "Kunal Bhalla <kunalb@fb.com>",
        "commit": "b00a4b7",
        "date": "2022-09-09",
        "message": "[torch.fx.wrap] Use callable / function.__name__ instead of function.__code__.co_name (#84373)\n\nRan across this issue while using torch.fx.wrap on a decorated function: it triggered a KeyError: 'wrapper_inside_decorator'. torch.fx.wrap stores function.__code__.co_name, but that isn't set correctly (and doesn't match it's name in the global namespace) for decorators; function.__name__ is set correctly.\n\nAlso adjusted to checking for callable instead of checking for the existing of __code__ to allow for a broader variety of functions that can be passed in. Eg. using functools.cache returns a cal... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[torch.fx.wrap] Use callable / function.__name__ instead of function.__code__.co_name (#84373)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/b00a4b7cf1655cf9a6cf78ef9536b63898cbfb08"
      },
      {
        "author": "Michael Suo <suo@fb.com>",
        "commit": "179dbd4",
        "date": "2020-06-30",
        "message": "[jit] preserve keys on dictionary input tracing (#40792)\n\nSummary:\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/40792\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/40529.\n\nOne followup should be to produce a better error message when a new\ndictionary has different keys than the traced input. Right now it\npresents as a fairly opaque `KeyError`.\n\nTest Plan: Imported from OSS\n\nDifferential Revision: D22311731\n\nPulled By: suo\n\nfbshipit-source-id: c9fbe0b54cf69daed2f11a191d988568521a3932",

        "author": "Kefei Lu <kefeilu@fb.com>",

        "message": "[fx2trt] fix acc_tracer when run against module that contains ScriptModule submodules (#67567)\n\nSummary:\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/67567\n\n- Fix an issue to allow it to work against modules that contains ScriptModule submodules.\n- Fix a bug where `getattr(base_class, method_name)` could raise KeyError\n\nTest Plan: linter; CI;\n\nReviewed By: 842974287\n\nDifferential Revision: D31956070\n\nfbshipit-source-id: 1114937f380af437fd6d36cd811ef609d7faefe7",

        "commit": "f11f9a8",

        "message": "[pytorch][improvement] Improve torch logging to identify problematic key (#45766)\n\nSummary:\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/45766\n\nAs per subj, making KeyError message more verbose.\n\nTest Plan:\nVerified that breakage can be successfully investigated with verbose error message\nunit tests\n\nReviewed By: esqu1\n\nDifferential Revision: D24080362\n\nfbshipit-source-id: f4e22a78809e5cff65a69780d5cbbc1e8b11b2e5",

      {
        "author": "Joel Schlosser <jbschlosser@meta.com>",
        "commit": "07b618e",
        "date": "2024-03-26",
        "message": "Graph break cleanly in Dynamo for module parametrization (#121041)\n\nFixes #118795\n\nThis is a graph breaking partial fix for #120914. We still need -actual- module parametrization tracing support, but at least it doesn't blow up hard now.\n\n**Background**: Module parametrization injects a property as the module parameter attribute that calls a `nn.Module` whose forward takes in a module parameter and returns a reparametrized module parameter.\nExample:\n```\nclass MyParametrization(nn.Module):\n    def forward(X):\n        # This reparametrization just negates the original parameter value\n        ret... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Graph break cleanly in Dynamo for module parametrization (#121041)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/07b618e2d4a46f2ecd57131f34d92d47f109d220"
      },

[truncated]",

      {
        "author": "Kevin Tse <ktse@fb.com>",
        "commit": "14b660f",
        "date": "2022-08-02",
        "message": "[DataPipe] Correct the type of exception that is being raised by ShufflerMapDataPipe (#82666)\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/data/issues/708\n\nThe following code snippet used to fail, now it has been added as a test case:\n```python\ndp1 = dp.map.SequenceWrapper(range(10))\nshuffle_dp1 = dp1.shuffle()\ndp2 = dp.map.SequenceWrapper(range(10))\nshuffle_dp2 = dp2.shuffle()\nzip_dp = shuffle_dp1.zip(shuffle_dp2)\nlist(zip_dp)  # This used to fail\n```\n\nThe issue was that `ShufflerMapDataPipe` raises a `KeyError` when an out of bound index is passed into it, but that was not handled by `zip_dp`'s `__geti... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[DataPipe] Correct the type of exception that is being raised by ShufflerMapDataPipe (#82666)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/14b660fcc0c32a7478c69a25a253b67d0ed36364"
      },
      {
        "author": "vfdev <vfdev.5@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "456b745",
        "date": "2025-06-27",
        "message": "Minor error message fix in device_mesh.py (#157096)\n\nFixed error message:\nOn main:\n```\nKeyError: (\"Invalid mesh_dim_names ('dp_shard', 'dp_shard') specified. \", 'Found mesh dim indices to slice: [(1,), (1,)]. ', 'Mesh dim indices should be in ascending order.')\n```\nOn PR:\n```\nKeyError: Invalid mesh_dim_names ('dp_shard', 'dp_shard') specified. Found mesh dim indices to slice: [(1,), (1,)]. Mesh dim indices should be in ascending order.'\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157096\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Minor error message fix in device_mesh.py (#157096)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/456b7451c78096fd512c60e26cc618386a251bc7"
      },
      {
        "author": "Peter Bell <peterbell10@live.co.uk>",
        "commit": "2784b3f",
        "date": "2024-07-24",
        "message": "[inductor] Fix split-scan interaction with multi-kernel (#131044)\n\nThis fixes a couple errors that come up when multi-kernel is used with\nsplit-scan.\n1. The split-scan was being marked as a persistent kernel, which allowed\n   a multi-kernel to be created but this isn't supported. Fix is to\n   never mark split-scan as persistent.\n2. Benchmark codegen was not handling WorkspaceArg, and would raise a\n   KeyError during codegen.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/131044\nApproved by: https://github.com/shunting314",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[inductor] Fix split-scan interaction with multi-kernel (#131044)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2784b3f1b7e574f0dfe1ad5f5a3624f0bb63a855"
      },
      {
        "author": "angelayi <yiangela7@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "01388d0",
        "date": "2024-01-25",
        "message": "[dynamo] Slightly better error message if key not in dict (#117902)\n\nWas debugging an export issue, and currently when `key` does not exist in `self.items`, the error message is\n```\n  File \"/opt/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/variables/dicts.py\", line 208, in getitem_const\n    return self.items[key]\n           ~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^\ntorch._dynamo.exc.InternalTorchDynamoError: <torch._dynamo.variables.dicts.ConstDictVariable._HashableTracker object at 0x7fd7697cbf90>\n```\nThis PR changes it to be the following.\n```\nFile \"/data/users/angelayi/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/variables/dicts.py\", line 199, in getitem_const\n... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Slightly better error message if key not in dict (#117902)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/01388d07900ff9adea4de42cbd07ffca384d2dbb"
      },
      {
        "author": "XiaobingSuper <xiaobing.zhang@intel.com>",
        "commit": "d4e0d89",
        "date": "2023-03-03",

        "author": "Kaiyu Shi <skyisno.1@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "444f792",
        "date": "2019-04-23",
        "message": "Fix lack of state init for adagrad and add share_memory flag (#17679)\n\nSummary:\nThe current code initialize the `state` in `__init__` method, but the initialization process is not invoked in `add_parameter_group`.\n\nI followed the same approach in other Optimizers to init the `state`.\n\n```python\nimport torch\n\nemb = torch.nn.Embedding(10,10)\nemb2 = torch.nn.Embedding(10,10)\n\noptim = torch.optim.Adagrad(emb.parameters())\nprint(optim.state[emb.weight])  # already initialized\n\noptim.add_param_group({'params': emb2.parameters()})\nprint(optim.state[emb2.weight])  # empty dict\n\nloss = emb2.weight.sum(... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Fix lack of state init for adagrad and add share_memory flag (#17679)",

        "author": "Eric Hanson <5846501+ericphanson@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "33dee72",
        "date": "2024-12-14",
        "message": "Reraise worker errors as runtime errors in more cases when the original exception can't be constructed (#140911)\n\nrelated to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/34130\n\nwhen pytorch attempts to re-raise an exception from a worker process (e.g. multiprocessing dataloader), if it can't reconstruct the original exception message due to a type error, it instead raises it as a runtime error. However, if it can't reconstruct the exception for some other reason, it throws an error with a stacktrace pointing to the `ExceptionWrapper` code rather than the original underlying issue.\n\nOne case in wh... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

        "message": "Support 3D mesh/point cloud (#20413)\n\nSummary:\nI started adding support for the new **[mesh/point cloud](https://github.com/tensorflow/graphics/blob/master/tensorflow_graphics/g3doc/tensorboard.md)** data type introduced to TensorBoard recently.\n\nI created the functions to add the data, created the appropriate summaries.\nThis new data type however requires a **Merged** summary containing the data for the vertices, colors and faces.\n\nI got stuck at this stage.       {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "7ede78f",
        "date": "2024-06-06",
        "message": "[dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)\n\nTracing through `__init__`  is important because it initializes (calls STORE_ATTR) on members. By doing that, we kick in the mutation tracking for these objects. So, things like mutating `_modules` etc is tracked automatically.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/126578\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\nghstack dependencies: #128001",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo][nn-modules] Trace through nn.Module dunder methods for UnspecializedNNModule (#126578)",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@fb.com>",
        "commit": "998c077",
        "date": "2023-06-27",
        "message": "[dynamo] fix deep nested closure cell KeyError (#104222)\n\nFix https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99639 by handling the case in `InliningInstructionTranslator`'s `LOAD_CLOSURE` definition when the requested cell is not in `self.closure_cells`.\n\nMy intuition is that the behavior of `LOAD_DEREF` and `STORE_DEREF` on a cell/freevar should not depend on whether or not we called `LOAD_CLOSURE` (that is, we shouldn't create a new cell var in `LOAD_CLOSURE` like in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/101357). But we need a way to push cells created by the inlined function that were not pres... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] fix deep nested closure cell KeyError (#104222)",

      }

[truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8ec551bb354ab2b85fbbba9d461740a20366d248\"},{\"author\":\"Rob Timpe <rtimpe@openteams.com>\",\"commit\":\"1463714\",\"date\":\"2025-09-04\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\n\\nCurrently, user-defined classes inside of a compiled frame will cause the whole\\nframe to be skipped by dynamo.  This change defers the Unsupported exception\\nuntil the __build_class__ builtin is actually called, which allows a graph break\\nto be inserted.  Fixes #161562\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670\\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/guilhermeleobas\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"f36f285\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\\n\\nThis PR implements the semantics change to `torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break`:\\n- ~`torch.compile` now has a new `error_on_graph_break` kwarg that serves as a lower-priority toggle for erroring/continuing on graph breaks~\\n- `error_on_graph_break` is a new internal `torch.compile `setting that is lower-priority than `fullgraph`. It allows the user to toggle erroring/continuing on graph breaks.\\n- `error_on_graph_break` does nothing when `fullgraph=True`\\n- `error_on_graph_break` does NOT guarantee a single graph\\n\\nFollowup [DONE]... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/f36f285953700f971552083a5da9d0ceacb63bbd\"},{\"author\":\"abmajumder <debabhi1396@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"0ef5ba4\",\"date\":\"2025-05-14\",\"message\":\"Fix negative dim issue in for parallel loss context manager (#152785)\\n\\nFacing similar issue as on #152016  , and added as per @tianyu-l 's solution.\\nFixes #152016\\n\\n Tagging @tianyu-l @atalman  for review.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152785\\nApproved by: https://github.com/tianyu-l\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fix negative dim issue in for parallel loss context manager (#152785)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0ef5ba43a6e7fe806ea9f27929bf4328ffd1ebf4\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"2c372a0\",\"date\":\"2025-06-19\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\n\\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\\n\\nImplementation notes:\\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"5e56db5\",\"date\":\"2025-06-22\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 2c372a0502578e0136a84423c3f49c19c26d6bb7.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154289 on behalf of https://github.com/ezyang due to All of this is responsible for regression, see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156561 ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154283#issuecomment-2994242583))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5e56db59d46e34e6b0e7b6e7a7bf213f01349969\"},{\"author\":\"rzou <zou3519@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"199c363\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"Fix Inductor Periodic (#161617)\\n\\nModels are now passing accuracy. # of graph breaks is larger because\\nthese were not actually tested in CI (if the model fails accuracy we\\ndo not assert on # of graph breaks).\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161617\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fix Inductor Periodic (#161617)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/199c3633bfb32cddbc60cde473b79a917226a705\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"1041805\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\\n\\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\\n\\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\\n\\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1041805c1ed0d4d96fd05cf485a0d89a0b5588d0\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"8b78ba0\",\"date\":\"2025-08-26\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\\n\\nNote: nested graph break tests (and wrapped tests) are xfailed/skipped for now - we will iteratively enable the tests as more of the nested graph break implementation is complete.\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D81084809](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81084809)\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8b78ba07b14d774f889f3b863a766e65d60cabff\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"ac6316c\",\"date\":\"2025-08-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\\n\\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\\n\\nAlso simplify some codegen.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678, #159817\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/ac6316caaa74513cbcf3c7f9269bc23cd74749db\"},{\"author\":\"Tristan Rice <rice@fn.lc>\",\"commit\":\"83700b4\",\"date\":\"2025-07-10\",\"message\":\"dist2: add group context manager (#157988)\\n\\nThis adds new context manager based PG management to dist2. This allows for managing the active process group much in the same way as a stream\\n\\n```py\\nwith dist2.process_group(pg):\\n   dist2.current_process_group().allreduce(...).wait()\\n```\\n\\nmatches\\n\\n```py\\nwith torch.cuda.stream(stream):\\n    torch.cuda.current_stream().synchronize()\\n```\\n\\nTest plan:\\n\\n```\\npytest test/distributed/test_dist2.py -k context\\n```\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157988\\nApproved by: https://github.com/fduwjj\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"dist2: add group context manager (#157988)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/83700b448874cac8e89356ab06292e4289577269\"},{\"author\":\"rzou <zou3519@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"aa2d541\",\"date\":\"2025-06-26\",\"message\":\"Add AOTDispatcher config to set backward autocast behavior (#156356)\\n\\nThis PR adds a new config `backward_pass_autocast`, to set the backward autocast\\nbehavior. It does not change the existing behavior.\\n\\nThe reason why we need this is that torch.compile acquires a forward and\\nbackward graph at the time of the forward pass. This means that\\nimplemented naively, if there are any context managers active outside\\nthe call to torch.compile, the backward graph will also get the\\nbehaviors from those context managers. This PR gives users a way to\\ntweak the autocast behavior of the backward pass.\\n\\nPlease... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Add AOTDispatcher config to set backward autocast behavior (#156356)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/aa2d54148d476383986855af3fe53862da861dda\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"7b7eafe\",\"date\":\"2025-06-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\n\\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\\n\\nImplementation notes:\\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7b7eafe7ba159feed8324ba64e53c113fe7cb8b2\"},{\"author\":\"bobrenjc93 <bobren@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"9c06dff\",\"date\":\"2025-05-29\",\"message\":\"[multigraph] use specializations in compile_and_call_fx_graph (#153449)\\n\\nThe goal of this multigraph work is to enable a compiled region that has a single dynamo trace but multiple backend specializations. This work was inspired by vLLM which does this in a somewhat hacky way where they use a custom backend to capture a dynamo graph and then manually invoke compile_fx multiple times to get specialized graphs.\\n\\nThere's really two parts of this work:\\n\\n**The frontend changes:**\\n1) we introduce an optional kwarg `specialize_on` to mark_{dynamic,unbacked} that takes in a list of specializations. I ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[multigraph] use specializations in compile_and_call_fx_graph (#153449)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/9c06dff1ce96a11705acb7fb8f66141f5aad949b\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"02fa5bf\",\"date\":\"2025-08-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, <yiangela7@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"5c67426\",\"date\":\"2025-09-05\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Add support for const prop on .item (#162204)\\n\\nFixes some of the errors in https://fb.workplace.com/groups/1028545332188949/permalink/1303030824740397/\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162204\\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Add support for const prop on .item (#162204)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5c67426d6847667a7c55a2dd01f470fa37238c18\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"10d9332\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\\n\\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\\n```python\\nimport torch\\n\\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\\n\\ndef inner(x):\\n    x = x + 1\\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n    return x + 2\\n\\n@torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\ndef outer(x):\\n    return inner(x)\\n\\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\\n```\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks internally ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329#issuecomment-3225617445))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"49d0332\",\"date\":\"2025-07-15\",\"message\":\"Introduce stages to aot_dispatch (#158213)\\n\\nThe starting point for this refactor is that I need access to the fully\\ngeneral joint graph representation in an export-like interface, but I\\nthen subsequently need a way to feed this joint graph into the rest of\\nthe compilation pipeline so I can get an actual callable that I can run\\nonce I've finished modifying it.  Previously, people had added export\\ncapabilities to AOTAutograd by having an export flag that toggled what\\nexactly the functions return and triggering aot_dispatch to go to a\\ndifferent \\\"export\\\" implementation, but I've found this difficu... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Introduce stages to aot_dispatch (#158213)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/49d0332cef68be82eed713e265800f82d6cbff75\"},{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"c5d3e7a\",\"date\":\"2025-06-18\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 920f6e681ec70b664ed952255b8c1f97962f5de0.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154289 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to inductor/test_flex_decoding.py::TestFlexDecodingCUDA::test_do_not_trigger_dynamic_shapes_on_empty_block_mask_cuda GH job link HUD commit link ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154289#issuecomment-2984774814))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/c5d3e7a4ff460eed70b8443485a7e3568e87aee9\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"920f6e6\",\"date\":\"2025-06-17\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\\n\\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\\n\\nImplementation notes:\\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/920f6e681ec70b664ed952255b8c1f97962f5de0\"},{\"author\":\"Sam Larsen <slarsen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"0639527\",\"date\":\"2025-07-31\",\"message\":\"Remove dynamo_timed from the CachingAutotuner.coordinate_descent_tuning() hot path. (#159588)\\n\\nSummary: When coordinate_descent_tuning==True, CachingAutotuner.coordinate_descent_tuning() is called for every call of CachingAutotuner.run() (at least for Triton templates), but immediately returns the launcher. Move the dynamo_timed call after the check for triton template so we don't incur the context manager overhead on every call.\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/159525\\n\\nTest Plan: Used the repro in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/159525 to make sure the overhead goes ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Remove dynamo_timed from the CachingAutotuner.coordinate_descent_tuning() hot path. (#159588)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/06395276e4bbb06f396fcee6f3240741fdd0b63c\"},{\"author\":\"Arsh Zahed <arshzahed@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"4c45090\",\"date\":\"2025-09-09\",\"message\":\"[DTensor] Check if tracing for sharding propagation to handle unhashable keys (#160798)\\n\\nFixes #159590\\n\\nThis is similar to the reverted commit #156868, except it resolves an issue with two caches becoming misaligned, leading to incorrect objects for stateful placements (i.e. `_MaskPartial`) as in issue #159601. This adds little to no overhead in eager ([see past benchmarks](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156868#issuecomment-3047831149)).\\n\\nThis also handles cases such as #159590  where dynamo is disabled during tracing by entering the Python Dispatcher ahead of the sharding propogation... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[DTensor] Check if tracing for sharding propagation to handle unhashable keys (#160798)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/4c45090cf7e2f39b44c2d4da367366c9ec0cba68\"},{\"author\":\"Brian Hirsh <hirsheybar@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"0d71a9d\",\"date\":\"2025-08-15\",\"message\":\"fix incorrect interaction between DDPOptimizer and donated buffers (#160745)\\n\\nThis should fix https://x.com/wightmanr/status/1953147089518772254?t=ng_R4t0-tRhO_qQE8NqOhw&s=19. Still working on adding a reasonable test.\\n\\nYou can see more of a description of the problem in the code comments. But the TLDR is that:\\n\\n* When using DDPOptimizer, we partition the graph and compile several subgraphs. So 1 dynamo graphs becomes N AOT/inductor artifacts\\n* We have some existing logic to stash graph metadata (`fw_metadata`) in dynamo's TracingContext. When using DDPOptimizer, we generate one `fw_metadata` ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"fix incorrect interaction between DDPOptimizer and donated buffers (#160745)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0d71a9dd5b4b6d1dde58d91c9b71d96bc6a6a171\"},{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"6686974\",\"date\":\"2025-08-26\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 9a756c2d710a0680bac93ab0b42db519ec2dc6cf.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516#issuecomment-3225659358))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6686974ddd7a616652d9f3dea9195ba8d6d02769\"},{\"author\":\"Simon Fan <xmfan@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"8085edc\",\"date\":\"2025-08-05\",\"message\":\"[autograd] torch._C._set_view_replay_enabled state leaking into other tests (#159840)\\n\\nThis was causing view_fns to pop up in tests that ran after `TestAutograd.test_view_replay_enabled` where it isn't used as a context manager. It is unclear to me why we would want `_force_original_view_tracking` to mutate global state on __init__ rather than on __enter__, that could be an alternative fix.\\n\\nFIXES https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/156306 https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/156289 https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/156265 https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/156209\\nPul... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[autograd] torch._C._set_view_replay_enabled state leaking into other tests (#159840)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8085edc8f9c98f670f585586b4286a942927537a\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"5b9df57\",\"date\":\"2025-04-22\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] context manager/decorator for dynamo config patching during tracing (#150586)\\n\\nImplement traceable config patching for Dynamo: enables restricted patching of Dynamo config where user can use a context manager/decorator to change tracing behavior for parts of the code.\\n\\nThe new `dont_skip_tracing` decorator/context manager for ignoring most trace rules is easily implemented with this more generic traceable config patching feature.\\n\\nImplementation:\\n- Create a new specialized context manager class representing a wrapper around torch._dynamo.config.patch\\n- Dynamo doesn't trace into the co... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] context manager/decorator for dynamo config patching during tracing (#150586)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5b9df57b50bc20a299da1329ee52fb7a8f68c930\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"600c25e\",\"date\":\"2025-09-02\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on torch.cuda.sychronize (#161925)\\n\\nToday, AOTDispatcher ignores cuda.synchornize. Even if we wrap it in\\nsome  HOP, we need it to be a barrier op to prevent any inductor\\nreordering. So graph breaking.\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/160751\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161925\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519, https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/mlazos\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on torch.cuda.sychronize (#161925)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/600c25e9a17fe56e3dee872be8854db08916ba0c\"},{\"author\":\"Simon Fan <xmfan@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"17b38b8\",\"date\":\"2025-06-17\",\"message\":\"[ca] Allow using compiled autograd context managers during backward runtime (#156120)\\n\\nAdded an invariant that nested compiled autograd context managers must exit before their parent context manager. This allows us to defer the thread check.\\n\\nFIXES https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/152219\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156120\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\\nghstack dependencies: #155521, #155480\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[ca] Allow using compiled autograd context managers during backward runtime (#156120)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/17b38b850e5362c2f5d7cd75e6552e3d149232f2\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"67d31f6\",\"date\":\"2025-08-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\\n\\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\\n\\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\\n\\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/67d31f6b281d3b15b205756fc7ebc450cdde1dab\"},{\"author\":\"Scott Wolchok <swolchok@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"e025c0f\",\"date\":\"2025-09-08\",\"message\":\"Dynamo: set_eval_frame microoptimization (#162220)\\n\\nOptimize for common case and remove a pair of refcount operations (see new comments.)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162220\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/williamwen42\\nghstack dependencies: #161591, #161595, #161633, #161634, #161692, #162219\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Dynamo: set_eval_frame microoptimization (#162220)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e025c0f45945eac2ece9091cc7c0c5f784b2b172\"},{\"author\":\"Tugsbayasgalan (Tugsuu) Manlaibaatar <tmanlaibaatar@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"2ac45c2\",\"date\":\"2025-08-01\",\"message\":\"Fix autocast context manager when there is exception (#159565)\\n\\nSummary: When exception occurs inside context manager, we need to either return False OR properly propagage exceptions via __exit__(exc_type, exc_val). But previously while tracing, we don't actually run the exit node so we end up swallowing the exception in a very weird way as outlined in https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/153202. This PR fixes it\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nnew test case\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D79348382\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159565\\nApproved by: https://github.com... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Fix autocast context manager when there is exception (#159565)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2ac45c2752b0467608869751b1c7eb305949db2e\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"26a1b9c\",\"date\":\"2025-09-08\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D81938289](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81938289)\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162318\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914\"},{\"author\":\"Sam Larsen <slarsen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"8542d55\",\"date\":\"2025-04-24\",\"message\":\"[logging] Clean up dynamo_timed usages in cudagraph_trees (#152136)\\n\\nSummary: I'm investigating differences in total torch.compile overhead in our two main internal sources: dynamo_compile and pt2_compile_events. One source of discrepancy is due to cudagraphs overheads. Currently, we have a context manager that optionally attributes a dynamo_timed region to a cudagraph-related column logged to dynamo_compile, but _all_ dynamo_timed regions show up in pt2_compile_events (hence the discrepancy; pt2_compile_events is overcounting). We could filter out these specific events from pt2_compile_events... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[logging] Clean up dynamo_timed usages in cudagraph_trees (#152136)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8542d55f0c003767fa8fbf5399a77fcf178e1795\"},{\"author\":\"bobrenjc93 <bobren@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"172015f\",\"date\":\"2025-05-29\",\"message\":\"[multigraph] add specialize_on kwarg to mark_{dynamic,unbacked} (#153433)\\n\\nThe goal of this multigraph work is to enable a compiled region that has a single dynamo trace but multiple backend specializations. This work was inspired by vLLM which does this in a somewhat hacky way where they use a custom backend to capture a dynamo graph and then manually invoke compile_fx multiple times to get specialized graphs.\\n\\nThere's really two parts of this work:\\n\\n**The frontend changes (this PR):**\\n1) we introduce an optional kwarg `specialize_on` to mark_{dynamic,unbacked} that takes in a list of special... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[multigraph] add specialize_on kwarg to mark_{dynamic,unbacked} (#153433)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/172015fc1131e7475af36f5ae8c9bdc05a4f356f\"},{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"46576f5\",\"date\":\"2025-08-26\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 67d31f6b281d3b15b205756fc7ebc450cdde1dab.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786#issuecomment-3225535752))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/46576f5a164fcf95ec7fceaa13516bcb1ca4f6ab\"},{\"author\":\"Yu, Guangye <guangye.yu@intel.com>\",\"commit\":\"2ba2f59\",\"date\":\"2025-08-05\",\"message\":\"[Dynamo] Add torch.xpu.stream to trace rules (#159844)\\n\\n# Motivation\\nPreviously, I thought using `with stream:` was sufficient. However, many older scripts still use `torch.xpu.stream` as the context manager. To maintain backward compatibility, I had to include `torch.xpu.stream` in the trace rules.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159844\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Dynamo] Add torch.xpu.stream to trace rules (#159844)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2ba2f598f3d6b9b656ce850a6b58be99b2d7b162\"},{\"author\":\"soulitzer <soulitzer@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"1e4dfee\",\"date\":\"2025-08-20\",\"message\":\"Add early_stop kwarg to torch.utils.checkpoint (#160781)\\n\\nWe already have a context manager \\\"set_checkpoint_early_stop\\\". This PR adds a kwarg that toggles the same setting.\\n\\nIt is also useful to have a kwarg version of the setting in addition to the context manager because is annoying to apply a context manager when the AC is being applied via CheckpointWrapper.\\n\\nSimilar to the \\\"debug\\\" kwarg and the corresponding \\\"set_checkpoint_debug_enabled\\\" context manager, the context manager defaults to None and overrides the local setting when non-None.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Add early_stop kwarg to torch.utils.checkpoint Yu <shangdiy@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"82a1ee1\",\"date\":\"2025-07-17\",\"message\":\"Refactor Provenance Tracking (#158399)\\n\\nSummary:\\nAs inductor provenance tracking is getting more use cases, we want to separate the inductor provenance tracking guarding flag from the general `trace.enabled`, so we can enable provenance tracking without all the overhead of `trace.enabled`\\n\\n- change the guard flag from `trace.enabled` to `trace.provenance_tracking`.  It is turned on by either `TORCH_COMPILE_DEBUG=1` or `INDUCTOR_PROVENANCE=1`.\\n- Move the provenance tracking logic and variables out of DebugContext, because DebugContext is only enabled with `trace.enabled`. Since the variables ar... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Refactor Provenance Tracking (#158399)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/82a1ee1135b054d371d10081883b848ac7b7419f\"},{\"author\":\"Edward Z. Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"5b0df25\",\"date\":\"2025-07-15\",\"message\":\"Pipeline _create_aot_dispatcher_function (#158173)\\n\\nTwo main things of note:\\n\\n- Review this diff without whitespace changes\\n- To ensure that context managers correctly propagate to later pipeline\\n  stages, I am using the ExitStack trick: there is an ExitStack which is\\n  in scope for the entire pipeline, and inside of the individual\\n  pipeline stages we push context managers onto this stack when we want\\n  them to survive into the next pipeline stage.  This is not obviously\\n  what the best final form of the code is, but\\n  create_aot_dispatcher_function is called from multiple locations so I\\n  ca... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Pipeline _create_aot_dispatcher_function (#158173)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5b0df2565ebb9677f34ae8eeb8c08fafaeacb15d\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"6b1900c\",\"date\":\"2025-08-28\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][hops] Remove const outputs from the speculated subgraph (#161355)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161355\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][hops] Remove const outputs from the speculated subgraph (#161355)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6b1900c22f1a07b9519346898d4c71d8a2b0f12f\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"8678d83\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] rename set_fullgraph to error_on_graph_break (#161739)\\n\\nRenaming `set_fullgraph` to `error_on_graph_break` for now. There are no semantic differences yet. In a followup PR, we will introduce a new `torch.compile` option `error_on_graph_break` that has lower priority than `fullgraph` so that `fullgraph` really returns 1 graph.\\n\\nI could keep `set_fullgraph` as a deprecated alias for `error_on_graph_break` for now, but I'm hoping that won't be necessary since it's still private API (there are no internal callsites yet, and there are no significant OSS callsites yet).\\n\\n cc @albanD @voznes... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] rename set_fullgraph to error_on_graph_break (#161739)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8678d831c48e616b717bff50f2d03141d2e9f965\"},{\"author\":\"Paul de Supinski <pdesupinski@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"33346b5\",\"date\":\"2025-08-23\",\"message\":\"Support NUMA Binding for Callable Entrypoints, Take 2 (#161183)\\n\\n# Context\\nIn #160163, we added support for NUMA binding for `Callable` entrypoints to `elastic_launch`. This requires special consideration, because they go through a different path to spawn subprocesses compared to `str` entrypoints, a path which does not provide a straightforward way to utilize `numactl` CLI. See #160006 for a full description of the challenges.\\n\\nAlthough #160163 worked in initial local experiments, we ran into some linker errors in other environments when we tried to call `numactl`. This appeared to be due to ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Support NUMA Binding for Callable Entrypoints, Take 2 (#161183)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/33346b58148c55592994a43385c321ae8c8808f2\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"e3068cd\",\"date\":\"2025-09-05\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Use relaxed CLOSURE_MATCH guard then ID_MATCH (#162247)\\n\\nI am unable to write a test that would fail here. The reason is that when we do _dynamo.disable(fn) in the compiled frame, the id of disabled function changes but currently we guard on the original function - `fn` whose id is not changing. This PR still guards on the `fn.__code__` just to be more precise.\\n\\nThanks to @thenumberouscode for pointing this out.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162247\\nApproved by: https://github.com/StrongerXi, https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Use relaxed CLOSURE_MATCH guard then ID_MATCH (#162247)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e3068cdb446adefb5a875616ba37a60235391439\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"6562646\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\\n\\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\\n\\nAlso simplify some codegen.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6562646dab09b76479466af3b2a0e866b9c08cdd\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"ef0ef6f\",\"date\":\"2025-08-25\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159817\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/ef0ef6f93f7ef6d16d71a6997b72185504acd4b6\"},{\"author\":\"Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"a1a555e\",\"date\":\"2025-08-15\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break on calling functions decorated with special context manager (#160703)\\n\\nAs title. This is a follow-up of the previous patch, with the goal of\\nsupporting a new pattern that showed up in ComfyUI:\\nhttps://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/blob/644b23ac0b92442b475e44397c62aa8de929d546/comfy/ops.py#L44\\n\\nEffectively, the semantics of calling a function decorated with a\\ncontext manager is:\\n\\n```python\\n@ctx_manager(args)\\ndef f(x):\\n    ...\\n\\nf(x)\\n# ----->\\nwith ctx_manager(args):\\n    f.__wrapped__(x)\\n```\\n\\nYes, a fresh context manager instance per invokation, see CPython source code... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break on calling functions decorated with special context manager (#160703)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a1a555ed7b98046e5c8ee18a903b9381e7ce322d\"},{\"author\":\"bobrenjc93 <bobren@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"e7bf72c\",\"date\":\"2025-05-29\",\"message\":\"[multigraph] fix composabilty with aotautograd cache (#153526)\\n\\nAOTAutogradCache uses FXGraphCache which uses the tracing context to get the ShapeEnv. Although the TracingContext global_context is cleared by the time we get around to reusing it, we don't actually need it. We just need the ShapeEnv in the TracingContext, which isn't cleared at the end of dynamo and does persist. This PR adds the tracing context manager around the specialized compile to ensure our caching infrastructure can get access to the ShapeEnv. A test was also added to prove correctness.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://gi... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[multigraph] fix composabilty with aotautograd cache (#153526)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e7bf72c90822c08d8e54f7c3e3af1d5ce4dcb5c7\"},{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"caf98fd\",\"date\":\"2025-08-26\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit ac6316caaa74513cbcf3c7f9269bc23cd74749db.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138#issuecomment-3225546707))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/caf98fde0d5c47452af45dc77099449edd521579\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"2efcf9d\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break registry loading in fbcode (#161550)\\n\\nSummary: Add `torch/_dynamo/graph_break_registry.json` as an internal dependency. Minor related fixes.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nTest on OSS.\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D81078973\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161550\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break registry loading in fbcode (#161550)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2efcf9d0815fd5c729abcbb5622746184b19c3cc\"},{\"author\":\"Sam Larsen <slarsen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"7a41f20\",\"date\":\"2025-07-08\",\"message\":\"[inductor] Quiesce Triton compile worker pool after each dynamo compile (#156187)\\n\\nFor internal usages, keeping the Triton compile worker pool active for the lifetime of the process has caused some challenges, e.g., it slows down and muddies profiling due to the huge number of threads on a box: N threads = 8 ranks * 32 subprocs * M threads started by torch. Also, each subproc can use more than 1GB each. This PR adds the functionality to shutdown worker subprocs after each dynamo compile when using the SubprocPool implementation. The idea is to leave the main sidecar process running, but signal... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[inductor] Quiesce Triton compile worker pool after each dynamo compile (#156187)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7a41f207943cc6c88160d7a531fc57bdbe149253\"},{\"author\":\"Ruben Rodriguez Buchillon <coconutruben@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"496052f\",\"date\":\"2025-08-28\",\"message\":\"[inductor][decompose-k] make part of template heuristics (#161098)\\n\\n# why\\n\\n- enable it to go through commont template heuristics point\\n- make easier to use in common extension point e.g. lookup table\\n\\n# what\\n\\n- break template heuristic into base + triton\\n- move k_split generation logic into a templateheuristic for decompose k\\n- register through normal mechanism\\n\\n- to make testing work, add a context manager to temporarily set\\n  template heuristics for a template/op to empty (effectively skipping\\n  it). This is used for decompose k test to disable triton choices\\n\\n# testing\\n\\n```\\npython3 -bb -m p... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[inductor][decompose-k] make part of template heuristics (#161098)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/496052faf6ca4f8c1df4e441e40b5d267272a89f\"},{\"author\":\"dolpm <34420038+dolpm@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"e0a62b2\",\"date\":\"2025-09-05\",\"message\":\"[aot-precompile] default-filter global guards (#162090)\\n\\nif the user doesn't provide their own guard filter fn, we should by default filter global guards.\\n\\npytest test/dynamo/test_aot_compile.py\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162090\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zhxchen17\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[aot-precompile] default-filter global guards (#162090)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e0a62b266c021b910ce6dc02a6c9429210487717\"},{\"author\":\"Raman-RH <ramakuma@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"20bfb25\",\"date\":\"2025-09-01\",\"message\":\"Skip compilation when FX graph has no calls and returns empty (#160536)\\n\\nFixes #160437\\n\\nSummary:\\nThis PR avoids compiling empty FX graphs generated during graph breaks. If there are no calls in the graph, we can just return the empty list of instructions.\\n\\nMore precisely,\\nIn compile_and_call_fx_graph, if the FX graph contains no calls (count_calls(self.graph) == 0) and the return value list is empty, we now return an empty instruction list immediately\\n\\nImpact:\\nmodule: dynamo\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160536\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Skip compilation when FX graph has no calls and returns empty (#160536)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/20bfb2539d7c5250379648eda35f80b8a7d642dd\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:dynamo OR message:\\\"graph break\\\" OR message:\\\"context manager\\\") repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d count:50\",\"totalCount\":64}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "3f80908",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/3f8090809faf0b51456d9f427dd93ed8da3a11f6"

      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "6afd766",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 02fa5bf6d80fa4baa6bb6dd2fa6a16d88852da91.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678#issuecomment-3225597425))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\"",

      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "0ff8eab",
        "date": "2025-09-06",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670 on behalf of https://github.com/jeanschmidt due to seems to have introduced https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379267 and https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379271 ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670#issuecomment-3261241229))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

        "author": "dolpm <34420038+dolpm@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "8ec551b",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)\n\npytest test/dynamo/test_aot_compile.py -k test_aot_compile_graph_break_error_fmt\n\nbefore\n```\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"/data/users/$USER/vllm-tests/graph-break.py\", line 15, in <module>\n    aot_compiled_fn = compiled.aot_compile((example_inputs, {}))\n                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n  File \"/data/users/$USER/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/eval_frame.py\", line 717, in aot_compile\n    return aot_compile_fullgraph(\n           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n  F... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8ec551bb354ab2b85fbbba9d461740a20366d248"
      },
      {
        "author": "Rob Timpe <rtimpe@openteams.com>",
        "commit": "1463714",
        "date": "2025-09-04",
        "message": "[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\n\nCurrently, user-defined classes inside of a compiled frame will cause the whole\nframe to be skipped by dynamo.  This change defers the Unsupported exception\nuntil the __build_class__ builtin is actually called, which allows a graph break\nto be inserted.  Fixes #161562\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/guilhermeleobas",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "f36f285",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\n\nThis PR implements the semantics change to `torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break`:\n- ~`torch.compile` now has a new `error_on_graph_break` kwarg that serves as a lower-priority toggle for erroring/continuing on graph breaks~\n- `error_on_graph_break` is a new internal `torch.compile `setting that is lower-priority than `fullgraph`. It allows the user to toggle erroring/continuing on graph breaks.\n- `error_on_graph_break` does nothing when `fullgraph=True`\n- `error_on_graph_break` does NOT guarantee a single graph\n\nFollowup [DONE]... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/f36f285953700f971552083a5da9d0ceacb63bbd"
      },
      {
        "author": "abmajumder <debabhi1396@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "0ef5ba4",
        "date": "2025-05-14",
        "message": "Fix negative dim issue in for parallel loss context manager (#152785)\n\nFacing similar issue as on #152016  , and added as per @tianyu-l 's solution.\nFixes #152016\n\n Tagging @tianyu-l @atalman  for review.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/152785\nApproved by: https://github.com/tianyu-l",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Fix negative dim issue in for parallel loss context manager (#152785)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0ef5ba43a6e7fe806ea9f27929bf4328ffd1ebf4"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "2c372a0",
        "date": "2025-06-19",
        "message": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\n\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\n\nImplementation notes:\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2c372a0502578e0136a84423c3f49c19c26d6bb7"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "5e56db5",
        "date": "2025-06-22",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 2c372a0502578e0136a84423c3f49c19c26d6bb7.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154289 on behalf of https://github.com/ezyang due to All of this is responsible for regression, see https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156561 ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/154283#issuecomment-2994242583))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\"",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5e56db59d46e34e6b0e7b6e7a7bf213f01349969"
      },
      {
        "author": "rzou <zou3519@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "199c363",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "Fix Inductor Periodic (#161617)\n\nModels are now passing accuracy. # of graph breaks is larger because\nthese were not actually tested in CI (if the model fails accuracy we\ndo not assert on # of graph breaks).\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161617\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Fix Inductor Periodic (#161617)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/199c3633bfb32cddbc60cde473b79a917226a705"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "1041805",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\n\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\n\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\n\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1041805c1ed0d4d96fd05cf485a0d89a0b5588d0"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "8b78ba0",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\n\nNote: nested graph break tests (and wrapped tests) are xfailed/skipped for now - we will iteratively enable the tests as more of the nested graph break implementation is complete.\n\nDifferential Revision: [D81084809](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81084809)\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8b78ba07b14d774f889f3b863a766e65d60cabff"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "ac6316c",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\n\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\n\nAlso simplify some codegen.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678, #159817",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/ac6316caaa74513cbcf3c7f9269bc23cd74749db"
      },
      {
        "author": "Tristan Rice <rice@fn.lc>",
        "commit": "83700b4",
        "date": "2025-07-10",
        "message": "dist2: add group context manager (#157988)\n\nThis adds new context manager based PG management to dist2. This allows for managing the active process group much in the same way as a stream\n\n```py\nwith dist2.process_group(pg):\n   dist2.current_process_group().allreduce(...).wait()\n```\n\nmatches\n\n```py\nwith torch.cuda.stream(stream):\n    torch.cuda.current_stream().synchronize()\n```\n\nTest plan:\n\n```\npytest test/distributed/test_dist2.py -k context\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157988\nApproved by: https://github.com/fduwjj",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "dist2: add group context manager (#157988)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/83700b448874cac8e89356ab06292e4289577269"
      },
      {
        "author": "rzou <zou3519@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "aa2d541",
        "date": "2025-06-26",
        "message": "Add AOTDispatcher config to set backward autocast behavior (#156356)\n\nThis PR adds a new config `backward_pass_autocast`, to set the backward autocast\nbehavior. It does not change the existing behavior.\n\nThe reason why we need this is that torch.compile acquires a forward and\nbackward graph at the time of the forward pass. This means that\nimplemented naively, if there are any context managers active outside\nthe call to torch.compile, the backward graph will also get the\nbehaviors from those context managers. This PR gives users a way to\ntweak the autocast behavior of the backward pass.\n\nPlease... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Add AOTDispatcher config to set backward autocast behavior (#156356)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/aa2d54148d476383986855af3fe53862da861dda"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "7b7eafe",
        "date": "2025-06-25",
        "message": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)\n\nImplements https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/144908.\n\nImplementation notes:\n- `set_fullgraph` is implemented using `patch_config`, which changes config correctly during runtime and tracing.\n- Moved setting `config.error_on_graph_break` from convert_frame.py to eval_frame.py. This is because this should only be done at the top-level decorated function. If we kept this in convert_frame.py, we would be changing `config.error_on_graph_break` on every top-level frame, which causes confusing behavior (see added test for example)... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] add set_fullgraph decorator/context manager (#154289)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7b7eafe7ba159feed8324ba64e53c113fe7cb8b2"
      },
      {
        "author": "bobrenjc93 <bobren@meta.com>",
        "commit": "9c06dff",
        "date": "2025-05-29",
        "message": "[multigraph] use specializations in compile_and_call_fx_graph (#153449)\n\nThe goal of this multigraph work is to enable a compiled region that has a single dynamo trace but multiple backend specializations. This work was inspired by vLLM which does this in a somewhat hacky way where they use a custom backend to capture a dynamo graph and then manually invoke compile_fx multiple times to get specialized graphs.\n\nThere's really two parts of this work:\n\n**The frontend changes:**\n1) we introduce an optional kwarg `specialize_on` to mark_{dynamic,unbacked} that takes in a list of specializations. I ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[multigraph] use specializations in compile_and_call_fx_graph (#153449)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/9c06dff1ce96a11705acb7fb8f66141f5aad949b"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "02fa5bf",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/02fa5bf6d80fa4baa6bb6dd2fa6a16d88852da91"
      },
      {
        "author": "angelayi <yiangela7@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "5c67426",
        "date": "2025-09-05",
        "message": "[dynamo] Add support for const prop on .item (#162204)\n\nFixes some of the errors in https://fb.workplace.com/groups/1028545332188949/permalink/1303030824740397/\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162204\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42",

        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/5c67426d6847667a7c55a2dd01f470fa37238c18"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "10d9332",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\n\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\n```python\nimport torch\n\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\n\ndef inner(x):\n    x = x + 1\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n    return x + 2\n\n@torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\ndef outer(x):\n    return inner(x)\n\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/10d93325b1f2ade44bf47fd75a93a8af64f3e620"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "a4fb657",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 8dab6d4c414bf997297804008c3da893e69cd51f.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internally ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329#issuecomment-3225617445))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\"",
