    "totalCount": 64
  }
}
16. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
  "revision": "26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: This module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\\n3: primarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\\n4: bytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\\n5: \\n6: - Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\\n7: - Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\\n8: - Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\\n9: - Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\\n10: - Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\\n11: \\n12: The module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\\n13: Python semantics and execution state.\\n14: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n15: \\n16: import copy\\n17: import dataclasses\\n18: import sys\\n19: import types\\n20: from collections.abc import Iterable\\n21: from contextlib import AbstractContextManager\\n22: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\\n23: \\n24: from .bytecode_transformation import (\\n25:     add_push_null,\\n26:     bytecode_from_template,\\n27:     create_call_function,\\n28:     create_instruction,\\n29:     create_jump_absolute,\\n30:     create_load_const,\\n31:     Instruction,\\n32:     overwrite_instruction,\\n33:     transform_code_object,\\n34:     unique_id,\\n35: )\\n36: from .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\\n37: \\n38: \\n39: # taken from code.h in cpython\\n40: CO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\\n41: CO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\\n42: CO_VARARGS = 0x0004\\n43: CO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\\n44: CO_NESTED = 0x0010\\n45: CO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\\n46: CO_NOFREE = 0x0040\\n47: CO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\\n48: CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\\n49: CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200\\n50: \\n51: # trace_rules.py import this constant for consistency\\n52: TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX = \\\"torch_dynamo_resume_in\\\"\\n53: IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME = \\\"__is_tracing_resume_prologue\\\"\\n54: \\n55: \\n56: def _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\\n57:     if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n58:         insts.append(create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"))\\n59:         if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\\n60:             insts.append(create_instruction(\\\"SWAP\\\", arg=2))\\n61: \\n62: \\n63: # Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\\n64: def _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n65:     template: Callable[..., Any],\\n66:     stack_index: int,\\n67:     varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n68: ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\\n69:     template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\\n70:     template_code.append(create_instruction(\\\"POP_TOP\\\"))\\n71: \\n72:     # adjust exception table entry depth\\n73:     for inst in template_code:\\n74:         if inst.exn_tab_entry:\\n75:             inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\\n76: \\n77:     # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\\n78:     dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\\n79:         (\\n80:             (i, inst)\\n81:             for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\\n82:             if inst.opname == \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\" and inst.argval == \\\"dummy\\\"\\n83:         ),\\n84:         (None, None),\\n85:     )\\n86:     assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\\n87: \\n88:     # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\\n89:     overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\\\"NOP\\\")])\\n90: \\n91:     # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\\n92:     assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \\\"POP_TOP\\\"\\n93:     overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\\\"NOP\\\")])\\n94: \\n95:     return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\\n96: \\n97: \\n98: def _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\\n99:     # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\\n100:     # on torch._dynamo.utils.\\n101:     global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\\n102:     try:\\n103:         dummy\\n104:     except:  # noqa: E722, B001\\n105:         __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\\n106:             stack_var_name\\n107:         )\\n108:         raise\\n109: \\n110: \\n111: @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\\n112: class ReenterWith:\\n113:     stack_index: int\\n114:     target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\\n115: \\n116:     def try_except_torch_function_mode(\\n117:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\\n118:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n119:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n120:         Codegen based off of:\\n121:         try:\\n122:             (rest)\\n123:         except:\\n124:             (restore previous tf mode stack)\\n125:             raise\\n126:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n127:         from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\\n128: \\n129:         setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n130:             _try_except_tf_mode_template,\\n131:             self.stack_index,\\n132:             varname_map={\\\"stack_var_name\\\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\\n133:         )\\n134:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\\n135: \\n136:         return setup_try_except\\n137: \\n138:     # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\\n139:     # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\\n140:     def try_finally(\\n141:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\\n142:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n143:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n144:         Codegen based off of:\\n145:         load args\\n146:         enter context\\n147:         try:\\n148:             (rest)\\n149:         finally:\\n150:             exit context\\n151:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n152:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\\n153:         load_args = []\\n154:         if self.target_values:\\n155:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\\n156:         ctx_name = unique_id(f\\\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\\\")\\n157:         if ctx_name not in code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"]:\\n158:             code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] += (ctx_name,)\\n159:         for name in [\\\"__enter__\\\", \\\"__exit__\\\"]:\\n160:             if name not in code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"]:\\n161:                 code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"] += (name,)\\n162: \\n163:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\\n164:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\\n165:         create_ctx.extend(\\n166:             [\\n167:                 *load_args,\\n168:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\\n169:                 create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=ctx_name),\\n170:             ]\\n171:         )\\n172: \\n173:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\\n174:             ctx.__enter__()\\n175:             try:\\n176:                 dummy\\n177:             finally:\\n178:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\\n179: \\n180:         setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n181:             _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\\\"ctx\\\": ctx_name}\\n182:         )\\n183:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\\n184:         return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\\n185: \\n186:     def __call__(\\n187:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\\n188:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\\n189:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n190:         Codegen based off of:\\n191:         with ctx(args):\\n192:             (rest)\\n193:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n194:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\\n195:         load_args = []\\n196:         if self.target_values:\\n197:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\\n198: \\n199:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\\n200:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\\n201:         create_ctx.extend(\\n202:             [\\n203:                 *load_args,\\n204:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\\n205:             ]\\n206:         )\\n207: \\n208:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\\n209:             with ctx:\\n210:                 dummy\\n211: \\n212:         setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\\n213:             _template, self.stack_index\\n214:         )\\n215:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\\n216: \\n217:         load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\\n218:             (\\n219:                 inst\\n220:                 for inst in setup_with\\n221:                 if inst.opname == \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\" and inst.argval == \\\"ctx\\\"\\n222:             ),\\n223:             None,\\n224:         )\\n225:         assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\\n226:         # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\\n227:         overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\\\"NOP\\\")])\\n228: \\n229:         # 3.11+ only\\n230:         push_exc_info_gen = (\\n231:             inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\"\\n232:         )\\n233:         push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\\n234:         # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\\n235:         assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\\n236: \\n237:         return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\\n238: \\n239: \\n240: @dataclasses.dataclass\\n241: class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\\n242:     code: types.CodeType\\n243:     instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\\n244:     # Python 3.11+ fields\\n245:     # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \\\"block\\\" consists\\n246:     # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\\n247: \\n248:     # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\\n249:     prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\\n250:         default_factory=list\\n251:     )\\n252:     # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\\n253:     block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\\n254:         default_factory=dict\\n255:     )\\n256: \\n257: \\n258: def _filter_iter(\\n259:     l1: Iterable[Any],\\n260:     l2: Iterable[Any],\\n261:     cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\\n262: ) -> list[Any]:\\n263:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n264:     Two-pointer conditional filter.\\n265:     e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\\n266:     returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\\n267:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n268:     it = iter(l2)\\n269:     res: list[Instruction] = []\\n270:     try:\\n271:         cur = next(it)\\n272:         for val in l1:\\n273:             if cond(val, cur):\\n274:                 res.append(val)\\n275:                 cur = next(it)\\n276:     except StopIteration:\\n277:         pass\\n278:     return res\\n279: \\n280: \\n281: def _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n282:     insts: list[Instruction] = []\\n283:     _initial_push_null(insts)\\n284:     insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\\n285:     insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\\n286:     return insts\\n287: \\n288: \\n289: class ContinueExecutionCache:\\n290:     cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\\n291:     generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\\n292: \\n293:     @classmethod\\n294:     def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\\n295:         if code not in cls.cache:\\n296:             cls.cache[code] = {}\\n297:         key = tuple(key)\\n298:         if key not in cls.cache[code]:\\n299:             cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\\n300:         return cls.cache[code][key]\\n301: \\n302:     @classmethod\\n303:     def generate(\\n304:         cls,\\n305:         code: types.CodeType,\\n306:         lineno: int,\\n307:         offset: int,\\n308:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\\n309:         nstack: int,\\n310:         argnames: tuple[str, ...],\\n311:         argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\\n312:         setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\\n313:         stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\\n314:         argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\\n315:         null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\\n316:         # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\\n317:         # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\\n318:         nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\\n319:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n320:         assert offset is not None\\n321:         assert not (\\n322:             code.co_flags\\n323:             & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\\n324:         )\\n325:         assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\\n326:         if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\\n327:             return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n328:                 code,\\n329:                 lineno,\\n330:                 offset,\\n331:                 setup_fn_target_offsets,\\n332:                 nstack,\\n333:                 argnames,\\n334:                 argnames_null,\\n335:                 setup_fns,\\n336:                 stack_ctx_vars,\\n337:                 argnames_ctx_vars,\\n338:                 null_idxes,\\n339:                 nested_code_objs,\\n340:             )\\n341: \\n342:         is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\\n343:         meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\\n344: \\n345:         def update(\\n346:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n347:         ) -> None:\\n348:             meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\\n349: \\n350:             args = [\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\", \\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"]\\n351:             args += [f\\\"___stack{i}\\\" for i in range(nstack)]\\n352:             args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\\n353:             freevars = tuple(code_options[\\\"co_cellvars\\\"] or []) + tuple(\\n354:                 code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"] or []\\n355:             )\\n356:             freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\\n357:             code_options[\\\"co_name\\\"] = (\\n358:                 f\\\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\\\"\\n359:             )\\n360:             if is_py311_plus:\\n361:                 qualified_path = code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"].rsplit(\\\".\\\", maxsplit=1)\\n362:                 if len(qualified_path) == 1:\\n363:                     code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"] = code_options[\\\"co_name\\\"]\\n364:                 else:\\n365:                     assert len(qualified_path) == 2\\n366:                     module_name, co_name = qualified_path\\n367:                     code_options[\\\"co_qualname\\\"] = (\\n368:                         f\\\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\\\"\\n369:                     )\\n370:             code_options[\\\"co_firstlineno\\\"] = lineno\\n371:             code_options[\\\"co_cellvars\\\"] = ()\\n372:             code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"] = freevars\\n373:             code_options[\\\"co_argcount\\\"] = len(args)\\n374:             code_options[\\\"co_posonlyargcount\\\"] = 0\\n375:             code_options[\\\"co_kwonlyargcount\\\"] = 0\\n376:             code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] = tuple(\\n377:                 args\\n378:                 + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\\n379:                 + [v for v in code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] if v not in args]\\n380:                 + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\\n381:             )\\n382:             code_options[\\\"co_flags\\\"] = code_options[\\\"co_flags\\\"] & ~(\\n383:                 CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\\n384:             )\\n385:             target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n386: \\n387:             prefix = []\\n388:             if is_py311_plus:\\n389:                 if freevars:\\n390:                     prefix.append(\\n391:                         create_instruction(\\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\", arg=len(freevars))\\n392:                     )\\n393:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"RESUME\\\", arg=0))\\n394: \\n395:             # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\\n396:             # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\\n397:             # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\\n398:             prefix.extend(\\n399:                 [\\n400:                     create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=True),\\n401:                     create_instruction(\\n402:                         \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n403:                     ),\\n404:                 ]\\n405:             )\\n406: \\n407:             cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\\n408:             hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\\n409:             hook_target_offsets = {\\n410:                 fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\\n411:                 for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\\n412:             }\\n413:             offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\\n414:             # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\\n415:             old_hook_target_remap = {}\\n416:             null_idxes_i = 0\\n417:             stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\\n418:             for i in range(nstack):\\n419:                 while (\\n420:                     null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\\n421:                     and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\\n422:                 ):\\n423:                     prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"))\\n424:                     null_idxes_i += 1\\n425:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=f\\\"___stack{i}\\\"))\\n426:                 if i in hooks:\\n427:                     hook = hooks.pop(i)\\n428:                     hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\\n429:                     prefix.extend(hook_insts)\\n430:                     if is_py311_plus:\\n431:                         hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\\n432:                         old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\\n433:                         meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\\n434:                         old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\\n435:                 if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\\n436:                     # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\\n437:                     # Load args for context variable and construct it\\n438:                     prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\\n439: \\n440:             if is_py311_plus:\\n441:                 # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\\n442:                 # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\\n443:                 meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\\n444:                     reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\\n445:                 )\\n446: \\n447:             assert not hooks\\n448: \\n449:             # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\\n450:             # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\\n451:             for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\\n452:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=name))\\n453:                 prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\\n454:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=name))\\n455: \\n456:             # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\\n457:             if argnames_null:\\n458:                 assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\\n459:                 for v in argnames_null:\\n460:                     assert v not in args\\n461:                     prefix.extend(\\n462:                         [\\n463:                             create_instruction(\\\"PUSH_NULL\\\"),\\n464:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=v),\\n465:                         ]\\n466:                     )\\n467: \\n468:             # Call nested resume function\\n469:             if nested_code_objs:\\n470:                 prefix.extend(\\n471:                     [\\n472:                         # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\\n473:                         *add_push_null(\\n474:                             [\\n475:                                 create_instruction(\\n476:                                     \\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"\\n477:                                 ),\\n478:                                 create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n479:                                 create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n480:                             ]\\n481:                         ),\\n482:                         # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\\n483:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n484:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n485:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n486:                         # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\\n487:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n488:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n489:                         create_instruction(\\\"BUILD_LIST\\\", arg=2),\\n490:                         # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\\n491:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n492:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n493:                         create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n494:                         # create [\\n495:                         #     __nested_resume_fns,\\n496:                         #     __nested_frame_values,\\n497:                         #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\\n498:                         # ]\\n499:                         create_instruction(\\\"LIST_EXTEND\\\", arg=1),\\n500:                         # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\\n501:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"),\\n502:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=-1),\\n503:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n504:                         # delete __nested values\\n505:                         create_instruction(\\\"DELETE_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_resume_fns\\\"),\\n506:                         create_instruction(\\n507:                             \\\"DELETE_FAST\\\", argval=\\\"__nested_frame_values\\\"\\n508:                         ),\\n509:                         # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\\n510:                         # in the nested resume\\n511:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=False),\\n512:                         create_instruction(\\n513:                             \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n514:                         ),\\n515:                         # finish the call\\n516:                         create_instruction(\\\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\\\", arg=0),\\n517:                     ]\\n518:                 )\\n519:             else:\\n520:                 # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\\n521:                 prefix.extend(\\n522:                     [\\n523:                         create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_CONST\\\", argval=False),\\n524:                         create_instruction(\\n525:                             \\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\\n526:                         ),\\n527:                     ]\\n528:                 )\\n529: \\n530:             prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\\n531: \\n532:             # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\\n533:             # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\\n534:             # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\\n535:             for inst in instructions:\\n536:                 if inst.offset == target.offset:\\n537:                     break\\n538:                 inst.starts_line = None\\n539:                 if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n540:                     inst.positions = None\\n541: \\n542:             if cleanup:\\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\\n545: \\n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\\n550:                     if (\\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\\n553:                     ):\\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\\n556:                         ]\\n557: \\n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\\n560: \\n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\\n563:         return new_code\\n564: \\n565:     @staticmethod\\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n567:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n568:         return [\\n569:             create_load_const(None),\\n570:             create_instruction(\\\"RAISE_VARARGS\\\", arg=1),\\n571:         ]\\n572: \\n573:     @classmethod\\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n575:         cls,\\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\\n577:         lineno: int,\\n578:         offset: int,\\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\\n580:         *args: Any,\\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n582:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: This module provides functionality for resuming Python execution at specific points in code,\n3: primarily used by PyTorch Dynamo for control flow handling and optimization. It implements\n4: bytecode transformation and execution state management to enable:\n5: \n6: - Resuming execution at arbitrary points in Python bytecode\n7: - Managing context managers and their state across execution boundaries\n8: - Transforming and generating new code objects with preserved execution state\n9: - Supporting Python 3.11+ exception handling and block management\n10: - Restoring torch function mode stacks and other execution context\n11: \n12: The module is critical for PyTorch Dynamo's ability to optimize code while preserving\n13: Python semantics and execution state.\n14: \"\"\"\n15: \n16: import copy\n17: import dataclasses\n18: import sys\n19: import types\n20: from collections.abc import Iterable\n21: from contextlib import AbstractContextManager\n22: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional\n23: \n24: from .bytecode_transformation import (\n25:     add_push_null,\n26:     bytecode_from_template,\n27:     create_call_function,\n28:     create_instruction,\n29:     create_jump_absolute,\n30:     create_load_const,\n31:     Instruction,\n32:     overwrite_instruction,\n33:     transform_code_object,\n34:     unique_id,\n35: )\n36: from .utils import ExactWeakKeyDictionary\n37: \n38: \n39: # taken from code.h in cpython\n40: CO_OPTIMIZED = 0x0001\n41: CO_NEWLOCALS = 0x0002\n42: CO_VARARGS = 0x0004\n43: CO_VARKEYWORDS = 0x0008\n44: CO_NESTED = 0x0010\n45: CO_GENERATOR = 0x0020\n46: CO_NOFREE = 0x0040\n47: CO_COROUTINE = 0x0080\n48: CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE = 0x0100\n49: CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR = 0x0200\n50: \n51: # trace_rules.py import this constant for consistency\n52: TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX = \"torch_dynamo_resume_in\"\n53: IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME = \"__is_tracing_resume_prologue\"\n54: \n55: \n56: def _initial_push_null(insts: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n57:     if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n58:         insts.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n59:         if sys.version_info < (3, 13):\n60:             insts.append(create_instruction(\"SWAP\", arg=2))\n61: \n62: \n63: # Generates bytecode from template and splits the code where LOAD_FAST dummy is present.\n64: def _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n65:     template: Callable[..., Any],\n66:     stack_index: int,\n67:     varname_map: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\n68: ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], list[Instruction]]:\n69:     template_code = bytecode_from_template(template, varname_map=varname_map)\n70:     template_code.append(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n71: \n72:     # adjust exception table entry depth\n73:     for inst in template_code:\n74:         if inst.exn_tab_entry:\n75:             inst.exn_tab_entry.depth += stack_index\n76: \n77:     # search for LOAD_FAST dummy and replace it with 2 NOPs (we can break up the bytecode between them)\n78:     dummy_idx, dummy_inst = next(\n79:         (\n80:             (i, inst)\n81:             for i, inst in enumerate(template_code)\n82:             if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"dummy\"\n83:         ),\n84:         (None, None),\n85:     )\n86:     assert dummy_idx is not None and dummy_inst is not None\n87: \n88:     # replace LOAD_FAST dummy with first NOP marking exception area\n89:     overwrite_instruction(dummy_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n90: \n91:     # POP_TOP follows LOAD_FAST dummy - replace with NOP marking end of exception area\n92:     assert template_code[dummy_idx + 1].opname == \"POP_TOP\"\n93:     overwrite_instruction(template_code[dummy_idx + 1], [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n94: \n95:     return template_code[: dummy_idx + 1], template_code[dummy_idx + 1 :]\n96: \n97: \n98: def _try_except_tf_mode_template(dummy: Any, stack_var_name: Any) -> None:\n99:     # NOTE: Make sure this name matches what is generated by symbolic_convert:import_source\n100:     # on torch._dynamo.utils.\n101:     global __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils\n102:     try:\n103:         dummy\n104:     except:  # noqa: E722, B001\n105:         __import_torch_dot__dynamo_dot_utils.set_torch_function_mode_stack(  # type: ignore[name-defined]\n106:             stack_var_name\n107:         )\n108:         raise\n109: \n110: \n111: @dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)\n112: class ReenterWith:\n113:     stack_index: int\n114:     target_values: Optional[tuple[Any, ...]] = None\n115: \n116:     def try_except_torch_function_mode(\n117:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n118:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\n119:         \"\"\"\n120:         Codegen based off of:\n121:         try:\n122:             (rest)\n123:         except:\n124:             (restore previous tf mode stack)\n125:             raise\n126:         \"\"\"\n127:         from .variables.torch_function import get_prev_stack_var_name\n128: \n129:         setup_try_except, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n130:             _try_except_tf_mode_template,\n131:             self.stack_index,\n132:             varname_map={\"stack_var_name\": get_prev_stack_var_name()},\n133:         )\n134:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n135: \n136:         return setup_try_except\n137: \n138:     # If we do not want to destroy the stack, we can do the same thing as a\n139:     # `SETUP_WITH` block, only that we store the context manager in a local_symbol\n140:     def try_finally(\n141:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n142:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\n143:         \"\"\"\n144:         Codegen based off of:\n145:         load args\n146:         enter context\n147:         try:\n148:             (rest)\n149:         finally:\n150:             exit context\n151:         \"\"\"\n152:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n153:         load_args = []\n154:         if self.target_values:\n155:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n156:         ctx_name = unique_id(f\"___context_manager_{self.stack_index}\")\n157:         if ctx_name not in code_options[\"co_varnames\"]:\n158:             code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (ctx_name,)\n159:         for name in [\"__enter__\", \"__exit__\"]:\n160:             if name not in code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n161:                 code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n162: \n163:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n164:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n165:         create_ctx.extend(\n166:             [\n167:                 *load_args,\n168:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n169:                 create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=ctx_name),\n170:             ]\n171:         )\n172: \n173:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n174:             ctx.__enter__()\n175:             try:\n176:                 dummy\n177:             finally:\n178:                 ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n179: \n180:         setup_try_finally, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n181:             _template, self.stack_index, varname_map={\"ctx\": ctx_name}\n182:         )\n183:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n184:         return create_ctx + setup_try_finally\n185: \n186:     def __call__(\n187:         self, code_options: dict[str, Any], cleanup: list[Instruction]\n188:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], Optional[Instruction]]:\n189:         \"\"\"\n190:         Codegen based off of:\n191:         with ctx(args):\n192:             (rest)\n193:         \"\"\"\n194:         # NOTE: we assume that TOS is a context manager CLASS!\n195:         load_args = []\n196:         if self.target_values:\n197:             load_args = [create_load_const(val) for val in self.target_values]\n198: \n199:         create_ctx: list[Instruction] = []\n200:         _initial_push_null(create_ctx)\n201:         create_ctx.extend(\n202:             [\n203:                 *load_args,\n204:                 *create_call_function(len(load_args), False),\n205:             ]\n206:         )\n207: \n208:         def _template(ctx: AbstractContextManager[Any], dummy: Any) -> None:\n209:             with ctx:\n210:                 dummy\n211: \n212:         setup_with, epilogue = _bytecode_from_template_with_split(\n213:             _template, self.stack_index\n214:         )\n215:         cleanup[:] = epilogue + cleanup\n216: \n217:         load_fast_ctx_inst = next(\n218:             (\n219:                 inst\n220:                 for inst in setup_with\n221:                 if inst.opname == \"LOAD_FAST\" and inst.argval == \"ctx\"\n222:             ),\n223:             None,\n224:         )\n225:         assert load_fast_ctx_inst is not None\n226:         # ctx already loaded on stack before the template - no need to LOAD_FAST\n227:         overwrite_instruction(load_fast_ctx_inst, [create_instruction(\"NOP\")])\n228: \n229:         # 3.11+ only\n230:         push_exc_info_gen = (\n231:             inst for inst in epilogue if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\"\n232:         )\n233:         push_exc_info_inst = next(push_exc_info_gen, None)\n234:         # expect only 1 PUSH_EXC_INFO in epilogue\n235:         assert next(push_exc_info_gen, None) is None\n236: \n237:         return create_ctx + setup_with, push_exc_info_inst\n238: \n239: \n240: @dataclasses.dataclass\n241: class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\n242:     code: types.CodeType\n243:     instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n244:     # Python 3.11+ fields\n245:     # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \"block\" consists\n246:     # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\n247: \n248:     # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n249:     prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\n250:         default_factory=list\n251:     )\n252:     # per-offset map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n253:     block_target_offset_remap: dict[int, dict[int, int]] = dataclasses.field(\n254:         default_factory=dict\n255:     )\n256: \n257: \n258: def _filter_iter(\n259:     l1: Iterable[Any],\n260:     l2: Iterable[Any],\n261:     cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\n262: ) -> list[Any]:\n263:     \"\"\"\n264:     Two-pointer conditional filter.\n265:     e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n266:     returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n267:     \"\"\"\n268:     it = iter(l2)\n269:     res: list[Instruction] = []\n270:     try:\n271:         cur = next(it)\n272:         for val in l1:\n273:             if cond(val, cur):\n274:                 res.append(val)\n275:                 cur = next(it)\n276:     except StopIteration:\n277:         pass\n278:     return res\n279: \n280: \n281: def _load_tuple_and_call(tup: tuple[Any, ...]) -> list[Instruction]:\n282:     insts: list[Instruction] = []\n283:     _initial_push_null(insts)\n284:     insts.extend(create_load_const(val) for val in tup)\n285:     insts.extend(create_call_function(len(tup), False))\n286:     return insts\n287: \n288: \n289: class ContinueExecutionCache:\n290:     cache = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n291:     generated_code_metadata = ExactWeakKeyDictionary()\n292: \n293:     @classmethod\n294:     def lookup(cls, code: types.CodeType, lineno: int, *key: Any) -> types.CodeType:\n295:         if code not in cls.cache:\n296:             cls.cache[code] = {}\n297:         key = tuple(key)\n298:         if key not in cls.cache[code]:\n299:             cls.cache[code][key] = cls.generate(code, lineno, *key)\n300:         return cls.cache[code][key]\n301: \n302:     @classmethod\n303:     def generate(\n304:         cls,\n305:         code: types.CodeType,\n306:         lineno: int,\n307:         offset: int,\n308:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],  # only used in Python 3.11+\n309:         nstack: int,\n310:         argnames: tuple[str, ...],\n311:         argnames_null: tuple[str, ...],\n312:         setup_fns: tuple[ReenterWith, ...],\n313:         stack_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n314:         argnames_ctx_vars: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]], ...],\n315:         null_idxes: tuple[int, ...],\n316:         # mainly used to ensure distinct code objects per stack trace,\n317:         # which prevents excessive recompilation of inner frames\n318:         nested_code_objs: tuple[types.CodeType],\n319:     ) -> types.CodeType:\n320:         assert offset is not None\n321:         assert not (\n322:             code.co_flags\n323:             & (CO_GENERATOR | CO_COROUTINE | CO_ITERABLE_COROUTINE | CO_ASYNC_GENERATOR)\n324:         )\n325:         assert code.co_flags & CO_OPTIMIZED\n326:         if code in ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata:\n327:             return cls.generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n328:                 code,\n329:                 lineno,\n330:                 offset,\n331:                 setup_fn_target_offsets,\n332:                 nstack,\n333:                 argnames,\n334:                 argnames_null,\n335:                 setup_fns,\n336:                 stack_ctx_vars,\n337:                 argnames_ctx_vars,\n338:                 null_idxes,\n339:                 nested_code_objs,\n340:             )\n341: \n342:         is_py311_plus = sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n343:         meta = ResumeFunctionMetadata(code)\n344: \n345:         def update(\n346:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n347:         ) -> None:\n348:             meta.instructions = copy.deepcopy(instructions)\n349: \n350:             args = [\"__nested_resume_fns\", \"__nested_frame_values\"]\n351:             args += [f\"___stack{i}\" for i in range(nstack)]\n352:             args.extend(v for v in argnames if v not in args)\n353:             freevars = tuple(code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] or []) + tuple(\n354:                 code_options[\"co_freevars\"] or []\n355:             )\n356:             freevars = tuple(sorted(freevars))\n357:             code_options[\"co_name\"] = (\n358:                 f\"{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{code_options['co_name']}_at_{lineno}\"\n359:             )\n360:             if is_py311_plus:\n361:                 qualified_path = code_options[\"co_qualname\"].rsplit(\".\", maxsplit=1)\n362:                 if len(qualified_path) == 1:\n363:                     code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = code_options[\"co_name\"]\n364:                 else:\n365:                     assert len(qualified_path) == 2\n366:                     module_name, co_name = qualified_path\n367:                     code_options[\"co_qualname\"] = (\n368:                         f\"{module_name}.{TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX}_{co_name}_at_{lineno}\"\n369:                     )\n370:             code_options[\"co_firstlineno\"] = lineno\n371:             code_options[\"co_cellvars\"] = ()\n372:             code_options[\"co_freevars\"] = freevars\n373:             code_options[\"co_argcount\"] = len(args)\n374:             code_options[\"co_posonlyargcount\"] = 0\n375:             code_options[\"co_kwonlyargcount\"] = 0\n376:             code_options[\"co_varnames\"] = tuple(\n377:                 args\n378:                 + [v for v in argnames_null if v not in args]\n379:                 + [v for v in code_options[\"co_varnames\"] if v not in args]\n380:                 + [IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME]\n381:             )\n382:             code_options[\"co_flags\"] = code_options[\"co_flags\"] & ~(\n383:                 CO_VARARGS | CO_VARKEYWORDS\n384:             )\n385:             target = next(i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n386: \n387:             prefix = []\n388:             if is_py311_plus:\n389:                 if freevars:\n390:                     prefix.append(\n391:                         create_instruction(\"COPY_FREE_VARS\", arg=len(freevars))\n392:                     )\n393:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"RESUME\", arg=0))\n394: \n395:             # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue to prevent graph breaks.\n396:             # This doesn't really do anything at runtime, but dynamo will trace this\n397:             # and will know that we're in a resume function prologue.\n398:             prefix.extend(\n399:                 [\n400:                     create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=True),\n401:                     create_instruction(\n402:                         \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n403:                     ),\n404:                 ]\n405:             )\n406: \n407:             cleanup: list[Instruction] = []\n408:             hooks = {fn.stack_index: fn for fn in setup_fns}\n409:             hook_target_offsets = {\n410:                 fn.stack_index: setup_fn_target_offsets[i]\n411:                 for i, fn in enumerate(setup_fns)\n412:             }\n413:             offset_to_inst = {inst.offset: inst for inst in instructions}\n414:             # map old hook targets to new targets generated by the hook\n415:             old_hook_target_remap = {}\n416:             null_idxes_i = 0\n417:             stack_ctx_vars_d = dict(stack_ctx_vars)  # type: ignore[var-annotated,arg-type]\n418:             for i in range(nstack):\n419:                 while (\n420:                     null_idxes_i < len(null_idxes)\n421:                     and null_idxes[null_idxes_i] == i + null_idxes_i\n422:                 ):\n423:                     prefix.append(create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"))\n424:                     null_idxes_i += 1\n425:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=f\"___stack{i}\"))\n426:                 if i in hooks:\n427:                     hook = hooks.pop(i)\n428:                     hook_insts, exn_target = hook(code_options, cleanup)\n429:                     prefix.extend(hook_insts)\n430:                     if is_py311_plus:\n431:                         hook_target_offset = hook_target_offsets.pop(i)\n432:                         old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n433:                         meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n434:                         old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n435:                 if i in stack_ctx_vars_d:\n436:                     # NOTE: we assume that current stack var is a context manager CLASS!\n437:                     # Load args for context variable and construct it\n438:                     prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(stack_ctx_vars_d[i]))\n439: \n440:             if is_py311_plus:\n441:                 # reverse the mapping since targets of later/nested contexts are inserted\n442:                 # into the mapping later, but show up earlier in the prefix.\n443:                 meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = list(\n444:                     reversed(meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap)\n445:                 )\n446: \n447:             assert not hooks\n448: \n449:             # NOTE: we assume that local var is a context manager CLASS!\n450:             # initialize inactive context vars in argnames\n451:             for name, vals in argnames_ctx_vars:\n452:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=name))\n453:                 prefix.extend(_load_tuple_and_call(vals))\n454:                 prefix.append(create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=name))\n455: \n456:             # 3.12+: store NULL into variables that were NULL\n457:             if argnames_null:\n458:                 assert sys.version_info >= (3, 12)\n459:                 for v in argnames_null:\n460:                     assert v not in args\n461:                     prefix.extend(\n462:                         [\n463:                             create_instruction(\"PUSH_NULL\"),\n464:                             create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=v),\n465:                         ]\n466:                     )\n467: \n468:             # Call nested resume function\n469:             if nested_code_objs:\n470:                 prefix.extend(\n471:                     [\n472:                         # set up __nested_resume_fns[-1] call\n473:                         *add_push_null(\n474:                             [\n475:                                 create_instruction(\n476:                                     \"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"\n477:                                 ),\n478:                                 create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n479:                                 create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n480:                             ]\n481:                         ),\n482:                         # del __nested_resume_fns[-1]\n483:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n484:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n485:                         create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n486:                         # load [__nested_resume_fns, __nested_frame_values]\n487:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n488:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n489:                         create_instruction(\"BUILD_LIST\", arg=2),\n490:                         # load __nested_frame_values[-1]\n491:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n492:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n493:                         create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n494:                         # create [\n495:                         #     __nested_resume_fns,\n496:                         #     __nested_frame_values,\n497:                         #     *__nested_frame_values[-1],\n498:                         # ]\n499:                         create_instruction(\"LIST_EXTEND\", arg=1),\n500:                         # del __nested_frame_values[-1]\n501:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"),\n502:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=-1),\n503:                         create_instruction(\"DELETE_SUBSCR\"),\n504:                         # delete __nested values\n505:                         create_instruction(\"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_resume_fns\"),\n506:                         create_instruction(\n507:                             \"DELETE_FAST\", argval=\"__nested_frame_values\"\n508:                         ),\n509:                         # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks\n510:                         # in the nested resume\n511:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n512:                         create_instruction(\n513:                             \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n514:                         ),\n515:                         # finish the call\n516:                         create_instruction(\"CALL_FUNCTION_EX\", arg=0),\n517:                     ]\n518:                 )\n519:             else:\n520:                 # Set is_tracing_resume_prologue back to allow graph breaks after the jump\n521:                 prefix.extend(\n522:                     [\n523:                         create_instruction(\"LOAD_CONST\", argval=False),\n524:                         create_instruction(\n525:                             \"STORE_FAST\", argval=IS_TRACING_RESUME_PROLOGUE_VARNAME\n526:                         ),\n527:                     ]\n528:                 )\n529: \n530:             prefix.append(create_jump_absolute(target))\n531: \n532:             # because the line number table monotonically increases from co_firstlineno\n533:             # remove starts_line for any instructions before the graph break instruction\n534:             # this will ensure the instructions after the break have the correct line numbers\n535:             for inst in instructions:\n536:                 if inst.offset == target.offset:\n537:                     break\n538:                 inst.starts_line = None\n539:                 if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n540:                     inst.positions = None\n541: \n542:             if cleanup:\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n545: \n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\n550:                     if (\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n553:                     ):\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n556:                         ]\n557: \n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n560: \n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n563:         return new_code\n564: \n565:     @staticmethod\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n567:         \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n568:         return [\n569:             create_load_const(None),\n570:             create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n571:         ]\n572: \n573:     @classmethod\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n575:         cls,\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\n577:         lineno: int,\n578:         offset: int,\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n580:         *args: Any,\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\n582:         \"\"\"\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n587:         resume functions).\n588:         \"\"\"\n589: \n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n591:             code\n592:         ]\n593:         new_offset = -1\n594: \n595:         def find_new_offset(\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n597:         ) -> None:\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n601:             (new_target,) = (\n602:                 i2\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n604:                 if i1 is target\n605:             )\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\n609: \n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\n612: \n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n620:                     new_offset\n621:                 ] = {}\n622: \n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n625:                 ) -> None:\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n630:                     # replaced.\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n635:                         ):\n636:                             break\n637:                         if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n639: \n640:                     # offsets into prefix\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n643:                     ):\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n645: \n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n649:                     )\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n653:                     )\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n656:                     )\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n659:                         targets,\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n661:                     )\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n664: \n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n666: \n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n671:             )\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n674:         )\n675: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py"
  }
}

map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\\n+    prefix_block_target_offset_remap: List[int] = None\\n+    # map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\\n@@ -238,1 +356,3 @@ class ContinueExecutionCache:\\n+        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n+            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = []\\n \\n@@ -288,0 +418,3 @@ class ContinueExecutionCache:\\n+                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\\n+                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\\n+                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\\n\",\"matchRanges\":[{\"endCharacter\":37,\"endLine\":3,\"startCharacter\":5,\"startLine\":3},{\"endCharacter\":50,\"endLine\":7,\"startCharacter\":18,\"startLine\":7},{\"endCharacter\":62,\"endLine\":11,\"startCharacter\":30,\"startLine\":11}],\"message\":\"[dynamo 3.11] implement 3.11 exceptiontable (#96511)\\n\\nSummary of changes:\\n- Add CPython exceptiontable parsing/assembling functions in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py, based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt.\\n- Add optional `exn_tab_entry` field to dynamo `Instruction`s in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py in order to virtualize exception table entries (start, end, target instructions).\\n- Add checks guarding against duplicate instructions in dynamo, so that jump/exceptiontable targets are unambiguous. See `get_indexof` in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_analysis.py. Ensure that bytecode generation throughout dynamo does not generate duplicate instructions.\\n- Allow dynamo bytecode generation logic to generate nested exception table entries for developer convenience. CPython expects entries to not overlap, so we flatten nested entries during assembly in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py:compute_exception_table.\\n- Simulate th...             "startLine": 8
          }
        ],
        "message": "[dynamo] Enable typechecking for resume_execution.py (#112564)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/112564\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/eellison\nghstack dependencies: #112561, #112562, #112563",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/fe41a9ce087c595c248c1247fe3b8303d3ff29c7"
      },
      {
        "authorName": "William Wen",
        "commit": "88c8c2b71b7b2dac0b346dfadc15e96eacaa7755",
        "diffHunk": "torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py\n@@ -200,0 +287,3 @@ class ReenterWith:\n+    # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n+    prefix_block_target_offset_remap: List[int] = None\n+    # map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n@@ -238,1 +356,3 @@ class ContinueExecutionCache:\n+        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n+            meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap = []\n \n@@ -288,0 +418,3 @@ class ContinueExecutionCache:\n+                        old_hook_target = offset_to_inst[hook_target_offset]\n+                        meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap.append(hook_target_offset)\n+                        old_hook_target_remap[old_hook_target] = exn_target\n",
        "matchRanges": [
          {
            "endCharacter": 37,
            "endLine": 3,
            "startCharacter": 5,
            "startLine": 3
          },

            "endCharacter": 50,
            "endLine": 7,
            "startCharacter": 18,
            "startLine": 7
          },
          {
            "endCharacter": 62,
            "endLine": 11,
            "startCharacter": 30,
            "startLine": 11
          }
        ],
        "message": "[dynamo 3.11] implement 3.11 exceptiontable (#96511)\n\nSummary of changes:\n- Add CPython exceptiontable parsing/assembling functions in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py, based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt.\n- Add optional `exn_tab_entry` field to dynamo `Instruction`s in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py in order to virtualize exception table entries (start, end, target instructions).\n- Add checks guarding against duplicate instructions in dynamo, so that jump/exceptiontable targets are unambiguous. See `get_indexof` in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_analysis.py. Ensure that bytecode generation throughout dynamo does not generate duplicate instructions.\n- Allow dynamo bytecode generation logic to generate nested exception table entries for developer convenience. CPython expects entries to not overlap, so we flatten nested entries during assembly in torch/_dynamo/bytecode_transformation.py:compute_exception_table.\n- Simulate th... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/88c8c2b71b7b2dac0b346dfadc15e96eacaa7755"
      }
    ],
    "totalCount": 3
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"2068:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n2069:             exn_tab_entry = self.current_instruction.exn_tab_entry\\n2070:             if exn_tab_entry:\\n2071:                 # Implementation is based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt\\n2072: \\n2073:                 # 1) pop values from the stack until it matches the stack depth\\n2074:                 # for the handler\\n2075:                 while len(self.stack) > exn_tab_entry.depth:\\n2076:                     self.pop()\\n2077: \\n2078:                 # 2) if 'lasti' is true, then push the offset that the exception was raised at\\n2079:                 if exn_tab_entry.lasti:\\n2080:                     self.push(\\n2081:                         variables.ConstantVariable(self.current_instruction.offset)\\n2082:                     )\\n2083: \\n2084:                 # 3) push the exception to the stack\\n2085:                 self.push(self.exn_vt_stack.get_current_exception())\\n2086: \\n2087:                 # 4) jump to the handler\\n2088:                 self.jump(exn_tab_entry)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2089:             else:\\n2090:                 # No handler found. Bubble the exception to the parent\\n2091:                 # instruction translator. We use special exception for this.\\n2092:                 self.stack.clear()\\n2093:                 if type(self) is InstructionTranslator:\\n2094:                     bubble_exception_to_interpreter()\\n2095:                 raise raised_exception\\n2096:         else:\\n2097:             if len(self.block_stack):\\n2098:                 # base implementation - https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L4455\\n2099: \\n2100:                 block_stack_entry = self.block_stack.pop()\\n2101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2068-2100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2068:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n2069:             exn_tab_entry = self.current_instruction.exn_tab_entry\n2070:             if exn_tab_entry:\n2071:                 # Implementation is based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt\n2072: \n2073:                 # 1) pop values from the stack until it matches the stack depth\n2074:                 # for the handler\n2075:                 while len(self.stack) > exn_tab_entry.depth:\n2076:                     self.pop()\n2077: \n2078:                 # 2) if 'lasti' is true, then push the offset that the exception was raised at\n2079:                 if exn_tab_entry.lasti:\n2080:                     self.push(\n2081:                         variables.ConstantVariable(self.current_instruction.offset)\n2082:                     )\n2083: \n2084:                 # 3) push the exception to the stack\n2085:                 self.push(self.exn_vt_stack.get_current_exception())\n2086: \n2087:                 # 4) jump to the handler\n2088:                 self.jump(exn_tab_entry)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2089:             else:\n2090:                 # No handler found. Bubble the exception to the parent\n2091:                 # instruction translator. We use special exception for this.\n2092:                 self.stack.clear()\n2093:                 if type(self) is InstructionTranslator:\n2094:                     bubble_exception_to_interpreter()\n2095:                 raise raised_exception\n2096:         else:\n2097:             if len(self.block_stack):\n2098:                 # base implementation - https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L4455\n2099: \n2100:                 block_stack_entry = self.block_stack.pop()\n2101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2068-2100"

Replace InstructionTranslator.checkpoint with speculate/restart (#112902)\\n\\nIn my work on making guards installed eagerly (look up the stack), I found that our checkpoint/restore mechanism is very broken.  There is lots of state (especially in shape_env) which we don't checkpoint and restore properly.  We also have lots of mutable state on variable trackers already which is not checkpointed/restored.  (See other PRs in this stack for some spot fixes.)\\n\\nSince we wanted to get rid of this anyway for making VariableTracker mutable, I figured I would just switch to restarting analysis.\\n\\nFor other usages of copy_graphstate/restore_graphstate:\\n1) Many usages were pointless and not needed, these are removed in PRs below this.\\n2) Some other usage (similar to this one) is removed in PRs above this.\\n3) The tricky one I am not handling is higher_order_ops, which uses checkpoint/restore a lot.         "message": "[dynamo] Replace InstructionTranslator.checkpoint with speculate/restart (#112902)\n\nIn my work on making guards installed eagerly (look up the stack), I found that our checkpoint/restore mechanism is very broken.  There is lots of state (especially in shape_env) which we don't checkpoint and restore properly.  We also have lots of mutable state on variable trackers already which is not checkpointed/restored.  (See other PRs in this stack for some spot fixes.)\n\nSince we wanted to get rid of this anyway for making VariableTracker mutable, I figured I would just switch to restarting analysis.\n\nFor other usages of copy_graphstate/restore_graphstate:\n1) Many usages were pointless and not needed, these are removed in PRs below this.\n2) Some other usage (similar to this one) is removed in PRs above this.\n3) The tricky one I am not handling is higher_order_ops, which uses checkpoint/restore a lot.    There might be some cases there where this speculate/restart trick won't work.\n\nPull Request r... [truncated]",

      }
    ],
    "totalCount": 2
  }
