{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"540:                     inst.positions = None\\n541: \\n542:             if cleanup:\\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\\n545: \\n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\\n550:                     if (\\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\\n553:                     ):\\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\\n556:                         ]\\n557: \\n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\\n560: \\n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\\n563:         return new_code\\n564: \\n565:     @staticmethod\\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\\n567:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n568:         return [\\n569:             create_load_const(None),\\n570:             create_instruction(\\\"RAISE_VARARGS\\\", arg=1),\\n571:         ]\\n572: \\n573:     @classmethod\\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\\n575:         cls,\\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\\n577:         lineno: int,\\n578:         offset: int,\\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\\n580:         *args: Any,\\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\\n582:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n587:         resume functions).\\n588:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n589: \\n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n591:             code\\n592:         ]\\n593:         new_offset = -1\\n594: \\n595:         def find_new_offset(\\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n597:         ) -> None:\\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n601:             (new_target,) = (\\n602:                 i2\\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n604:                 if i1 is target\\n605:             )\\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n609: \\n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\\n612: \\n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\\n620:                     new_offset\\n621:                 ] = {}\\n622: \\n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n625:                 ) -> None:\\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n630:                     # replaced.\\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n635:                         ):\\n636:                             break\\n637:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n639: \\n640:                     # offsets into prefix\\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n643:                     ):\\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\\n645: \\n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n649:                     )\\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n653:                     )\\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n656:                     )\\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n659:                         targets,\\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n661:                     )\\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n664: \\n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n666: \\n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n671:             )\\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n674:         )\\n675: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL540-675\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "540:                     inst.positions = None\n541: \n542:             if cleanup:\n543:                 prefix.extend(cleanup)\n544:                 prefix.extend(cls.unreachable_codes(code_options))\n545: \n546:             # remap original instructions' exception table entries\n547:             if old_hook_target_remap:\n548:                 assert is_py311_plus\n549:                 for inst in instructions:\n550:                     if (\n551:                         inst.exn_tab_entry\n552:                         and inst.exn_tab_entry.target in old_hook_target_remap\n553:                     ):\n554:                         inst.exn_tab_entry.target = old_hook_target_remap[  # type: ignore[assignment]\n555:                             inst.exn_tab_entry.target\n556:                         ]\n557: \n558:             # TODO(jansel): add dead code elimination here\n559:             instructions[:] = prefix + instructions\n560: \n561:         new_code, _ = transform_code_object(code, update)\n562:         ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[new_code] = meta\n563:         return new_code\n564: \n565:     @staticmethod\n566:     def unreachable_codes(code_options: dict[str, Any]) -> list[Instruction]:\n567:         \"\"\"Codegen a `raise None` to make analysis work for unreachable code\"\"\"\n568:         return [\n569:             create_load_const(None),\n570:             create_instruction(\"RAISE_VARARGS\", arg=1),\n571:         ]\n572: \n573:     @classmethod\n574:     def generate_based_on_original_code_object(\n575:         cls,\n576:         code: types.CodeType,\n577:         lineno: int,\n578:         offset: int,\n579:         setup_fn_target_offsets: tuple[int, ...],\n580:         *args: Any,\n581:     ) -> types.CodeType:\n582:         \"\"\"\n583:         This handles the case of generating a resume into code generated\n584:         to resume something else.  We want to always generate starting\n585:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\n586:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\n587:         resume functions).\n588:         \"\"\"\n589: \n590:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\n591:             code\n592:         ]\n593:         new_offset = -1\n594: \n595:         def find_new_offset(\n596:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n597:         ) -> None:\n598:             nonlocal new_offset\n599:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\n600:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\n601:             (new_target,) = (\n602:                 i2\n603:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\n604:                 if i1 is target\n605:             )\n606:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\n607:             assert new_target.offset is not None\n608:             new_offset = new_target.offset\n609: \n610:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\n611:         assert new_offset >= 0\n612: \n613:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n614:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\n615:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\n616:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\n617:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\n618:             if new_offset not in meta.block_target_offset_remap:\n619:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap[\n620:                     new_offset\n621:                 ] = {}\n622: \n623:                 def remap_block_offsets(\n624:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\n625:                 ) -> None:\n626:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\n627:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\n628:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\n629:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\n630:                     # replaced.\n631:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\n632:                     for inst in instructions:\n633:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\n634:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n635:                         ):\n636:                             break\n637:                         if inst.opname == \"PUSH_EXC_INFO\":\n638:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\n639: \n640:                     # offsets into prefix\n641:                     for inst, o in zip(\n642:                         prefix_blocks, meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\n643:                     ):\n644:                         block_target_offset_remap[cast(int, inst.offset)] = o\n645: \n646:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\n647:                     old_start_offset = (\n648:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\n649:                     )\n650:                     # offsets into old bytecode\n651:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\n652:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\n653:                     )\n654:                     targets = _filter_iter(\n655:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\n656:                     )\n657:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\n658:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\n659:                         targets,\n660:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\n661:                     )\n662:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\n663:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\n664: \n665:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\n666: \n667:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n668:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n669:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[new_offset][n]\n670:                 for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n671:             )\n672:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n673:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n674:         )\n675: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL540-675"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"2060:                 context=f\\\"raised exception {curr_exc.python_type_name()}({curr_exc.args})\\\",  # type: ignore[union-attr]\\n2061:                 explanation=observed_exn_gb_explanation,\\n2062:                 hints=[\\n2063:                     *graph_break_hints.USER_ERROR,\\n2064:                     *graph_break_hints.SUPPORTABLE,\\n2065:                 ],\\n2066:             )\\n2067: \\n2068:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n2069:             exn_tab_entry = self.current_instruction.exn_tab_entry\\n2070:             if exn_tab_entry:\\n2071:                 # Implementation is based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt\\n2072: \\n2073:                 # 1) pop values from the stack until it matches the stack depth\\n2074:                 # for the handler\\n2075:                 while len(self.stack) > exn_tab_entry.depth:\\n2076:                     self.pop()\\n2077: \\n2078:                 # 2) if 'lasti' is true, then push the offset that the exception was raised at\\n2079:                 if exn_tab_entry.lasti:\\n2080:                     self.push(\\n2081:                         variables.ConstantVariable(self.current_instruction.offset)\\n2082:                     )\\n2083: \\n2084:                 # 3) push the exception to the stack\\n2085:                 self.push(self.exn_vt_stack.get_current_exception())\\n2086: \\n2087:                 # 4) jump to the handler\\n2088:                 self.jump(exn_tab_entry)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2089:             else:\\n2090:                 # No handler found. Bubble the exception to the parent\\n2091:                 # instruction translator. We use special exception for this.\\n2092:                 self.stack.clear()\\n2093:                 if type(self) is InstructionTranslator:\\n2094:                     bubble_exception_to_interpreter()\\n2095:                 raise raised_exception\\n2096:         else:\\n2097:             if len(self.block_stack):\\n2098:                 # base implementation - https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L4455\\n2099: \\n2100:                 block_stack_entry = self.block_stack.pop()\\n2101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py%3FL2060-2100\"}",

    "content": "2060:                 context=f\"raised exception {curr_exc.python_type_name()}({curr_exc.args})\",  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n2061:                 explanation=observed_exn_gb_explanation,\n2062:                 hints=[\n2063:                     *graph_break_hints.USER_ERROR,\n2064:                     *graph_break_hints.SUPPORTABLE,\n2065:                 ],\n2066:             )\n2067: \n2068:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n2069:             exn_tab_entry = self.current_instruction.exn_tab_entry\n2070:             if exn_tab_entry:\n2071:                 # Implementation is based on https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Objects/exception_handling_notes.txt\n2072: \n2073:                 # 1) pop values from the stack until it matches the stack depth\n2074:                 # for the handler\n2075:                 while len(self.stack) > exn_tab_entry.depth:\n2076:                     self.pop()\n2077: \n2078:                 # 2) if 'lasti' is true, then push the offset that the exception was raised at\n2079:                 if exn_tab_entry.lasti:\n2080:                     self.push(\n2081:                         variables.ConstantVariable(self.current_instruction.offset)\n2082:                     )\n2083: \n2084:                 # 3) push the exception to the stack\n2085:                 self.push(self.exn_vt_stack.get_current_exception())\n2086: \n2087:                 # 4) jump to the handler\n2088:                 self.jump(exn_tab_entry)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2089:             else:\n2090:                 # No handler found. Bubble the exception to the parent\n2091:                 # instruction translator. wrap torch.profiler.* as NullContextVariable and do nothing\\n173:         def fn(x):\\n174:             y = x**2\\n175:             with torch.profiler.profile():\\n176:                 y = y + 2\\n177:                 with torch.profiler.record_function(\\\"my_function\\\"):\\n178:                     z = y**3\\n179:                     z.tolist()  # graph break\\n180:                     z = z + 1\\n181:             return z\\n182: \\n183:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\\n184:         ref = fn(x)\\n185:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n186:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n187:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n188:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n189:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n190: \\n191:     def test_autograd_profiler(self):\\n192:         # graph break\\n200:                     z = z + 1\\n201:             return z\\n202: \\n203:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\\n204:         ref = fn(x)\\n205:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n206:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n207:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n208:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n209:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n210: \\n211:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \\\"requires cuda\\\")\\n212:     def test_cuda_stream_context_manager1(self):\\n213:         def fn(x):\\n214:             s = torch.cuda.Stream()\\n215:             x = torch.mul(x, 5)\\n216:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\\n217:             current_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()\\n218:             s.wait_stream(current_stream)\\n219:             with torch.cuda.stream(s):\\n220:                 x = torch.relu(x)\\n221:             current_stream.wait_stream(s)\\n222:             x = torch.add(x, 1)\\n223:             x = torch.cos(x)\\n224:             return x\\n225: \\n226:         x = torch.randn((2, 2), device=\\\"cuda\\\")\\n227:         ref = fn(x)\\n228:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n229:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts, fullgraph=True)\\n230:         res = opt_fn(x)\\n231:         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\\n232:         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 1)\\n233:         self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 12)\\n234: \\n235:     @unittest.expectedFailure  # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/118204\\n236:     @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \\\"requires cuda\\\")\\n237:     def test_cuda_stream_across_graph_break(self):\\n238:         def fn(x):\\n239:             s = torch.cuda.Stream()\\n240:             x = torch.mul(x, 5)\\n241:             x = torch.add(x, 2)\\n242: \\n243:             print(\\\"foo\\\")\\n244: \\n245:             tcs = torch.cuda.stream(s)\\n246:             current_stream = torch.cuda.current_stream()\\n247:             s.wait_stream(current_stream)\\n248: \\n249:             with tcs:\\n250:                 x = torch.relu(x)\\n251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py%3FL140-250\"}",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1855:         def reversible(x):\\n1856:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\\n1858: \\n1859:         def fn(x):\\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\\n1867:                     return x.grad\\n1868: \\n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1870:         x.grad = None\\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\\n1873: \\n1874:         x.grad = None\\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\\n1879: \\n1880:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\\n1881:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\\n1882:         def fn(x):\\n1883:             with torch.no_grad():\\n1884:                 y = x * 3\\n1885:                 print(\\\"Break\\\")\\n1886:                 z = x + 2\\n1887:             return y, z\\n1888: \\n1889:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1890:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1891:         y, z = opt_fn(x)\\n1892:         self.assertFalse(y.requires_grad)\\n1893:         self.assertFalse(z.requires_grad)\\n1894: \\n1895:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\\n1896:         # tests that we correctly bail out of __setattr__ calls\\n1897: \\n1898:         # TODO: does not ensure ABC classes are correctly inferred as ClassVariables\\n1899:         # (doesn't test the fix for 'super()')\\n1900: \\n1901:         class BaseModule(torch.nn.Module, ABC):\\n1902:             def blah(self, x):\\n1903:                 return x + 1\\n1904: \\n1905:         class Derived(BaseModule):\\n1906:             def __setattr__(self, name, value) -> None:\\n1907:                 super().__setattr__(name, value)\\n1908: \\n1909:             def forward(self, x):\\n1910:                 # expect a graph break on __setattr__\\n1911:                 self.foo = 0\\n1912:                 return self.blah(x)\\n1913: \\n1914:             def blah(self, x):\\n1915:                 return super().blah(x)\\n1916: \\n1917:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\\n1918:         mod = Derived()\\n1919:         opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n1920:         opt_mod(x)\\n1921: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1855-1920\"}",

    "content": "1855:         def reversible(x):\n1856:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n1857:             return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n1858: \n1859:         def fn(x):\n1860:             # nested context manager failed previously\n1861:             with torch.no_grad():\n1862:                 with torch.enable_grad():\n1863:                     a = torch.sin(x)\n1864:                     b = reversible(a)\n1865:                     c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n1866:                     c.sum().backward()\n1867:                     return x.grad\n1868: \n1869:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1870:         x.grad = None\n1871:         with torch.no_grad():\n1872:             ref = fn(x)\n1873: \n1874:         x.grad = None\n1875:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1876:         with torch.no_grad():\n1877:             res = opt_fn(x)\n1878:         self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n1879: \n1880:     # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\n1881:     def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\n1882:         def fn(x):\n1883:             with torch.no_grad():\n1884:                 y = x * 3\n1885:                 print(\"Break\")\n1886:                 z = x + 2\n1887:             return y, z\n1888: \n1889:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1890:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n1891:         y, z = opt_fn(x)\n1892:         self.assertFalse(y.requires_grad)\n1893:         self.assertFalse(z.requires_grad)\n1894: \n1895:     def test_abc_setattr(self):\n1896:         # tests that we correctly bail out of __setattr__ calls\n1897: \n1898:         # TODO: does not ensure ABC classes are correctly inferred as ClassVariables\n1899:         # (doesn't test the fix for 'super()')\n1900: \n1901:         class BaseModule(torch.nn.Module, ABC):\n1902:             def blah(self, x):\n1903:                 return x + 1\n1904: \n1905:         class Derived(BaseModule):\n1906:             def __setattr__(self, name, value) -> None:\n1907:                 super().__setattr__(name, value)\n1908: \n1909:             def forward(self, x):\n1910:                 # expect a graph break on __setattr__\n1911:                 self.foo = 0\n1912:                 return self.blah(x)\n1913: \n1914:             def blah(self, x):\n1915:                 return super().blah(x)\n1916: \n1917:         x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n1918:         mod = Derived()\n1919:         opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\")\n1920:         opt_mod(x)\n1921: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@cbe1a35dbdfdc9b7490212bdd812f3e2f4ce9e2d/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL1855-1920"
  }

for use in test_side_effects_globals\\n global1, global2, global3, global4 = (torch.zeros(3),) * 4\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":68},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":21,\"startLine\":68},\"section\":\"for test in tests:\"},{\"body\":\"         self.assertEqual(ref, res)\\n         self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 2)\\n \\n-    @unittest.expectedFailure\\n-    def test_ctx_manager(self):\\n-        global global_val\\n-        global_val = 0\\n+    def test_supported_ctx_manager(self):\\n+        global check, check_disabled, f1, f2, f3\\n \\n         @torch._dynamo.disable\\n-        def f1():\\n-            return global_val\\n+        def check_disabled(value):\\n+            assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == value\\n \\n-        def f2(x2):\\n-            with CustomizedCtxManager(8):\\n-                x2 = x2 + (1 << 4)\\n-                x2 = x2 + f1()  # 15\\n-                x2 = x2 + (1 << 5)\\n-            x2 = x2 << 2\\n-            x2 = x2 + global_val  # 3\\n-            with CustomizedCtxManager(4):\\n-                x2 = x2 << 4\\n-                x2 = x2 + f1()  #   "pattern": "test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"limitHit\":false,\"query\":\"type:diff content:test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d count:20\",\"results\":[{\"authorName\":\"Yanbo Liang\",\"commit\":\"9be9592f28023eff3caf87e13c1a89901c1819cf\",\"diffHunk\":\"/dev/null test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py\\n@@ -0,0 +79,3 @@ \\n+\\n+    def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\\n+        def fn(x):\\ntest/dynamo/test_misc.py test/dynamo/test_misc.py\\n@@ -936,3 +877,0 @@ class MiscTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\\n-\\n-    def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\\n-        def fn(x):\\n\",\"matchRanges\":[{\"endCharacter\":42,\"endLine\":3,\"startCharacter\":9,\"startLine\":3},{\"endCharacter\":42,\"endLine\":8,\"startCharacter\":9,\"startLine\":8}],\"message\":\"[Dynamo] Code refactor: move context managers out of misc.py (#97958)\\n\\nmisc.py and test_misc.py is too big, moving context managers to context.py and test_context.py.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/97958\\nApproved by: https://github.com/ezyang, https://github.com/anijain2305, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/voznesenskym\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/9be9592f28023eff3caf87e13c1a89901c1819cf\"},{\"authorName\":\"jon-chuang\",\"commit\":\"7a192cc51c7172860efd35413acf9a8b9aafd2c9\",\"diffHunk\":\"test/dynamo/test_misc.py test/dynamo/test_misc.py\\n@@ -912,1 +912,3 @@ class MiscTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\\n \\n+    def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\\n+        def fn(x):\\n\",\"matchRanges\":[{\"endCharacter\":42,\"endLine\":3,\"startCharacter\":9,\"startLine\":3}],\"message\":\"dynamo: wrap graph break inst in try except block - with context manager setup/teardown (#94758)\\n\\nReplacement to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/94672.\\n\\nFollow up to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/94137.\\n\\nWe simply replace the set grad mode try except blocks with one for a more generic contextmanager (using `__enter__` and `__exit__`), storing the context manager into a `symbolic_local` for the duration of the try block.\\n\\n(see https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/207 for the original motivation)\\n\\nThis allows us to handle calling inner functions with graph breaks for any arbitrarily deep nesting of live context managers subclassing `AbstractContextManager`. (see tests)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/94758\\nApproved by: https://github.com/yanboliang\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7a192cc51c7172860efd35413acf9a8b9aafd2c9\"}],\"totalCount\":2}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "limitHit": false,
    "query": "type:diff content:test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d count:20",
    "results": [
      {
        "authorName": "Yanbo Liang",
        "commit": "9be9592f28023eff3caf87e13c1a89901c1819cf",
        "diffHunk": "/dev/null test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py\n@@ -0,0 +79,3 @@ \n+\n+    def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\n+        def fn(x):\ntest/dynamo/test_misc.py test/dynamo/test_misc.py\n@@ -936,3 +877,0 @@ class MiscTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n-\n-    def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\n-        def fn(x):\n",
        "matchRanges": [
          {
            "endCharacter": 42,
            "endLine": 3,
            "startCharacter": 9,
            "startLine": 3
          },
          {
            "endCharacter": 42,
            "endLine": 8,
            "startCharacter": 9,
            "startLine": 8
          }
        ],
        "message": "[Dynamo] Code refactor: move context managers out of misc.py (#97958)\n\nmisc.py and test_misc.py is too big, moving context managers to context.py and test_context.py.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/97958\nApproved by: https://github.com/ezyang, https://github.com/anijain2305, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/voznesenskym",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/9be9592f28023eff3caf87e13c1a89901c1819cf"
      },
      {
        "authorName": "jon-chuang",
        "commit": "7a192cc51c7172860efd35413acf9a8b9aafd2c9",
        "diffHunk": "test/dynamo/test_misc.py test/dynamo/test_misc.py\n@@ -912,1 +912,3 @@ class MiscTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n \n+    def test_nested_grad_mode_graph_break(self):\n+        def fn(x):\n",
        "matchRanges": [
          {
            "endCharacter": 42,
            "endLine": 3,
            "startCharacter": 9,
            "startLine": 3
          }
        ],
        "message": "dynamo: wrap graph break inst in try except block - with context manager setup/teardown (#94758)\n\nReplacement to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/94672.\n\nFollow up to https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/94137.\n\nWe simply replace the set grad mode try except blocks with one for a more generic contextmanager (using `__enter__` and `__exit__`), storing the context manager into a `symbolic_local` for the duration of the try block.\n\n(see https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/207 for the original motivation)\n\nThis allows us to handle calling inner functions with graph breaks for any arbitrarily deep nesting of live context managers subclassing `AbstractContextManager`. (see tests)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/94758\nApproved by: https://github.com/yanboliang",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7a192cc51c7172860efd35413acf9a8b9aafd2c9"
      }
    ],
    "totalCount": 2
  }
}

        "diffHunk": "torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py\n@@ -2362,1 +2376,3 @@ class InstructionTranslatorBase(\n+            reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),\n+            stack_pops=2,\n         )\n",
        "matchRanges": [
          {
            "endCharacter": 25,
            "endLine": 3,
            "startCharacter": 13,
            "startLine": 3
          }
        ],
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] refactor codegen to minimize NULL codegen'ing (#153510)\n\nStop codegening NULLs that we need to pop later. Some output_graph.py changes to prepare for nested graph break support.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/153510\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\nghstack dependencies: #151056",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1fe98429222a8ba5e16dd9381f50a8fb90edcf0e"
      }
    ],
    "totalCount": 3
  }
}

Supported context managers can cross graph break boundaries only if they are local non-closure variables, or are intermediate values.\\n   Hint: File an issue to PyTorch. Simple context managers can potentially be supported, but note that context managers can't be supported in general\\n \\n   Developer debug context: Attempted SETUP_WITH/BEFORE_WITH on it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\\n+        `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\\n+\\n+        Returns:\\n+            - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\\n+            - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\\n+            - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\\n+                (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        tx.prune_dead_locals()\\n+\\n+        stack_values = []\\n+        meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\\n+\\n+        # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\\n+        # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\\n+        for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\\n+            variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\\n+            # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\\n+            if len(tx.stack) -     "files": [
      {
        "added": 1,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " Unsupported context manager\n   Explanation: Dynamo does not know how to enter a `int` context manager.\n   Hint: Avoid using the unsupported context manager.\n+  Hint: If the context manager seems like it should be supported (e.g. torch.set_grad_enabled), then it may be the case that it was created outside the compiled region, which Dynamo does not support. Supported context managers can cross graph break boundaries only if they are local non-closure variables, or are intermediate values.\n   Hint: File an issue to PyTorch. Simple context managers can potentially be supported, but note that context managers can't be supported in general\n \n   Developer debug context: Attempted SETUP_WITH/BEFORE_WITH on ConstantVariable(int: 3)\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 239
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 239
            },
            "section": "from user code:"
          }
        ],
        "path": "test/dynamo/test_error_messages.py"
      },
      {
        "added": 0,
        "deleted": 16,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "                 create_instruction(\"UNPACK_SEQUENCE\", arg=n),\n             ]\n \n-    def pop_null(self):\n-        # POP_TOP doesn't work for null, so we pop nulls by pushing in a\n-        # nop function, calling it (which consumes the null), and popping the result.\n-        assert sys.version_info >= (3, 11)\n-        return [\n-            self.create_load_const_unchecked(lambda: None),\n-            # 3.13 swapped NULL and callable\n-            *(\n-                (create_instruction(\"SWAP\", arg=2),)\n-                if sys.version_info >= (3, 13)\n-                else ()\n-            ),\n-            *create_call_function(0, False),\n-            create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"),\n-        ]\n-\n     def pop_top(self):\n         self.append_output(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n \n",

            "section": "from .variables.builder import ("

          {
            "body": "         return self._aotautograd_guards\n \n \n+@dataclass\n+class StackLocalsMetadata:\n+    \"\"\"\n+    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n+    \"\"\"\n+\n+    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n+    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n+    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n+    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n+    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n+\n+\n class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n     \"\"\"\n     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 19,
              "startLine": 324
            },

              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 323
            },
            "section": "class OutputGraphGuardsState:"
          },
          {
            "body": "         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\n         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\n \n+    def _get_stack_values_to_restore(self, tx, stack_pops):\n+        \"\"\"\n+        Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\n+\n+        Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\n+\n+        NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\n+        elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\n+        `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\n+\n+        Returns:\n+            - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\n+            - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\n+            - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\n+                (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\n+        \"\"\"\n+        tx.prune_dead_locals()\n+\n+        stack_values = []\n+        meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\n+\n+        # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\n+        # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\n+        for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\n+            variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\n+            # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\n+            if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\n+                stack_values.append(value)\n+                continue\n+            if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\n+                meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\n+            else:\n+                stack_values.append(value)\n+            if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\n+                target_values = (\n+                    () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\n+                )\n+                # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\n+                meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\n+                meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\n+\n+        # Add all the local vars to the \"stack\" so restore at the end\n+        restore_vars: list[str] = []\n+        val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\n+        # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\n+        # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\n+        # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n+        # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\n+        # is only needed for partial graphs.\n+        # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\n+        # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\n+        # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\n+        for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\n+            # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\n+            # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_\n...             "body": "                     )\n                 else:\n                     prefix_insts.append(copy.copy(inst))\n+        self.add_output_instructions(prefix_insts)\n+\n         assert not (self.pregraph_bytecode and self.export), (\n             \"export does not support pregraph_bytecode\"\n         )\n-        prefix_insts.extend(self.pregraph_bytecode)\n-        alias_insts, overridden_sources = self.handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(tx)\n-        prefix_insts.extend(alias_insts)\n+        self.add_output_instructions(self.pregraph_bytecode)\n \n-        def append_prefix_insts():\n-            self.add_output_instructions(prefix_insts)\n-            prefix_insts.clear()\n+        alias_insts, overridden_sources = self.handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\n+            self.root_tx\n+        )\n+        self.add_output_instructions(alias_insts)\n \n-        for block in reversed(tx.block_stack):\n-            block.exit(tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\n+        # Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored\n+        for block in reversed(self.root_tx.block_stack):\n+            block.exit(self.root_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\n \n         self.cleanup_graph()\n-        tx.prune_dead_locals()\n-        stack_values = list(tx.stack)\n \n-        # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\n-        # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\n-        for value in stack_values:\n-            variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\n+        # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order\n+        # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame, first element corresponds to current frame\n+        all_stack_values = []\n+        all_restore_vars = []\n+        all_stack_locals_metas = []\n+        cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx\n+        while True:\n+            assert cur_tx is not None\n+            stack_values, restore_vars, meta = self._get_stack_values_to_restore(\n+                cur_tx, stack_pops\n+            )\n+            all_stack_values.append(stack_values)\n+            all_restore_vars.append(restore_vars)\n+            all_stack_locals_metas.append(meta)\n+            if cur_tx is self.root_tx:\n+                break\n+            cur_tx = tx.parent\n \n         # Use nn.Module \"proxies\" in the constructed GraphModule so that\n         # the resulting GM does not hold additional strong references to the original modules.\n",

            "body": "             name: nn_module_proxy(mod) for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items()\n         }\n         root = FakeRootModule(nn_modules_proxies)\n-        # Add all the local vars to the \"stack\" so restore at the end\n-        restore_vars: list[str] = []\n-        val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\n-        # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\n-        # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\n-        # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n-        # last instruction and no more locals are used. <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"f36f285\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\\n\\nThis PR implements the semantics change to `torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break`:\\n- ~`torch.compile` now has a new `error_on_graph_break` kwarg that serves as a lower-priority toggle for erroring/continuing on graph breaks~\\n- `error_on_graph_break` is a new internal `torch.compile `setting that is lower-priority than `fullgraph`. It allows the user to toggle erroring/continuing on graph breaks.\\n- `error_on_graph_break` does nothing when `fullgraph=True`\\n- `error_on_graph_break` does NOT guarantee a single graph\\n\\nFollowup [DONE]... <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"10d9332\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\\n\\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\\n```python\\nimport torch\\n\\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\\n\\ndef inner(x):\\n    x = x + 1\\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n    return x + 2\\n\\n@torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\ndef outer(x):\\n    return inner(x)\\n\\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\\n```\\n\\nPull Request resolved: (#160934)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 8f31aa97a3e1e17bed29b6cedf9884f0c6b145e9.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160934 on behalf of https://github.com/anijain2305 due to causes memory leak leading to OOMs ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160934#issuecomment-3234426359))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] [guard] Add caching for inside torch.compile.disable function to avoid unnecessary recompilation. (#160934)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/049c08eda8bd820a8514f5d785b10d4024bfd72b\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"68fa882\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Correctly track mutation class source for MutableMappingVariable (#161568)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/161505\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161568\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/malfet\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Correctly track mutation class source for MutableMappingVariable (#161568)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/68fa882dad760767a462e9c1e81dc76167078529\"},{\"author\":\"Arsh Zahed <arshzahed@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"4c45090\",\"date\":\"2025-09-09\",\"message\":\"[DTensor] Check if tracing for sharding propagation to handle unhashable keys (#160798)\\n\\nFixes #159590\\n\\nThis is similar to the reverted commit #156868, except it resolves an issue with two caches becoming misaligned, leading to incorrect objects for stateful placements (i.e. `_MaskPartial`) as in issue #159601. This adds little to no overhead in eager ([see past benchmarks](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156868#issuecomment-3047831149)).\\n\\nThis also handles cases such as #159590  where dynamo is disabled during tracing by entering the Python Dispatcher ahead of the sharding propogation... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[DTensor] Check if tracing for sharding propagation to handle unhashable keys (#160798)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/4c45090cf7e2f39b44c2d4da367366c9ec0cba68\"},{\"author\":\"Rob Timpe <rtimpe@openteams.com>\",\"commit\":\"1463714\",\"date\":\"2025-09-04\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\n\\nCurrently, user-defined classes inside of a compiled frame will cause the whole\\nframe to be skipped by dynamo.  This change defers the Unsupported exception\\nuntil the __build_class__ builtin is actually called, which allows a graph break\\nto be inserted.  Fixes #161562\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670\\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/guilhermeleobas\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"2efcf9d\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break registry loading in fbcode (#161550)\\n\\nSummary: Add `torch/_dynamo/graph_break_registry.json` as an internal dependency. Minor related fixes.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nTest on OSS.\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D81078973\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161550\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break registry loading in fbcode (#161550)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/2efcf9d0815fd5c729abcbb5622746184b19c3cc\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"e3068cd\",\"date\":\"2025-09-05\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Use relaxed CLOSURE_MATCH guard then ID_MATCH (#162247)\\n\\nI am unable to write a test that would fail here. The reason is that when we do _dynamo.disable(fn) in the compiled frame, the id of disabled function changes but currently we guard on the original function - `fn` whose id is not changing. This PR still guards on the `fn.__code__` just to be more precise.\\n\\nThanks to @thenumberouscode for pointing this out.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162247\\nApproved by: https://github.com/StrongerXi, https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Use relaxed CLOSURE_MATCH guard then ID_MATCH (#162247)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e3068cdb446adefb5a875616ba37a60235391439\"},{\"author\":\"Edward Yang <ezyang@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"bbedc71\",\"date\":\"2025-09-02\",\"message\":\"test: ensure editable cached wrapper is respected (#160943)\\n\\n## Summary\\n- add a test verifying that editing the local cache wrapper is picked up after Dynamo reset\\n\\n## Testing\\n- `lintrunner -a` *(fails: FLAKE8 failure, TEST_HAS_MAIN failure, CODESPELL failure, PYFMT failure)*\\n- `PYTHONPATH=. python test/inductor/test_codecache.py TestPyCodeCache.test_editable_cached_wrapper -v`\\n\\n------\\nhttps://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_68a3aa3fcc9883239b17d1f4250d1e89\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160943\\nApproved by: https://github.com/xmfan\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"test: ensure editable cached wrapper is respected (#160943)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/bbedc71fd3267c639c38b4ec25eaa22f973d9c4d\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"e9481b6\",\"date\":\"2025-09-01\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Prevent unnecessary recompile on disabled functions in the compiled frame (#161883)\\n\\nTrying out a re-impl of https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160934\\n\\nThe above PR led to OOM, most likely because of the cache holding to a nested function (which if not held in the cache would have been garbage collected), which holds on to cuda tensors in its closure.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161883\\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Prevent unnecessary recompile on disabled functions in the compiled frame (#161883)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e9481b6617b5576b099d8ca5798111592e9ad090\"},{\"author\":\"Shangdi Yu <shangdiy@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"92c2dae\",\"date\":\"2025-08-28\",\"message\":\"Add inductor provenance tracking artifacts to cache (#161440)\\n\\nSummary:\\n\\n- Add inductor provenance tracking artifacts to cache\\n- Update the tlparse version pin to `0.4.0`. The old tlparse version errors out on the new tlparse output. The lowest tlparse version that works is `0.3.42`.\\n\\ntlparse error:\\n```\\nthread 'main' panicked at src/parsers.rs:671:71:\\ncalled `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: Error(\\\"EOF while parsing a value\\\", line: 1, column: 0)\\nstack backtrace:\\n   0:     0x55e4ff1c7f00 - <std::sys::backtrace::BacktraceLock::print::DisplayBacktrace as core::fmt::Display>::fmt::h6d42cc84fc8... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Add inductor provenance tracking artifacts to cache (#161440)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/92c2daebb6338c13c40e86a7805c8a8c0a3ca5b8\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"26a1b9c\",\"date\":\"2025-09-08\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D81938289](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81938289)\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162318\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/26a1b9cce29f2edfbed661a229ead67fed613914\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"8678d83\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] rename set_fullgraph to error_on_graph_break (#161739)\\n\\nRenaming `set_fullgraph` to `error_on_graph_break` for now. There are no semantic differences yet. In a followup PR, we will introduce a new `torch.compile` option `error_on_graph_break` that has lower priority than `fullgraph` so that `fullgraph` really returns 1 graph.\\n\\nI could keep `set_fullgraph` as a deprecated alias for `error_on_graph_break` for now, but I'm hoping that won't be necessary since it's still private API (there are no internal callsites yet, and there are no significant OSS callsites yet).\\n\\n cc @albanD @voznes... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] rename set_fullgraph to error_on_graph_break (#161739)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8678d831c48e616b717bff50f2d03141d2e9f965\"},{\"author\":\"dolpm <34420038+dolpm@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"8ec551b\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)\\n\\npytest test/dynamo/test_aot_compile.py -k test_aot_compile_graph_break_error_fmt\\n\\nbefore\\n```\\nTraceback (most recent call last):\\n  File \\\"/data/users/$USER/vllm-tests/graph-break.py\\\", line 15, in <module>\\n    aot_compiled_fn = compiled.aot_compile((example_inputs, {}))\\n                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\\n  File \\\"/data/users/$USER/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/eval_frame.py\\\", line 717, in aot_compile\\n    return aot_compile_fullgraph(\\n           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\\n  F... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8ec551bb354ab2b85fbbba9d461740a20366d248\"},{\"author\":\"Raman-RH <ramakuma@redhat.com>\",\"commit\":\"20bfb25\",\"date\":\"2025-09-01\",\"message\":\"Skip compilation when FX graph has no calls and returns empty (#160536)\\n\\nFixes #160437\\n\\nSummary:\\nThis PR avoids compiling empty FX graphs generated during graph breaks. If there are no calls in the graph, we can just return the empty list of instructions.\\n\\nMore precisely,\\nIn compile_and_call_fx_graph, if the FX graph contains no calls (count_calls(self.graph) == 0) and the return value list is empty, we now return an empty instruction list immediately\\n\\nImpact:\\nmodule: dynamo\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160536\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Skip compilation when FX graph has no calls and returns empty (#160536)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/20bfb2539d7c5250379648eda35f80b8a7d642dd\"},{\"author\":\"Will Constable <whc@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"4fd761f\",\"date\":\"2025-08-28\",\"message\":\"[DTensor] Wrap sharding prop error with contextual exception (#161574)\\n\\nMainly, this helps tell the user more info about the operator that\\nfailed to run if it fails during sharding propagation.\\n\\nPreviously, only this exception would be raised:\\n```\\nRuntimeError: ('Attempted to flatten sharded dimension 1, ', 'but only the leftmost dim of a Flatten can be sharded.')\\n```\\n\\nNow you get both the above exception as well as\\n\\n```\\nThe above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:\\nRuntimeError: Sharding propagation failed for Op(op=aten.view.default, args_schema=Spec((Replicate(), Shar... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[DTensor] Wrap sharding prop error with contextual exception (#161574)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/4fd761fecca86693c8cd7755ea6d13d61bb94090\"},{\"author\":\"Avik Chaudhuri <avik@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"12c0cf3\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"switch prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards in export (#160111)\\n\\nSummary:\\nIn preparation for checking shape guards in export, this PR effectively switches `prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards` to `False`, matching Dynamo.\\n\\nActually that's a lie: we switch it to `allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts`, which is `False` by default but can be controlled via an internally API to be `True`. This makes the two flags synchronized, so we should be able to kill `allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts` at this point.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nupdated tests\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D79... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"switch prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards in export (#160111)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/12c0cf3fab74e4669d5b760be6bd2be008633ada\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"a3c1cbd\",\"date\":\"2025-08-24\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][higher order ops] Refactor for out spec (#161354)\\n\\nPreparing for the next PR to add more info in the output spec.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161354\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][higher order ops] Refactor for out spec (#161354)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a3c1cbdbc6843c6e7ab2f38977a6462b4e510903\"},{\"author\":\"angelayi <yiangela7@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"5c67426\",\"date\":\"2025-09-05\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Add support for const prop on .item (#162204)\\n\\nFixes some of the errors in https://fb.workplace.com/groups/1028545332188949/permalink/1303030824740397/\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162204\\nApproved by: <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"3302859\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Make the MRO walk more narrow (#162105)\\n\\nI dont have a failing test case but just saw an extra guard somewhere.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162105\\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/StrongerXi, <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"1041805\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\\n\\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\\n\\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\\n\\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1041805c1ed0d4d96fd05cf485a0d89a0b5588d0\"},{\"author\":\"Michael Lazos <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"0ff8eab\",\"date\":\"2025-09-06\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670 on behalf of https://github.com/jeanschmidt due to seems to have introduced https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379267 and https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379271 ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670#issuecomment-3261241229))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0ff8eabf1387de5acd6712a03bda61f1a3dfa27f\"},{\"author\":\"Brian Hirsh <hirsheybar@fb.com>\",\"commit\":\"0d71a9d\",\"date\":\"2025-08-15\",\"message\":\"fix incorrect interaction between DDPOptimizer and donated buffers (#160745)\\n\\nThis should fix https://x.com/wightmanr/status/1953147089518772254?t=ng_R4t0-tRhO_qQE8NqOhw&s=19. Still working on adding a reasonable test.\\n\\nYou can see more of a description of the problem in the code comments. But the TLDR is that:\\n\\n* When using DDPOptimizer, we partition the graph and compile several subgraphs. So 1 dynamo graphs becomes N AOT/inductor artifacts\\n* We have some existing logic to stash graph metadata (`fw_metadata`) in dynamo's TracingContext. When using DDPOptimizer, we generate one `fw_metadata` ... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"fix incorrect interaction between DDPOptimizer and donated buffers (#160745)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0d71a9dd5b4b6d1dde58d91c9b71d96bc6a6a171\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"6b1900c\",\"date\":\"2025-08-28\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][hops] Remove const outputs from the speculated subgraph (#161355)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161355\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][hops] Remove const outputs from the speculated subgraph (#161355)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6b1900c22f1a07b9519346898d4c71d8a2b0f12f\"},{\"author\":\"can-gaa-hou <jiahaochen535@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"c0ed87c\",\"date\":\"2025-08-28\",\"message\":\"[Dynamo] Fix weakref.proxy error when `torch.compile` (#161508)\\n\\nFixes #159258\\n\\nThe error occurs when we attempt to create a weak reference from a weak reference proxy.\\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/e9d42b3880dcdbd823bbdc9370c8b0b3af0ba2e3/torch/_dynamo/guards.py#L2910-L2915\\n\\nIn fact, we shouldn't create a weak reference from another reference or proxy, as it would check in CPython.\\nhttps://github.com/python/cpython/blob/f60f8225ed146a8f9b5fbf1eeed3474782127ea8/Objects/weakrefobject.c#L410-L418\\n\\nHowever, `__weakrefoffset__` is not equal to **0** when the `guarded_object` is in `weakr... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[Dynamo] Fix weakref.proxy error when `torch.compile` (#161508)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/c0ed87c82d9417960791c9b35ccffc302c71eca1\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"3d40642\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][vllm] Support typing.get_type_hints (#161362)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161362\\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/StrongerXi, https://github.com/jansel\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][vllm] Support typing.get_type_hints (#161362)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/3d406429b0ac472b65d944c0634203ac1548a7c9\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"d5643e8\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks that cause skipped frames (#160470)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160470\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138, #159786\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks that cause skipped frames (#160470)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d5643e8f3a648a99636bfa1f2a41d54bd3c0d0f1\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"6562646\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\\n\\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\\n\\nAlso simplify some codegen.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6562646dab09b76479466af3b2a0e866b9c08cdd\"},{\"author\":\"Roman Bobniev <ironsided@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"47ecd20\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[ONNX] Fix index_put_ usage (#161263)\\n\\nSummary:\\nIt's hard to understand how it's working in most of our models, but in general it looks like `aten::copy_` is replaced incorrectly.\\nThere are two schemas for `aten::copy_`:\\n1. `aten::copy_.Tensor(Tensor(a!) self, Tensor other) -> Tensor(a!)`\\n2. `aten::copy_(Tensor(a!) self, Tensor src, bool non_blocking=False) -> Tensor(a!)`\\n\\nAccording to the logic in the comments we don't need one of the parameters for `aten::index_put_`.\\n\\nIt seems logic has been inferred from ordinary `aten::copy` where there could be a third parameter which is `non_blocking` f... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[ONNX] Fix index_put_ usage (#161263)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/47ecd2042f2f99368cd2d60731d23f4437eecd1d\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"600c25e\",\"date\":\"2025-09-02\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on torch.cuda.sychronize (#161925)\\n\\nToday, AOTDispatcher ignores cuda.synchornize. Even if we wrap it in\\nsome  HOP, we need it to be a barrier op to prevent any inductor\\nreordering. So graph breaking.\\n\\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/160751\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161925\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519, https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/mlazos\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on torch.cuda.sychronize (#161925)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/600c25e9a17fe56e3dee872be8854db08916ba0c\"},{\"author\":\"Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"403a3a3\",\"date\":\"2025-09-01\",\"message\":\"Defer loading hipify until it is needed (#160824)\\n\\nSaves a few milliseconds when running a test case:\\n\\nBefore:\\n```\\n$ PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1 python test/dynamo/cpython/3_13/test_float.py GeneralFloatCases.test_float_pow\\nframes [('total', 1), ('ok', 1)]\\ninline_call []\\n.\\n----------------------------------------------------------------------\\nRan 1 test in 1.497s\\n```\\n\\nAfter:\\n```\\n$ PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1 python test/dynamo/cpython/3_13/test_float.py GeneralFloatCases.test_float_pow\\nframes [('total', 1), ('ok', 1)]\\ninline_call []\\n.\\n-------------------------------------------------------------... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Defer loading hipify until it is needed (#160824)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/403a3a393cda7e60f503f3b04b8805a845dcf45d\"}],\"limitHit\":true,\"query\":\"type:commit (message:STORE_ATTR OR message:dynamo) repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d after:2025-08-01 count:100\",\"totalCount\":43}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "789d494",
        "date": "2025-09-02",
        "message": "Defer loading hipify until it is needed (#160824)\n\nSaves a few milliseconds when running a test case:\n\nBefore:\n```\n$ PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1 python test/dynamo/cpython/3_13/test_float.py GeneralFloatCases.test_float_pow\nframes [('total', 1), ('ok', 1)]\ninline_call []\n.\n----------------------------------------------------------------------\nRan 1 test in 1.497s\n```\n\nAfter:\n```\n$ PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1 python test/dynamo/cpython/3_13/test_float.py GeneralFloatCases.test_float_pow\nframes [('total', 1), ('ok', 1)]\ninline_call []\n.\n-------------------------------------------------------------... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Defer loading hipify until it is needed (#160824)",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "d0a242e",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159817\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678",

        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)",

      },
      {
        "author": "zhxchen17 <zhxchen17@fb.com>",
        "commit": "c36d18d",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[rfc] aot precompile with custom backend api (#161383)\n\nAdding a new feature to torch.compile(fullgraph=True) which \"aot_compile\" a function with given example inputs.\n\nOn user side it should look like:\n\n```\ndef foo(x, y):\n    return x + y\n\ncompiled_fn = torch.compile(fullgraph=True).aot_compile(((torch.randn(3, 4), torch.randn(3, 4)), {}))\n```\n\nThis is different from the traditional `torch.compile` workflow where compiled object will be a drop-in replacement for the original eager model:\n```\ntensor input -> torch.compile() -> tensor output (and populates the cache entry)\n```\n`aot_compile` wil... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[rfc] aot precompile with custom backend api (#161383)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/c36d18d7e86597092e9e3f1ea7aabbb70788155b"
      },
      {
        "author": "Scott Wolchok <swolchok@meta.com>",
        "commit": "e025c0f",
        "date": "2025-09-08",
        "message": "Dynamo: set_eval_frame microoptimization (#162220)\n\nOptimize for common case and remove a pair of refcount operations (see new comments.)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162220\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/williamwen42\nghstack dependencies: #161591, #161595, #161633, #161634, #161692, #162219",

      {
        "author": "dolpm <34420038+dolpm@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "e0a62b2",
        "date": "2025-09-05",
        "message": "[aot-precompile] default-filter global guards (#162090)\n\nif the user doesn't provide their own guard filter fn, we should by default filter global guards.\n\npytest test/dynamo/test_aot_compile.py\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162090\nApproved by: https://github.com/zhxchen17",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[aot-precompile] default-filter global guards (#162090)",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "f36f285",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\n\nThis PR implements the semantics change to `torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break`:\n- ~`torch.compile` now has a new `error_on_graph_break` kwarg that serves as a lower-priority toggle for erroring/continuing on graph breaks~\n- `error_on_graph_break` is a new internal `torch.compile `setting that is lower-priority than `fullgraph`. It allows the user to toggle erroring/continuing on graph breaks.\n- `error_on_graph_break` does nothing when `fullgraph=True`\n- `error_on_graph_break` does NOT guarantee a single graph\n\nFollowup [DONE]... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)",

      },
      {
        "author": "Simon Fan <xmfan@meta.com>",
        "commit": "15670f9",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dtensor] support local_map as a decorator (#161353)\n\nAnd extract it out as a convenience function for dynamo to wrap\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161353\nApproved by: https://github.com/zpcore",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "10d9332",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\n\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\n```python\nimport torch\n\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\n\ndef inner(x):\n    x = x + 1\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n    return x + 2\n\n@torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\ndef outer(x):\n    return inner(x)\n\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/10d93325b1f2ade44bf47fd75a93a8af64f3e620"
      },
      {
        "author": "Justin Chu <justinchuby@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "bd39e47",
        "date": "2025-09-02",
        "message": "[ONNX] Default to dynamo export (#159646)\n\nSet dynamo=True and enable fallback.\n\n1. Implemented the compatible behavior where BytesIO objects as `f` is accepted\n2. Update tests to explicitly set dynamo=False\n\n#151693\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159646\nApproved by: https://github.com/titaiwangms",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

(#160934)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 8f31aa97a3e1e17bed29b6cedf9884f0c6b145e9.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160934 on behalf of https://github.com/anijain2305 due to causes memory leak leading to OOMs ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160934#issuecomment-3234426359))",

      {
        "author": "Arsh Zahed <arshzahed@meta.com>",
        "commit": "4c45090",
        "date": "2025-09-09",
        "message": "[DTensor] Check if tracing for sharding propagation to handle unhashable keys (#160798)\n\nFixes #159590\n\nThis is similar to the reverted commit #156868, except it resolves an issue with two caches becoming misaligned, leading to incorrect objects for stateful placements (i.e. `_MaskPartial`) as in issue #159601. This adds little to no overhead in eager ([see past benchmarks](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/156868#issuecomment-3047831149)).\n\nThis also handles cases such as #159590  where dynamo is disabled during tracing by entering the Python Dispatcher ahead of the sharding propogation... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[DTensor] Check if tracing for sharding propagation to handle unhashable keys (#160798)",
