      {
        "author": "Rob Timpe <rtimpe@openteams.com>",
        "commit": "1463714",
        "date": "2025-09-04",
        "message": "[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\n\nCurrently, user-defined classes inside of a compiled frame will cause the whole\nframe to be skipped by dynamo.  This change defers the Unsupported exception\nuntil the __build_class__ builtin is actually called, which allows a graph break\nto be inserted.  Fixes #161562\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/guilhermeleobas",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "2efcf9d",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo] Fix graph break registry loading in fbcode (#161550)\n\nSummary: Add `torch/_dynamo/graph_break_registry.json` as an internal dependency. Minor related fixes.\n\nTest Plan:\nTest on OSS.\n\nRollback Plan:\n\nDifferential Revision: D81078973\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161550\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",

      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "e3068cd",
        "date": "2025-09-05",
        "message": "[dynamo] Use relaxed CLOSURE_MATCH guard then ID_MATCH (#162247)\n\nI am unable to write a test that would fail here. The reason is that when we do _dynamo.disable(fn) in the compiled frame, the id of disabled function changes but currently we guard on the original function - `fn` whose id is not changing. This PR still guards on the `fn.__code__` just to be more precise.\n\nThanks to @thenumberouscode for pointing this out.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162247\nApproved by: https://github.com/StrongerXi, https://github.com/jansel",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Use relaxed CLOSURE_MATCH guard then ID_MATCH (#162247)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e3068cdb446adefb5a875616ba37a60235391439"
      },
      {
        "author": "Edward Yang <ezyang@meta.com>",
        "commit": "bbedc71",
        "date": "2025-09-02",
        "message": "test: ensure editable cached wrapper is respected (#160943)\n\n## Summary\n- add a test verifying that editing the local cache wrapper is picked up after Dynamo reset\n\n## Testing\n- `lintrunner -a` *(fails: FLAKE8 failure, TEST_HAS_MAIN failure, CODESPELL failure, PYFMT failure)*\n- `PYTHONPATH=. python test/inductor/test_codecache.py TestPyCodeCache.test_editable_cached_wrapper -v`\n\n------\nhttps://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_68a3aa3fcc9883239b17d1f4250d1e89\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160943\nApproved by: https://github.com/xmfan",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "test: ensure editable cached wrapper is respected (#160943)",

      },
      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "e9481b6",
        "date": "2025-09-01",
        "message": "[dynamo] Prevent unnecessary recompile on disabled functions in the compiled frame (#161883)\n\nTrying out a re-impl of https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160934\n\nThe above PR led to OOM, most likely because of the cache holding to a nested function (which if not held in the cache would have been garbage collected), which holds on to cuda tensors in its closure.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161883\nApproved by: https://github.com/jansel",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Prevent unnecessary recompile on disabled functions in the compiled frame (#161883)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e9481b6617b5576b099d8ca5798111592e9ad090"
      },

        "commit": "92c2dae",
        "date": "2025-08-28",
        "message": "Add inductor provenance tracking artifacts to cache (#161440)\n\nSummary:\n\n- Add inductor provenance tracking artifacts to cache\n- Update the tlparse version pin to `0.4.0`. The old tlparse version errors out on the new tlparse output. The lowest tlparse version that works is `0.3.42`.\n\ntlparse error:\n```\nthread 'main' panicked at src/parsers.rs:671:71:\ncalled `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: Error(\"EOF while parsing a value\", line: 1, column: 0)\nstack backtrace:\n   0:     0x55e4ff1c7f00 - <std::sys::backtrace::BacktraceLock::print::DisplayBacktrace as core::fmt::Display>::fmt::h6d42cc84fc8... [truncated]",

      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "26a1b9c",
        "date": "2025-09-08",
        "message": "[dynamo] fix resume_execution.py KeyError in Python 3.11+ (#162318)\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/162313\n\nDifferential Revision: [D81938289](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81938289)\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162318\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela, https://github.com/mlazos, https://github.com/anijain2305",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "8678d83",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[dynamo] rename set_fullgraph to error_on_graph_break (#161739)\n\nRenaming `set_fullgraph` to `error_on_graph_break` for now. There are no semantic differences yet. In a followup PR, we will introduce a new `torch.compile` option `error_on_graph_break` that has lower priority than `fullgraph` so that `fullgraph` really returns 1 graph.\n\nI could keep `set_fullgraph` as a deprecated alias for `error_on_graph_break` for now, but I'm hoping that won't be necessary since it's still private API (there are no internal callsites yet, and there are no significant OSS callsites yet).\n\n cc @albanD @voznes... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] rename set_fullgraph to error_on_graph_break (#161739)",

      },

        "commit": "8ec551b",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)\n\npytest test/dynamo/test_aot_compile.py -k test_aot_compile_graph_break_error_fmt\n\nbefore\n```\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"/data/users/$USER/vllm-tests/graph-break.py\", line 15, in <module>\n    aot_compiled_fn = compiled.aot_compile((example_inputs, {}))\n                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n  File \"/data/users/$USER/pytorch/torch/_dynamo/eval_frame.py\", line 717, in aot_compile\n    return aot_compile_fullgraph(\n           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n  F... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[aot-compile] strip internal tracebacks for non-verbose graph breaks + include user file/lineno (#162005)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8ec551bb354ab2b85fbbba9d461740a20366d248"
      },
      {
        "author": "Raman-RH <ramakuma@redhat.com>",
        "commit": "20bfb25",
        "date": "2025-09-01",
        "message": "Skip compilation when FX graph has no calls and returns empty (#160536)\n\nFixes #160437\n\nSummary:\nThis PR avoids compiling empty FX graphs generated during graph breaks. If there are no calls in the graph, we can just return the empty list of instructions.\n\nMore precisely,\nIn compile_and_call_fx_graph, if the FX graph contains no calls (count_calls(self.graph) == 0) and the return value list is empty, we now return an empty instruction list immediately\n\nImpact:\nmodule: dynamo\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160536\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Skip compilation when FX graph has no calls and returns empty (#160536)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/20bfb2539d7c5250379648eda35f80b8a7d642dd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Will Constable <whc@meta.com>",
        "commit": "4fd761f",
        "date": "2025-08-28",
        "message": "[DTensor] Wrap sharding prop error with contextual exception (#161574)\n\nMainly, this helps tell the user more info about the operator that\nfailed to run if it fails during sharding propagation.\n\nPreviously, only this exception would be raised:\n```\nRuntimeError: ('Attempted to flatten sharded dimension 1, ', 'but only the leftmost dim of a Flatten can be sharded.')\n```\n\nNow you get both the above exception as well as\n\n```\nThe above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:\nRuntimeError: Sharding propagation failed for Op(op=aten.view.default, args_schema=Spec((Replicate(), Shar... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[DTensor] Wrap sharding prop error with contextual exception (#161574)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/4fd761fecca86693c8cd7755ea6d13d61bb94090"
      },
      {
        "author": "Avik Chaudhuri <avik@meta.com>",
        "commit": "12c0cf3",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "switch prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards in export (#160111)\n\nSummary:\nIn preparation for checking shape guards in export, this PR effectively switches `prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards` to `False`, matching Dynamo.\n\nActually that's a lie: we switch it to `allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts`, which is `False` by default but can be controlled via an internally API to be `True`. This makes the two flags synchronized, so we should be able to kill `allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts` at this point.\n\nTest Plan:\nupdated tests\n\nRollback Plan:\n\nDifferential Revision: D79... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "switch prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards in export (#160111)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/12c0cf3fab74e4669d5b760be6bd2be008633ada"
      },
      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "a3c1cbd",
        "date": "2025-08-24",
        "message": "[dynamo][higher order ops] Refactor for out spec (#161354)\n\nPreparing for the next PR to add more info in the output spec.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161354\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo][higher order ops] Refactor for out spec (#161354)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a3c1cbdbc6843c6e7ab2f38977a6462b4e510903"

        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "3302859",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[dynamo] Make the MRO walk more narrow (#162105)\n\nI dont have a failing test case but just saw an extra guard somewhere.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/162105\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/StrongerXi, https://github.com/jansel",

      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "1041805",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\n\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\n\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\n\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1041805c1ed0d4d96fd05cf485a0d89a0b5588d0"
      },
      {
        "author": "Michael Lazos <mlazos@meta.com>",
        "commit": "be55d7a",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "Revert \"[Dynamo] Allow inlining into AO quantization modules (#152934)\" (#161567)\n\nThis reverts commit 20e2ca3e29ce9eb33eef17db077696222c175764.\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/157434\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161567\nApproved by: https://github.com/Lucaskabela",

      },
      {
        "author": "Brian Hirsh <hirsheybar@fb.com>",
        "commit": "0d71a9d",
        "date": "2025-08-15",
        "message": "fix incorrect interaction between DDPOptimizer and donated buffers (#160745)\n\nThis should fix https://x.com/wightmanr/status/1953147089518772254?t=ng_R4t0-tRhO_qQE8NqOhw&s=19. Still working on adding a reasonable test.\n\nYou can see more of a description of the problem in the code comments. But the TLDR is that:\n\n* When using DDPOptimizer, we partition the graph and compile several subgraphs. So 1 dynamo graphs becomes N AOT/inductor artifacts\n* We have some existing logic to stash graph metadata (`fw_metadata`) in dynamo's TracingContext. When using DDPOptimizer, we generate one `fw_metadata` ... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "fix incorrect interaction between DDPOptimizer and donated buffers (#160745)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0d71a9dd5b4b6d1dde58d91c9b71d96bc6a6a171"
      },
      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "6b1900c",
        "date": "2025-08-28",
        "message": "[dynamo][hops] Remove const outputs from the speculated subgraph (#161355)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161355\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo][hops] Remove const outputs from the speculated subgraph (#161355)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6b1900c22f1a07b9519346898d4c71d8a2b0f12f"
      },
      {
        "author": "can-gaa-hou <jiahaochen535@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "c0ed87c",
        "date": "2025-08-28",
        "message": "[Dynamo] Fix weakref.proxy error when `torch.compile` (#161508)\n\nFixes #159258\n\nThe error occurs when we attempt to create a weak reference from a weak reference proxy.\nhttps://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/blob/e9d42b3880dcdbd823bbdc9370c8b0b3af0ba2e3/torch/_dynamo/guards.py#L2910-L2915\n\nIn fact, we shouldn't create a weak reference from another reference or proxy, as it would check in CPython.\nhttps://github.com/python/cpython/blob/f60f8225ed146a8f9b5fbf1eeed3474782127ea8/Objects/weakrefobject.c#L410-L418\n\nHowever, `__weakrefoffset__` is not equal to **0** when the `guarded_object` is in `weakr... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[Dynamo] Fix weakref.proxy error when `torch.compile` (#161508)",

      },

        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "3d40642",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo][vllm] Support typing.get_type_hints (#161362)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161362\nApproved by: https://github.com/Skylion007, https://github.com/StrongerXi, https://github.com/jansel",

      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "6562646",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\n\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\n\nAlso simplify some codegen.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6562646dab09b76479466af3b2a0e866b9c08cdd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Roman Bobniev <ironsided@meta.com>",
        "commit": "47ecd20",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[ONNX] Fix index_put_ usage (#161263)\n\nSummary:\nIt's hard to understand how it's working in most of our models, but in general it looks like `aten::copy_` is replaced incorrectly.\nThere are two schemas for `aten::copy_`:\n1. `aten::copy_.Tensor(Tensor(a!) self, Tensor other) -> Tensor(a!)`\n2. `aten::copy_(Tensor(a!) self, Tensor src, bool non_blocking=False) -> Tensor(a!)`\n\nAccording to the logic in the comments we don't need one of the parameters for `aten::index_put_`.\n\nIt seems logic has been inferred from ordinary `aten::copy` where there could be a third parameter which is `non_blocking` f... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[ONNX] Fix index_put_ usage (#161263)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/47ecd2042f2f99368cd2d60731d23f4437eecd1d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>",
        "commit": "600c25e",
        "date": "2025-09-02",
        "message": "[dynamo] Graph break on torch.cuda.sychronize (#161925)\n\nToday, AOTDispatcher ignores cuda.synchornize. Even if we wrap it in\nsome  HOP, we need it to be a barrier op to prevent any inductor\nreordering. So graph breaking.\n\nFixes https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/160751\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161925\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519, https://github.com/jansel, https://github.com/mlazos",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Graph break on torch.cuda.sychronize (#161925)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/600c25e9a17fe56e3dee872be8854db08916ba0c"
      },
      {
        "author": "Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "403a3a3",
        "date": "2025-09-01",
        "message": "Defer loading hipify until it is needed (#160824)\n\nSaves a few milliseconds when running a test case:\n\nBefore:\n```\n$ PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1 python test/dynamo/cpython/3_13/test_float.py GeneralFloatCases.test_float_pow\nframes [('total', 1), ('ok', 1)]\ninline_call []\n.\n----------------------------------------------------------------------\nRan 1 test in 1.497s\n```\n\nAfter:\n```\n$ PYTORCH_TEST_WITH_DYNAMO=1 python test/dynamo/cpython/3_13/test_float.py GeneralFloatCases.test_float_pow\nframes [('total', 1), ('ok', 1)]\ninline_call []\n.\n-------------------------------------------------------------... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Defer loading hipify until it is needed (#160824)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/403a3a393cda7e60f503f3b04b8805a845dcf45d"
      }
    ],

  "content": "{\"commits\":[{\"author\":\"PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>\",\"commit\":\"0ff8eab\",\"date\":\"2025-09-06\",\"message\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\\"\\n\\nThis reverts commit 146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb.\\n\\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670 on behalf of https://github.com/jeanschmidt due to seems to have introduced https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379267 and https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379271 ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670#issuecomment-3261241229))\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Revert \\\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\\"\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0ff8eabf1387de5acd6712a03bda61f1a3dfa27f\"},{\"author\":\"Rob Timpe <rtimpe@openteams.com>\",\"commit\":\"1463714\",\"date\":\"2025-09-04\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\\n\\nCurrently, user-defined classes inside of a compiled frame will cause the whole\\nframe to be skipped by dynamo.  This change defers the Unsupported exception\\nuntil the __build_class__ builtin is actually called, which allows a graph break\\nto be inserted.  Fixes #161562\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670\\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/guilhermeleobas\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"f36f285\",\"date\":\"2025-09-03\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\\n\\nThis PR implements the semantics change to `torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break`:\\n- ~`torch.compile` now has a new `error_on_graph_break` kwarg that serves as a lower-priority toggle for erroring/continuing on graph breaks~\\n- `error_on_graph_break` is a new internal `torch.compile `setting that is lower-priority than `fullgraph`. It allows the user to toggle erroring/continuing on graph breaks.\\n- `error_on_graph_break` does nothing when `fullgraph=True`\\n- `error_on_graph_break` does NOT guarantee a single graph\\n\\nFollowup [DONE]... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics #159786\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks that cause skipped frames (#160470)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d5643e8f3a648a99636bfa1f2a41d54bd3c0d0f1\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"1041805\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\\n\\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\\n\\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\\n\\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1041805c1ed0d4d96fd05cf485a0d89a0b5588d0\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"6562646\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\\n\\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\\n\\nAlso simplify some codegen.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen #159329\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/3f8090809faf0b51456d9f427dd93ed8da3a11f6\"},{\"author\":\"William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"10d9332\",\"date\":\"2025-08-27\",\"message\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\\n\\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\\n```python\\nimport torch\\n\\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\\n\\ndef inner(x):\\n    x = x + 1\\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n    return x + 2\\n\\n@torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\ndef outer(x):\\n    return inner(x)\\n\\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\\n```\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/10d93325b1f2ade44bf47fd75a93a8af64f3e620\"},{\"author\":\"Guilherme Leobas <zhxchen17@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"74124d1\",\"date\":\"2025-08-26\",\"message\":\"[reland] [dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. [5/n] (#161514)\\n\\nSummary:\\nconvert_frame.compile_frame used to take a callback transform function which will capture the frame object it has, but the frame information is not passed directly into compile_frame function.\\n\\nThis PR changes the signature of compile_frame so that frame information is directly passed in the function without taking a callback. This makes it easier to build fullgraph capture API on top of compile_frame.\\n\\nTest Plan:\\nCI\\n\\nRollback Plan:\\n\\nDifferential Revision: D81041296\\n\\nPull Request res... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[reland] [dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. This makes it easier to build fullgraph capture API on top of compile_frame.\\n@exported-using-ghexport\\n\\nDifferential Revision: [D80469801](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/inter... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. [5/n] (#160900)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1113e7de30da95973c1eac7921601f9a0e94f2db\"},{\"author\":\"zeshengzong <zesheng.zong@outlook.com>\",\"commit\":\"510825e\",\"date\":\"2025-08-25\",\"message\":\"Optimize `dynamo` typing (#147499)\\n\\nOptimize dynamo methods type annotation.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147499\\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Optimize `dynamo` typing (#147499)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/510825e5fed8b56eb5e9352c12f0df1feeadb810\"},{\"author\":\"James Wu <jjwu@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"9668210\",\"date\":\"2025-08-20\",\"message\":\"Allow bypasses for Precompile when guards, etc. cannot be serialized (#160902)\\n\\nThis adds a new function `bypass_package` and `CompilePackage.bypass_current_entry()`. This allows us to safely bypass if there are models with unserializable or incompatible parts. When we encounter something incompatible, we'll raise a bypass and ignore that particular code in DynamoCodeEntry.\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160902\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zhxchen17\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Allow bypasses for Precompile when guards, etc. cannot be serialized (#160902)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/96682103026b5ea27f19e6db9303e17572095b0e\"},{\"author\":\"Lucas Kabela <lucaskabela@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"54cc63b\",\"date\":\"2025-08-20\",\"message\":\"[BE][Dynamo] Type coverage for symbolic_convert (#160922)\\n\\nAs part of better engineering, we add type coverage to `dynamo/symbolic_convert.py`, which is the main work engine of dynamo for emulating python bytecode.\\n\\nRunning\\n```\\nmypy torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py --linecount-report /tmp/coverage_log\\n```\\n\\n| -------- | Lines Annotated | Lines Total | % lines covered | Funcs Annotated | Funcs Total | % funcs covered |\\n| -------- | ------- | -------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- |\\n| Main  |  764 | 4286 | 17.83% | 43 | 241 | 17.84% |\\n| This PR | 4322 | 4322 | 100.00% | 241 | 241 | 100... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[BE][Dynamo] Type coverage for symbolic_convert (#160922)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/54cc63b467f24242cf0d6538d3e1df39e553daf1\"},{\"author\":\"Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"a1a555e\",\"date\":\"2025-08-15\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break on calling functions decorated with special context manager (#160703)\\n\\nAs title. This is a follow-up of the previous patch, with the goal of\\nsupporting a new pattern that showed up in ComfyUI:\\nhttps://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/blob/644b23ac0b92442b475e44397c62aa8de929d546/comfy/ops.py#L44\\n\\nEffectively, the semantics of calling a function decorated with a\\ncontext manager is:\\n\\n```python\\n@ctx_manager(args)\\ndef f(x):\\n    ...\\n\\nf(x)\\n# ----->\\nwith ctx_manager(args):\\n    f.__wrapped__(x)\\n```\\n\\nYes, a fresh context manager instance per invokation, see CPython source code... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Fix graph break on calling functions decorated with special context manager (#160703)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a1a555ed7b98046e5c8ee18a903b9381e7ce322d\"},{\"author\":\"Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>\",\"commit\":\"72b559b\",\"date\":\"2025-08-15\",\"message\":\"[dynamo] Fix crash and silent incorrectness issues in `attention.sdpa_kernel` calls with kwargs (#160684)\\n\\nThis patch fixes 2 issues, illustrated by the test cases added:\\n1. using `sdpa_kernel(backends=..., set_priority=...)` due to an\\n   internal assert that forgot to be updated after #147768.\\n2. forgetting to convert the `set_priority` VariableTracker back to a\\n   python constant so that its value is properly used by `sdpa_kernel`,\\n   also from #147768.\\n\\nI ran into (1) because ComfyUI had a recent update that actually sues\\nthis pattern\\nhttps://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/blob/644b23ac0... [truncated]\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo] Fix crash and silent incorrectness issues in `attention.sdpa_kernel` calls with kwargs (#160684)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/72b559b2c8b606b629a22d0f89416d9cbbd09589\"},{\"author\":\"Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"f019da2\",\"date\":\"2025-08-16\",\"message\":\"Implement `list(UserDefinedObject)` via `force_unpack_var_sequence` (#159864)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159864\\nApproved by: https://github.com/mlazos\\nghstack dependencies: #159365, #159366, #159368, #159483, #159902\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"Implement `list(UserDefinedObject)` via `force_unpack_var_sequence` (#159864)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/f019da2979ab67f48cdd5d5351b6a9d24aec3384\"},{\"author\":\"Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>\",\"commit\":\"d387a48\",\"date\":\"2025-08-14\",\"message\":\"[generator] Raise `StopIteration(value)` with value from the return stmt (#157152)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157152\\nApproved by: https://github.com/zou3519\\nghstack dependencies: #157148\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[generator] Raise `StopIteration(value)` with value from the return stmt (#157152)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d387a48c38f28d479bd305307bb8edddaaf64b46\"},{\"author\":\"Animesh Jain <anijain@umich.edu>\",\"commit\":\"a4f69a5\",\"date\":\"2025-08-08\",\"message\":\"[dynamo][guards] Remove guards on stdlib modules (#159913)\\n\\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159913\\nApproved by: https://github.com/StrongerXi\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d\",\"title\":\"[dynamo][guards] Remove guards on stdlib modules (#159913)\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a4f69a5da08eace1c1e6469dec6a18aa842da73b\"}],\"limitHit\":false,\"query\":\"type:commit file:torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py|test/dynamo/test_ctx_manager.py repo:github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d after:2025-08-01 count:100\",\"totalCount\":39}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "commits": [
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "0ff8eab",
        "date": "2025-09-06",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670 on behalf of https://github.com/jeanschmidt due to seems to have introduced https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379267 and https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/actions/runs/17507127561/job/49733379271 ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670#issuecomment-3261241229))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\"",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/0ff8eabf1387de5acd6712a03bda61f1a3dfa27f"
      },
      {
        "author": "Rob Timpe <rtimpe@openteams.com>",
        "commit": "1463714",
        "date": "2025-09-04",
        "message": "[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)\n\nCurrently, user-defined classes inside of a compiled frame will cause the whole\nframe to be skipped by dynamo.  This change defers the Unsupported exception\nuntil the __build_class__ builtin is actually called, which allows a graph break\nto be inserted.  Fixes #161562\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161670\nApproved by: https://github.com/williamwen42, https://github.com/guilhermeleobas",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Graph break on on user-defined class in compiled region (#161670)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/146371483318e17929daefd37c8e459d9d6d47bb"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "f36f285",
        "date": "2025-09-03",
        "message": "[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)\n\nThis PR implements the semantics change to `torch._dynamo.error_on_graph_break`:\n- ~`torch.compile` now has a new `error_on_graph_break` kwarg that serves as a lower-priority toggle for erroring/continuing on graph breaks~\n- `error_on_graph_break` is a new internal `torch.compile `setting that is lower-priority than `fullgraph`. It allows the user to toggle erroring/continuing on graph breaks.\n- `error_on_graph_break` does nothing when `fullgraph=True`\n- `error_on_graph_break` does NOT guarantee a single graph\n\nFollowup [DONE]... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] change error_on_graph_break/fullgraph semantics (#161747)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/f36f285953700f971552083a5da9d0ceacb63bbd"
      },
      {
        "author": "Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "480c739",
        "date": "2025-09-04",
        "message": "Capture TypeError in `CONTAINS_OP` (#161069)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/161069\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Capture TypeError in `CONTAINS_OP` (#161069)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/480c7391126656154318fabf1d57ebc01e196e63"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "d5643e8",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks that cause skipped frames (#160470)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160470\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138, #159786",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks that cause skipped frames (#160470)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d5643e8f3a648a99636bfa1f2a41d54bd3c0d0f1"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "1041805",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\n\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\n\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\n\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/1041805c1ed0d4d96fd05cf485a0d89a0b5588d0"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "6562646",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\n\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\n\nAlso simplify some codegen.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678, #159817",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/6562646dab09b76479466af3b2a0e866b9c08cdd"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "d0a242e",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159817\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329, #159678",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/d0a242e54723af5e56075d70b0305fb670be70ba"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "3f80908",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #159329",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/3f8090809faf0b51456d9f427dd93ed8da3a11f6"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "10d9332",
        "date": "2025-08-27",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\n\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\n```python\nimport torch\n\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\n\ndef inner(x):\n    x = x + 1\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n    return x + 2\n\n@torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\ndef outer(x):\n    return inner(x)\n\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/10d93325b1f2ade44bf47fd75a93a8af64f3e620"
      },
      {
        "author": "Guilherme Leobas <guilhermeleobas@gmail.com>",
        "commit": "7c8f049",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "[OrderedDict] Implement `OrderedDict.move_to_end(key, last=False)` (#155152)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/155152\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #160156, #155072",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[OrderedDict] Implement `OrderedDict.move_to_end(key, last=False)` (#155152)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/7c8f049d5488010acf619362a7f0c8e1256a7d64"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "8b78ba0",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\n\nNote: nested graph break tests (and wrapped tests) are xfailed/skipped for now - we will iteratively enable the tests as more of the nested graph break implementation is complete.\n\nDifferential Revision: [D81084809](https://our.internmc.facebook.com/intern/diff/D81084809)\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8b78ba07b14d774f889f3b863a766e65d60cabff"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "6686974",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 9a756c2d710a0680bac93ab0b42db519ec2dc6cf.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516#issuecomment-3225659358))",

      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "caf98fd",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\"\n\nThis reverts commit ac6316caaa74513cbcf3c7f9269bc23cd74749db.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138#issuecomment-3225546707))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\"",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/caf98fde0d5c47452af45dc77099449edd521579"
      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",
        "commit": "46576f5",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 67d31f6b281d3b15b205756fc7ebc450cdde1dab.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to failing internal tests ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786#issuecomment-3225535752))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\"",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/46576f5a164fcf95ec7fceaa13516bcb1ca4f6ab"
      },
      {
        "author": "Zhengxu Chen <zhxchen17@meta.com>",
        "commit": "74124d1",
        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "[reland] [dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. [5/n] (#161514)\n\nSummary:\nconvert_frame.compile_frame used to take a callback transform function which will capture the frame object it has, but the frame information is not passed directly into compile_frame function.\n\nThis PR changes the signature of compile_frame so that frame information is directly passed in the function without taking a callback. This makes it easier to build fullgraph capture API on top of compile_frame.\n\nTest Plan:\nCI\n\nRollback Plan:\n\nDifferential Revision: D81041296\n\nPull Request res... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[reland] [dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. [5/n] (#161514)",

      },
      {
        "author": "PyTorch MergeBot <pytorchmergebot@users.noreply.github.com>",

        "date": "2025-08-26",
        "message": "Revert \"[dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. [5/n] (#160900)\"\n\nThis reverts commit 447d34b5f80fb7350f79decd855cb599cab39083.\n\nReverted https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160900 on behalf of https://github.com/atalman due to reverting since can't land existing diff internally, will need to reland it ([comment](https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160900#issuecomment-3224029031))",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Revert \"[dynamo] Refactor convert_frame.compile_frame to be self contained function. [5/n] (#160900)\"",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/e795450a35bca909902e12de99245e1c0e7e2872"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "67d31f6",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)\n\nNested continuation function code objects are now unique w.r.t. stack trace below (and including) the current code object.\n\nWithout this change, e.g. in the added test, `f3` would be recompiled on the second graph break.\n\nFollowup: we can skip guards on continuation functions.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159786\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678, #159817, #160138",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] prevent excessive recompilations (#159786)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/67d31f6b281d3b15b205756fc7ebc450cdde1dab"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "ac6316c",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)\n\nFix comments to reflect that we no longer codegen cells to be sent to resume function as inputs - they are instead codegen'd after the unsupported instruction in order to build resume functions that are closures.\n\nAlso simplify some codegen.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160138\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678, #159817",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] clean up comments and codegen (#160138)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/ac6316caaa74513cbcf3c7f9269bc23cd74749db"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "ef0ef6f",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159817\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329, #159678",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested closures (#159817)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/ef0ef6f93f7ef6d16d71a6997b72185504acd4b6"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "02fa5bf",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159678\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516, #159329",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support nested graph breaks x context managers (#159678)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/02fa5bf6d80fa4baa6bb6dd2fa6a16d88852da91"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "8dab6d4",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)\n\ne.g. this graph breaks once now:\n```python\nimport torch\n\ntorch._dynamo.config.nested_graph_breaks = True\n\ndef inner(x):\n    x = x + 1\n    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n    return x + 2\n\n@torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\ndef outer(x):\n    return inner(x)\n\nprint(outer(torch.ones(3)))\n```\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/159329\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281, #144516",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] support very simple nested graph breaks (#159329)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/8dab6d4c414bf997297804008c3da893e69cd51f"
      },
      {
        "author": "William Wen <williamwen@meta.com>",
        "commit": "9a756c2",
        "date": "2025-08-25",
        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] add nested graph break tests (#144516)\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/144516\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305\nghstack dependencies: #157971, #159281",

        "message": "[dynamo, nested graph breaks] implement new resume frame stack/locals/cell layout convention (#157971)\n\nThe comments/conventions are not exactly correct here, as the implementation at this PR is partial. They will be fixed in #160138.\n\nNo tests added, since there shouldn't be any overall semantic changes.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/157971\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",

        "message": "Optimize `dynamo` typing (#147499)\n\nOptimize dynamo methods type annotation.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/147499\nApproved by: https://github.com/anijain2305",

      {
        "author": "James Wu <jjwu@meta.com>",
        "commit": "9668210",
        "date": "2025-08-20",
        "message": "Allow bypasses for Precompile when guards, etc. cannot be serialized (#160902)\n\nThis adds a new function `bypass_package` and `CompilePackage.bypass_current_entry()`. This allows us to safely bypass if there are models with unserializable or incompatible parts. When we encounter something incompatible, we'll raise a bypass and ignore that particular code in DynamoCodeEntry.\n\nPull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/160902\nApproved by: https://github.com/zhxchen17",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "Allow bypasses for Precompile when guards, etc. cannot be serialized (#160902)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/96682103026b5ea27f19e6db9303e17572095b0e"
      },

        "author": "Lucas Kabela <lucaskabela@meta.com>",
        "commit": "54cc63b",
        "date": "2025-08-20",
        "message": "[BE][Dynamo] Type coverage for symbolic_convert (#160922)\n\nAs part of better engineering, we add type coverage to `dynamo/symbolic_convert.py`, which is the main work engine of dynamo for emulating python bytecode.\n\nRunning\n```\nmypy torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py --linecount-report /tmp/coverage_log\n```\n\n| -------- | Lines Annotated | Lines Total | % lines covered | Funcs Annotated | Funcs Total | % funcs covered |\n| -------- | ------- | -------- | ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- |\n| Main  |  764 | 4286 | 17.83% | 43 | 241 | 17.84% |\n| This PR | 4322 | 4322 | 100.00% | 241 | 241 | 100... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[BE][Dynamo] Type coverage for symbolic_convert (#160922)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/54cc63b467f24242cf0d6538d3e1df39e553daf1"
      },
      {
        "author": "Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>",
        "commit": "a1a555e",
        "date": "2025-08-15",
        "message": "[dynamo] Fix graph break on calling functions decorated with special context manager (#160703)\n\nAs title. This is a follow-up of the previous patch, with the goal of\nsupporting a new pattern that showed up in ComfyUI:\nhttps://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/blob/644b23ac0b92442b475e44397c62aa8de929d546/comfy/ops.py#L44\n\nEffectively, the semantics of calling a function decorated with a\ncontext manager is:\n\n```python\n@ctx_manager(args)\ndef f(x):\n    ...\n\nf(x)\n# ----->\nwith ctx_manager(args):\n    f.__wrapped__(x)\n```\n\nYes, a fresh context manager instance per invokation, see CPython source code... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Fix graph break on calling functions decorated with special context manager (#160703)",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d/-/commit/a1a555ed7b98046e5c8ee18a903b9381e7ce322d"
      },
      {
        "author": "Ryan Guo <ryanguo99@meta.com>",
        "commit": "72b559b",
        "date": "2025-08-15",
        "message": "[dynamo] Fix crash and silent incorrectness issues in `attention.sdpa_kernel` calls with kwargs (#160684)\n\nThis patch fixes 2 issues, illustrated by the test cases added:\n1. using `sdpa_kernel(backends=..., set_priority=...)` due to an\n   internal assert that forgot to be updated after #147768.\n2. forgetting to convert the `set_priority` VariableTracker back to a\n   python constant so that its value is properly used by `sdpa_kernel`,\n   also from #147768.\n\nI ran into (1) because ComfyUI had a recent update that actually sues\nthis pattern\nhttps://github.com/comfyanonymous/ComfyUI/blob/644b23ac0... [truncated]",
        "repository": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d",
        "title": "[dynamo] Fix crash and silent incorrectness issues in `attention.sdpa_kernel` calls with kwargs (#160684)",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"240: @dataclasses.dataclass\\n241: class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\\n242:     code: types.CodeType\\n243:     instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\\n244:     # Python 3.11+ fields\\n245:     # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \\\"block\\\" consists\\n246:     # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\\n247: \\n248:     # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\\n249:     prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\\n250:         default_factory=list\\n251:     )\\n252:     # map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\\n253:     block_target_offset_remap: Optional[dict[int, int]] = None\\n254: \\n255: \\n256: def _filter_iter(\\n257:     l1: Iterable[Any],\\n258:     l2: Iterable[Any],\\n259:     cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\\n260: ) -> list[Any]:\\n261:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n262:     Two-pointer conditional filter.\\n263:     e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\\n264:     returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\\n265:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n266: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@8f114650eba2e8fd25ec1134dde02fe5570c471c/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL240-265\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "240: @dataclasses.dataclass\n241: class ResumeFunctionMetadata:\n242:     code: types.CodeType\n243:     instructions: list[Instruction] = dataclasses.field(default_factory=list)\n244:     # Python 3.11+ fields\n245:     # NOTE: Python 3.11 removed blocks, but for our purposes, a \"block\" consists\n246:     # of instructions of all exception table entries that have the same target.\n247: \n248:     # map from PUSH_EXC_INFO's in the prefix to original block target offset\n249:     prefix_block_target_offset_remap: list[int] = dataclasses.field(\n250:         default_factory=list\n251:     )\n252:     # map from new block target offsets to original block target offsets\n253:     block_target_offset_remap: Optional[dict[int, int]] = None\n254: \n255: \n256: def _filter_iter(\n257:     l1: Iterable[Any],\n258:     l2: Iterable[Any],\n259:     cond: Callable[[Any, Any], bool],\n260: ) -> list[Any]:\n261:     \"\"\"\n262:     Two-pointer conditional filter.\n263:     e.g. _filter_iter(insts, sorted_offsets, lambda i, o: i.offset == o)\n264:     returns the instructions with offsets in sorted_offsets\n265:     \"\"\"\n266: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@8f114650eba2e8fd25ec1134dde02fe5570c471c/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL240-265"
  }

  "content": "{\"content\":\"583:         from the original code object so that if control flow paths\\n584:         converge we only generated 1 resume function (rather than 2^n\\n585:         resume functions).\\n586:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n587: \\n588:         meta: ResumeFunctionMetadata = ContinueExecutionCache.generated_code_metadata[\\n589:             code\\n590:         ]\\n591:         new_offset = None\\n592: \\n593:         def find_new_offset(\\n594:             instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n595:         ) -> None:\\n596:             nonlocal new_offset\\n597:             (target,) = (i for i in instructions if i.offset == offset)\\n598:             # match the functions starting at the last instruction as we have added a prefix\\n599:             (new_target,) = (\\n600:                 i2\\n601:                 for i1, i2 in zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions))\\n602:                 if i1 is target\\n603:             )\\n604:             assert target.opcode == new_target.opcode\\n605:             new_offset = new_target.offset\\n606: \\n607:         transform_code_object(code, find_new_offset)\\n608: \\n609:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n610:             # setup_fn_target_offsets currently contains the target offset of\\n611:             # each setup_fn, based on `code`. When we codegen the resume function\\n612:             # based on the original code object, `meta.code`, the offsets in\\n613:             # setup_fn_target_offsets must be based on `meta.code` instead.\\n614:             if not meta.block_target_offset_remap:\\n615:                 block_target_offset_remap = meta.block_target_offset_remap = {}\\n616: \\n617:                 def remap_block_offsets(\\n618:                     instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, Any]\\n619:                 ) -> None:\\n620:                     # NOTE: each prefix block generates exactly one PUSH_EXC_INFO,\\n621:                     # so we can tell which block a prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO belongs to,\\n622:                     # by counting. Then we can use meta.prefix_block-target_offset_remap\\n623:                     # to determine where in the original code the PUSH_EXC_INFO offset\\n624:                     # replaced.\\n625:                     prefix_blocks: list[Instruction] = []\\n626:                     for inst in instructions:\\n627:                         if len(prefix_blocks) == len(\\n628:                             meta.prefix_block_target_offset_remap\\n629:                         ):\\n630:                             break\\n631:                         if inst.opname == \\\"PUSH_EXC_INFO\\\":\\n632:                             prefix_blocks.append(inst)\\n633: \\n634:                     # old bytecode targets are after the prefix PUSH_EXC_INFO's\\n641:                     old_start_offset = (\\n642:                         cast(int, prefix_blocks[-1].offset) if prefix_blocks else -1\\n643:                     )\\n644:                     # offsets into old bytecode\\n645:                     old_inst_offsets = sorted(\\n646:                         n for n in setup_fn_target_offsets if n > old_start_offset\\n647:                     )\\n648:                     targets = _filter_iter(\\n649:                         instructions, old_inst_offsets, lambda inst, o: inst.offset == o\\n650:                     )\\n651:                     new_targets = _filter_iter(\\n652:                         zip(reversed(instructions), reversed(meta.instructions)),\\n653:                         targets,\\n654:                         lambda v1, v2: v1[0] is v2,\\n655:                     )\\n656:                     for new, old in zip(new_targets, targets):\\n657:                         block_target_offset_remap[old.offset] = new[1].offset\\n658: \\n659:                 transform_code_object(code, remap_block_offsets)\\n660: \\n661:             # if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\\n662:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\\n663:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[n] for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\\n664:             )\\n665:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\\n666:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\\n667:         )\\n668: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@8f114650eba2e8fd25ec1134dde02fe5570c471c/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/resume_execution.py%3FL583-675\"}",

if offset is not in setup_fn_target_offsets, it is an error\n662:             setup_fn_target_offsets = tuple(\n663:                 meta.block_target_offset_remap[n] for n in setup_fn_target_offsets\n664:             )\n665:         return ContinueExecutionCache.lookup(\n666:             meta.code, lineno, new_offset, setup_fn_target_offsets, *args\n667:         )\n668: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"7155:         allowed = [0, 1, 2, 3]\\n7156: \\n7157:         def fn(x):\\n7158:             x = x + 1\\n7159:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\\n7160:             key = [key for key in keys if key in allowed]\\n7161: \\n7162:             def inner():\\n7163:                 nonlocal key\\n7164: \\n7165:             return x + key[0]\\n7166: \\n7167:         self.assertEqual(\\n7168:             fn(torch.ones(3)), torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")(torch.ones(3))\\n7169:         )\\n7170: \\n7171:     def test_unbind_copy_out(self):\\n7172:         def f(eye, out):\\n7173:             torch.unbind_copy(eye, out=out)\\n7174: \\n7175:         eye = torch.eye(3)\\n7176:         out_ref = (torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3))\\n7177:         out_res = (torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3))\\n7178: \\n7179:         f(eye, out_ref)\\n7180:         torch.compile(f, backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)(eye, out_res)\\n7181:         self.assertEqual(out_ref, out_res)\\n7182: \\n7183:     def test_setitem_tensor_prop(self):\\n7184:         # Using the composite implicit of the forward would be incorrect\\n7185:         class MyFn(torch.autograd.Function):\\n7186:             @staticmethod\\n7187:             def forward(ctx, x):\\n7188:                 return torch.matmul(x, x.t())\\n7189: \\n7190:             @staticmethod\\n7191:             def backward(ctx, grad_out):\\n7192:                 return grad_out\\n7193: \\n7194:         def fn(x, y):\\n7195:             x[0] = y[0]\\n7196:             return MyFn.apply(x)\\n7197: \\n7198:         def inputs():\\n7199:             torch.manual_seed(123)\\n7200:             x = torch.randn(10, 10)\\n7201:             y = torch.randn(10, 10, requires_grad=True)\\n7202:             return x, y\\n7203: \\n7204:         x1, y1 = inputs()\\n7205:         fn(x1, y1).sum().backward()\\n7206:         self.assertTrue(x1.requires_grad)\\n7207: \\n7208:         x2, y2 = inputs()\\n7209:         torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\")(x2, y2).sum().backward()\\n7210:         self.assertTrue(x2.requires_grad)\\n7211: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@8f114650eba2e8fd25ec1134dde02fe5570c471c/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL7155-7210\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "7155:         allowed = [0, 1, 2, 3]\n7156: \n7157:         def fn(x):\n7158:             x = x + 1\n7159:             torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n7160:             key = [key for key in keys if key in allowed]\n7161: \n7162:             def inner():\n7163:                 nonlocal key\n7164: \n7165:             return x + key[0]\n7166: \n7167:         self.assertEqual(\n7168:             fn(torch.ones(3)), torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.ones(3))\n7169:         )\n7170: \n7171:     def test_unbind_copy_out(self):\n7172:         def f(eye, out):\n7173:             torch.unbind_copy(eye, out=out)\n7174: \n7175:         eye = torch.eye(3)\n7176:         out_ref = (torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3))\n7177:         out_res = (torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3), torch.zeros(3))\n7178: \n7179:         f(eye, out_ref)\n7180:         torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(eye, out_res)\n7181:         self.assertEqual(out_ref, out_res)\n7182: \n7183:     def test_setitem_tensor_prop(self):\n7184:         # Using the composite implicit of the forward would be incorrect\n7185:         class MyFn(torch.autograd.Function):\n7186:             @staticmethod\n7187:             def forward(ctx, x):\n7188:                 return torch.matmul(x, x.t())\n7189: \n7190:             @staticmethod\n7191:             def backward(ctx, grad_out):\n7192:                 return grad_out\n7193: \n7194:         def fn(x, y):\n7195:             x[0] = y[0]\n7196:             return MyFn.apply(x)\n7197: \n7198:         def inputs():\n7199:             torch.manual_seed(123)\n7200:             x = torch.randn(10, 10)\n7201:             y = torch.randn(10, 10, requires_grad=True)\n7202:             return x, y\n7203: \n7204:         x1, y1 = inputs()\n7205:         fn(x1, y1).sum().backward()\n7206:         self.assertTrue(x1.requires_grad)\n7207: \n7208:         x2, y2 = inputs()\n7209:         torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(x2, y2).sum().backward()\n7210:         self.assertTrue(x2.requires_grad)\n7211: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--cbe1a35d@8f114650eba2e8fd25ec1134dde02fe5570c471c/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL7155-7210"
